Title: Introducing Artificial Intelligence (AI) in Companies – Processtune Blog
Categories: AI,Azure Active Directory,Microservice,Microsoft Azure,Microsoft Graph,MongoDB,SlideShow,Visual Studio
BodyText: %改行%リンク（#content）%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/）%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/microsoft-azure/）%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/microsoft-azure/azure-active-directory/）%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/visual-studio/）%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/microservice/）%改行%リンク（#）%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/）%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/microsoft-azure/）%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/microsoft-azure/azure-active-directory/）%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/visual-studio/）%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/microservice/）%改行%Introducing Artificial Intelligence (AI) in Companies%改行%図（https://blog.processtune.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/03/blogAIheader.png）%改行%byリンク（https://blog.processtune.com/author/takao/）%改行%onリンク（https://blog.processtune.com/2024/02/07/）%改行%There is a lot of interest in introducing AI in companies, and many types of language models also are created by many organizations.%改行%AI has the size indicated by the parameters of a language model, and AI has purposes in a commensurate manner with its size.%改行%This article describes how to introduce AI in companies.%改行%• Large Language Model (LLM) and Small Language Model (SLM)%改行%AI comes in different sizes and aims. LLM is aiming for AGI (Artificial General Intelligence). And I think SLM aims to solve specific tasks with AI orchestration. Therefore Multimodal AI, RAG (Retrieval-Augmented Generation), and so on are essential for SLM.%改行%図（https://blog.processtune.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/01/AIOutlook.png）%改行%What I would like you to note here is that Apple is not included in the figure above.%改行%Apple’s Edge AI does not add to this article about the size and purpose of Edge AI, because it’s used in a different use-case than Cloud AI.%改行%It is possible to introduce Edge AI into a company, but at this stage, I think it is difficult to introduce Edge AI into a company unless cloud AI has already been introduced.%改行%This article will focus on cloud AI, rather than Edge AI, as an introduction to AI in companies.%改行%ⓘ Edge AI%改行%図（https://blog.processtune.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/01/MegaCapGreenLine20240129.png）%改行%The image on the right is a screen capture of https://companiesmarketcap.com/ on 29th Jan. 2024. During the last year, investors expected investment in cloud AI of Microsoft, Google (Alphabet), NVIDIA, and Amazon. The green line charts are its results.%改行%However, while cloud AI has been attracting attention so far, this year we may be entering the era of Edge AI.%改行%図（https://blog.processtune.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/01/EdgeAIUsecase.png）%改行%• Utilization of accumulated data in the AI era%改行%Size is one of the indicators for AI classification, and another indicator for AI classification is the “confidentiality of the information source.” More precisely, it is the right to access information.%改行%図（https://blog.processtune.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/02/SizeAndUsage.png）%改行%Many companies find it useful to orchestrate using a small language model without the need for a large language model. At this time, it is possible to use RAG to generate a solution that incorporates information from outside the company, or it is also possible to generate a solution using confidential internal data. What is important is that companies utilize accumulated data in the same way that they have done in the past, and this will not change in the AI era.%改行%Oracle, one of the companies that specializes in data utilization, provides the foundation for corporate data utilization extending with AI (below left). This website describes an architecture for introducing AI with a product called VizSeek (below right) that provides secure access to data.%改行%図（https://blog.processtune.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/01/OracleDataScienceBlog.png）図（https://blog.processtune.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/01/OracleVizSeek.png）%改行%• If the organization already introduced Microsoft 365%改行%Accumulated data with Microsoft 365 are already protected with Microsoft 365. In responding toリンク（https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/microsoft-365/community/microsoft365-maturity-model--governance-and-compliance）(below right), there is secure access that is under compliance policy, and paradigm with the governance of the organization.%改行%Thus for introducing AI to organizations, it is not good that move their data to other storage bother, such as S3 of AWS or Google Drive, because it should re-struct IAM roles of AWS Control Tower of AWS Organizations IAM, or Google Cloud Project.%改行%Utilizing accumulated data of the company with AI is the same as leveraging SharePoint data or OneDrive data in Microsoft 365. The data can not be accessed in SharePoint is also can not be accessed using AI. On the other hand, when generating content with AI, it will be grounded with SharePoint data OneDrive data, and so on that can be accessed. Copilot for Microsoft 365 is the service, and Microsoft Copilot Studio (below left) is used to customize its service for specific companies.%改行%図（https://blog.processtune.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/02/CopilotStudioOverview.png）図（https://blog.processtune.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/02/Microsoft365MaturityModel.png）%改行%• AI in Companies without Microsoft 365 data%改行%Accounting data or human resource data is divided from business data generally, so there are cases in which several workforces have an accounting system’s ID or human resource system’s ID. If the identity federation is not complete, Its permissions are held in these systems. In addition, there might be device management without Intune, asset management, and access to IoT data as unmanaged.%改行%These data should be stored in individual storage match access permission, then divided schemes of several AI on orchestration foundation. These cases can be implemented with Azure AI Studio or semantic kernel programming.%改行%図（https://blog.processtune.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/02/Prepare.png）%改行%>>リンク（）%改行%ⓘ Navigation%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/2024/03/26/steps-of-introducing-ai-to-companies/）%改行%Copilot Studio(under construction)%改行%Azure AI Studio(under construction)%改行%Prompt Injection(under construction)%改行%Categories:%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/ai/）リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/microsoft-graph/）リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/mongodb/）リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/slide-show/）%改行%Tags:%改行%No Tag%改行%Post navigation%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/2022/10/30/http-3-and-the-grpc-service-in-net-framework/）%改行%Post navigation%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/2024/03/26/steps-of-introducing-ai-to-companies/）%改行%No responses yet%改行%Leave a Replyリンク（https://blog.processtune.com/2024/02/07/introducing-artificial-intelligence-ai-in-companies/#respond）%改行%Your email address will not be published.Required fields are marked*%改行%Comment*%改行%Name*%改行%Email*%改行%Website%改行%Save my name, email, and website in this browser for the next time I comment.%改行%© 2025 Processtune Blog. Title: kiosk mode operation design 1 – Processtune Blog
Categories: Active Directory,Azure Active Directory,kiosk mode,Microservice,Microsoft Azure,Visual Studio,Windows 10 App
BodyText: %改行%リンク（#content）%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/）%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/microsoft-azure/）%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/microsoft-azure/azure-active-directory/）%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/visual-studio/）%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/microservice/）%改行%リンク（#）%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/）%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/microsoft-azure/）%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/microsoft-azure/azure-active-directory/）%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/visual-studio/）%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/microservice/）%改行%kiosk mode operation design 1%改行%byリンク（https://blog.processtune.com/author/tetsuro-takao/）%改行%onリンク（https://blog.processtune.com/2018/05/26/）%改行%This article explains how to create app which runs single account like kiosk mode. And also this article explains how to manage multiple accounts for multiple operation scenarios using this single account apps respectively.%改行%Therefor, this article ‘DOES NOT’ explain how to set a device of kiosk mode by the AssignedAccess configuration service provider(it says ‘CSP‘ from this). Not only that, this article ‘DOES NOT’ use the kiosk feature of Windows operating system.%改行%This article explains how to create ‘fake’ kiosk mode for some requirements which are difficult to create by UWP, such as creating local account of a device, or modifying the Windows Registry to set logon script for individual account. This ‘fake’ kiosk mode create by WPF.%改行%・What is kiosk mode%改行%The kiosk mode is just one of features which is provided by Windows operating system, so we should consider details of operation scenarios when we want to use the feature.%改行%The kiosk mode is designed thought to be used single application of a device, and the app is executed by single account. So if your operation scenarios does not fit for it, you should create same other mechanisms which is equal to or greater than the kiosk mode of Windows OS.%改行%If you want to use the kiosk mode with several different accounts to fit your several scenarios, you should manage multiple accounts with multiple correct security levels. And if you want to provide different application respectively for individual account, you should control the application to launch by correct account.%改行%リンク（https://www.slideshare.net/ssusera5136d/kiosk-mode-and-customizing-lockdown）(Japanese:日本語)%改行%・The AssignedAccess configuration service provider%改行%The kiosk mode is controlled by theCSP, theCSPset some parameters below with XML format%改行%(see involved article provided by Microsoft >>リンク（https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/windows/client-management/mdm/assignedaccess-csp）).%改行%1.Define the account which is used in kiosk mode.%改行%2.The account is used for specified one application.%改行%3.The application can only use specified features of Windows OS. It is the Lockdown feature of kiosk mode.%改行%New features of kiosk mode%改行%you can assign group for kiosk app(Windows 10 Ver.1803-)%改行%you can assign multiple apps which created as UWP or native app to account(or group), and can set auto run an app of them(Windows 10 Ver.1809-)%改行%you can set specific folders when user use the File Open Dialog（Windows 10 Ver.1809～）%改行%・What is the Lockdown feature of kiosk mode%改行%The lockdown feature is controlled byリンク（https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/windows/uwp/packaging/app-capability-declarations）of UWP, it’s also available for native desktop application usingCSP.%改行%Almost all of applications on Windows OS run in the AppContainers of Windows OS. If an application needs features of Windows OS, Windows OS provides the AppCntainer which bridges an instance which has minimum features of Windows OS for the application, these features are controlled by the App capability declarations.%改行%This mechanism intends to divide an application from Windows os, this isolation includes window isolation , network isolation and process isolation(see involved article provided by Microsoft >>リンク（https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/windows/desktop/SecAuthZ/appcontainer-isolation）), this mechanism is provided by modern Windows OS(since Windows 8).%改行%the Lockdown feature is the App capability declarations which is specified on application development.%改行%図（https://blog.processtune.com/wp-content/uploads/2019/03/lockdown.png）%改行%リンク（）%改行%・Scenarios of this article%改行%I want to explain something about ‘fake’ kiosk mode lockdown mechanism for the scenario that multiple accounts which ready for several operation scenarios use specific app respectively. The overview below is scenarios of this article.%改行%1.It is assumed that, you create an app which is used like kiosk mode by end user.%改行%2.The app will be used by several dozens or hundreds users at many places.%改行%3.The app is supported by application operators who are dispatched at the places where the app is set. So you plan to create another application for the application operator.%改行%4.When you procure new Windows 10 devices, you order setup works of your app to maintenance operators. And you think that this scenario needs another application.%改行%5.You attempt to set an administrator account because you want to manage the devices completely.%改行%>>As a result, you planned creating three apps, setting four account and logging working state of maintenance operators and application operators.%改行%・How to lockdown a device for multiple accounts respectively%改行%This article explain how to control several accounts like kiosk mode for above scenario using my blog’s implementations [リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/?p=4551）],[リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/?p=4781）],[リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/?p=4785）],[リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/?p=4590）] by C#.%改行%・Preparation of sample code%改行%This sample is created by WPF. And almost all logic of sample code is in ‘App_Startup’ method in App.xaml.cs. So at first, we set tiniest modify at App.xaml.cs , see below.%改行%Modify [StartupUri=”MainWindow.xaml”] to [Startup=”App_Startup”] of App.xaml, and add [void App_Startup()] method to [App] class of App.xaml.cs.%改行%<Application x:Class="WPFLockdownSample.App" xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/winfx/2006/xaml/presentation" xmlns:x="http://schemas.microsoft.com/winfx/2006/xaml" xmlns:local="clr-namespace:WPFLockdownSample" Startup="App_Startup"> <Application.Resources> </Application.Resources> </Application> public partial class App : Application { void App_Startup(object sender, StartupEventArgs e) { } }%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/?p=4562）%改行%Categories:%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/technical-memo/active-directory/）リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/technical-memo/kiosk-mode/）リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/microsoft-azure/）リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/visual-studio/）リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/winui/universal-apps/windows-store-app/windows-10-app/）%改行%Tags:%改行%No Tag%改行%Post navigation%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/2017/11/09/how-to-create-the-qna-manker-my-service/）%改行%Post navigation%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/2018/06/30/kiosk-mode-operation-design-2/）%改行%No responses yet%改行%Leave a Replyリンク（https://blog.processtune.com/2018/05/26/kiosk-mode-operation-design-1/#respond）%改行%Your email address will not be published.Required fields are marked*%改行%Comment*%改行%Name*%改行%Email*%改行%Website%改行%Save my name, email, and website in this browser for the next time I comment.%改行%© 2025 Processtune Blog. Title: ASP.NET Core Options Pattern – Processtune Blog
Categories: .NET Core,ASP.NET Core,Azure Active Directory,C#,Microservice,Microsoft Azure,Service,SlideShow,Visual Studio,Visual Studio Code
BodyText: %改行%リンク（#content）%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/）%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/microsoft-azure/）%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/microsoft-azure/azure-active-directory/）%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/visual-studio/）%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/microservice/）%改行%リンク（#）%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/）%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/microsoft-azure/）%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/microsoft-azure/azure-active-directory/）%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/visual-studio/）%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/microservice/）%改行%ASP.NET Core Options Pattern%改行%図（https://blog.processtune.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/03/OptionsPatternSeriesTitle.png）%改行%byリンク（https://blog.processtune.com/author/tetsuro-takao/）%改行%onリンク（https://blog.processtune.com/2022/06/28/）%改行%A typical web application separates setting from the App. In the ASP .NET Core web app allows encapsulation and separation of an interest of setting using the options pattern. It allows enables continuous integration while improving maintainability of an application.%改行%This article describes as an introduce of the ASP.NET Core Options Pattern using globalization sample.%改行%For globalization of ASP .NET Core samples using ‘.resx’ files areリンク（https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/aspnet/core/fundamentals/localization?view=aspnetcore-6.0&WT.mc_id=DT-MVP-4029060）. These samples are created, edited, debugged, and continuously managed in Visual Studio, so users who aren’t using Visual Studio can use tools such as the online xml converter to get ‘.resx’ files.%改行%If your development environment for productions that support many languages, I recommend that you do so, but if you create an application that supports a few languages such as just only Japanese and English, or lightweight web application such as PoC, demo or sample code. These apps globalize only few words, so It is useful that globalization using the options pattern without adopting full-scale globalization using ‘.resx’ files.%改行%>>リンク（https://github.com/TetsuroTakao/GlobalizationUseSettings）%改行%・Overview%改行%The way of separating ASP.NET settings from app hasn’t changed with the way of existing. Each method can use defined the setting value in the JSON file after registering the service on the host of the application.%改行%In the case of ASP.NET MVC, if you register the service of the setting value in the host created by CreateBuilder, you can store the service received by the constructor of the controller in the variable defined globally in the scope of the controller, so can use settings variable in POST or GET method. You can use that variable in the processing of the request.%改行%[Program.cs] builder.Services.Configure<ApplicationContext>(builder.Configuration); [Pages/Search.cshtml.cs] public class SearchModel : PageModel { private readonly ApplicationContext Context; public SearchModel(ILogger<IndexModel> logger, IConfiguration configuration) { Context = new ApplicationContext(); Context.Languages.Add(ApplicationContext.English); configuration.GetSection(ApplicationContext.Japanese).Bind(Context); } public void OnGet() { ViewData["Message"] = Context.Languages.Count; ... } }%改行%ⓘ As additional information%改行%At that time, if the setting values ​​are separated using the options pattern, maintainable encapsulation can be performed. You can encapsulate by below.%改行%1. The settings for individual methods or controllers, respectively.%改行%2. At the level of “system” and “business” information such as logs and IP whitelists.%改行%3. Semantics of settings.%改行%4. more …%改行%Often In the Domain Driven Design, the same thing is called by deferent name depending on boundary context on the scene in which it is used. When you purchase some product, what is displayed as “payment” in the member’s app is displayed as “sales” in the sales management system. It is OK that use deferent letteral objects on each individual systems if it mapped deferent labels by an individual system of a legacy system. It is important that using deferent settings value to handle same object of stateful middleware on deferent service in a modern system.%改行%Thus, it can be said that the value of the settings handled in each boundary context with several same attributes are different when design settings elements.%改行%・Implementation%改行%In this article, the reason of using an implementation example of globalization is for describing the example of “Continuous integration of applications that read different setting values ​​while having the same attributes” to express the features of the options pattern.%改行%図（https://blog.processtune.com/wp-content/uploads/2022/06/StepsOfTakingConfiguration.png）%改行%In this article, the reason of using an implementation example of globalization is for describing the example of “Continuous integration of applications that read different setting values ​​while having the same attributes” to express the features of the options pattern.%改行%At first, prepare Japanese and English sections in the appsettings.json, and it has brand name and the Pages segment. The segment has an action names as the ActionKey and a page titles as the Title.%改行%図（https://blog.processtune.com/wp-content/uploads/2022/06/Appsettings.png）%改行%図（https://blog.processtune.com/wp-content/uploads/2022/06/ApplicationContext.png）%改行%Next, prepare a class with the same structure as the JSON file used for the configuration service registration for application builder.%改行%Add the ApplicationContext.cs to the project, I recommend that adding the Models folder in the project at first, then input the ApplicationContext.cs into it.%改行%One of the features of the ASP.NET options pattern is that defined const is name of the object and ignore it as structure of the object. Thus, this const can be used when get certain section of configuration service without hard coding ‘Japanese’ or ‘English’.%改行%Then, Register target section of the builder configuration as the configuration service with the type same as certain section of appsettings.json.%改行%図（https://blog.processtune.com/wp-content/uploads/2022/06/RegisterService.png）%改行%図（https://blog.processtune.com/wp-content/uploads/2022/06/UsingConfigurationService.png）%改行%Sincethe setting value registered as configuration service can be received by the argument of the constructor, store it in a variable at the global level scope of the class. It can use in individual method of the class, respectively.%改行%ⓘ As additional information%改行%I’m using the ViewData object because I needed to layer the objects when separating the options pattern. When using the options pattern as a mere encapsulation, the ViewBag object may be sufficient, as it is a set of parallel key / value combinations without grouped hierarchies.%改行%Categories:%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/winui/net-core/）リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/asp-net-core/）リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/c/）リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/asp-net-core/service/）リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/slide-show/）リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/visual-studio/visual-studio-code/）%改行%Tags:%改行%No Tag%改行%Post navigation%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/2022/05/30/asp-net-core-grpc-grpc-communication-tools/）%改行%Post navigation%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/2022/07/23/encapsulation-of-information-affects-an-app-flexibility-asp-net-core-options-pattern/）%改行%No responses yet%改行%Leave a Replyリンク（https://blog.processtune.com/2022/06/28/asp-net-core-options-pattern/#respond）%改行%Your email address will not be published.Required fields are marked*%改行%Comment*%改行%Name*%改行%Email*%改行%Website%改行%Save my name, email, and website in this browser for the next time I comment.%改行%© 2025 Processtune Blog. Title: Microsoft Graph API provides several grant types of The OAuth 2.0 Authorization Framework – Processtune Blog
Categories: Azure Active Directory,Microservice,Microsoft Azure,Microsoft Graph,SlideShow,Visual Studio,未分類
BodyText: %改行%リンク（#content）%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/）%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/microsoft-azure/）%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/microsoft-azure/azure-active-directory/）%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/visual-studio/）%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/microservice/）%改行%リンク（#）%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/）%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/microsoft-azure/）%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/microsoft-azure/azure-active-directory/）%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/visual-studio/）%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/microservice/）%改行%Microsoft Graph API provides several grant types of The OAuth 2.0 Authorization Framework%改行%図（https://blog.processtune.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/03/AuthenticationAuthorizationSeriesTitle.png）%改行%byリンク（https://blog.processtune.com/author/tetsuro-takao/）%改行%onリンク（https://blog.processtune.com/2019/10/30/）%改行%If your application can’t use OAuth 2.0 authorization flow as a user-interactive flow, such as RPA(Robotic Process Automation) or observation system for some event, it is good that use OAuth 2.0 authorization flow with grant_type parameter as password or client_credentials.%改行%This article describes how to use properly these two cases.%改行%・User-interactive type and user-less type flow%改行%An app which observes events of a system or executes some function, RPA also have to access to some resource. And when access to some resources, an app has to be authorized to access some resources by the resource owner.%改行%In case of an app to access a resource managed by Microsoft Graph, an app can use Microsoft Graph API which solves these processes.%改行%Microsoft Graph API provides several features such as exposing the Office 365 API, resources management the Azure Active Directory. And provides the authentication feature that is a fundamental feature. The authentication feature of Microsoft Graph API supports WS-Fed, SAML, OAuth 2.0, and OpenID Connect.%改行%Particularly OAuth 2.0 is programmable flow, so this article describes how to control the OAuth 2.0 flow of Microsoft Graph API.%改行%リンク（https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc6749）defines user-interaction type flow( ‘authorization_code’ grant flow and ‘implicit’ grant flow), and user-less type flows(‘client_credentials’ grant flow and ‘password’ grant flow).%改行%So ‘client_credentials’ grant flow and ‘password’ grant flow are usable to an app such as observes events of a system, executes some function or RPA.%改行%OAuth 2.0 defines a mechanism that an app how to access to resources of a resource owner as below.%改行%1. An app requests for an authorization server to acquire code which represents a resource owner is log in. Meanwhile, an authorization server returns a confirmation form to an app for a user allowing access to user resources. An app waits for a user to allow.%改行%2. When a user allows access to user resources, an authorization server returns a code to an app, then an app sends a code to a resource server.%改行%3. Resource server issues access token to an app for an app to access the resource of the owner.%改行%The ‘client_credentials’ grant flow and the ‘password’ grant flow skips ‘1’ step and ‘2’ step Instead, an app send credential to the resource server on step ‘2’.%改行%・Grant type client_credentials flow%改行%An app uses a credential to acquire a token that Microsoft Graph API provided has information which an account and resource that the account can access, maintenance or search. The ‘client_credentials’ grant flow uses credential for an impersonated user which is kept at Microsoft Azure Active Directory application.%改行%>>The article ‘リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/?p=5085）‘%改行%Use the Application (client) ID which Azure Active Directory application issues as an account when using the ‘client_cledential’ grant flow. In ‘The OAuth 2.0 Authorization Framework’, it says ‘Entity’. For this entity, the Azure Active Directory application issues the Client secret as a password of this account.%改行%The entity in Microsoft Graph API has default access permission to several resources. Reading basic information of Azure Active Directory accounts and so on.%改行%>>The article ‘リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/?p=5099）‘%改行%・Grant type password flow%改行%The ‘password’ grant flow asserts the user explicitly, the ‘client_credentials’ grant flow uses an impersonated user. So when using ‘password’ grant_type flow, Microsoft Graph API returns an access_token that has several permissions depend on the login user.%改行%For Example, add delegate permit ‘User.Read.All’ to the Azure Active Directory application.%改行%図（https://blog.processtune.com/wp-content/uploads/2020/03/addpermission.png）%改行%Then acquire two access tokens both the ‘client_credentials’ grant type and the ‘password’ grant type with the Postman. At the last, send a post request to URL ‘https://graph.microsoft.com/v1.0/users’ with an access token which is acquired.%改行%図（https://blog.processtune.com/wp-content/uploads/2020/03/gettokenClientCredentials.png）%改行%>>The article ‘リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/?p=5099）‘%改行%図（https://blog.processtune.com/wp-content/uploads/2020/03/gettokenPassword.png）%改行%key : username and value : user account@domain name. key : password and value : password of user account. key : client_id and value : copy from Application (client) ID of Azure portal. key : client _secretand and value : copy from where kept place at when client secret issued. key : grant_type and value : put 'password'.%改行%The access token using the ‘client_credentials’ grant type will return fail, and the ‘password’ grant type will return users list of the domain.%改行%図（https://blog.processtune.com/wp-content/uploads/2020/03/getUserList.png）%改行%Thus, please check the chart below.%改行%Microsoft account%改行%Organization account%改行%Microsoft Graph authentication grant type%改行%client_credentials*1%改行%password%改行%client_credentials%改行%Azure Active Directory application API permissions type%改行%Application permission%改行%Application permission%改行%Delegate permission%改行%Application permission*2%改行%Using individual Azure Active Directory applications per feature.%改行%Use to access Microsoft Graph resource or executing common features that sending a mail, post a message to the Microsoft Teams and so on.%改行%Use to access resources of Azure Active Directory user or executing a feature that Admin Consent Required.%改行%Use to access Microsoft Graph resource or executing common features that sending a mail, post a message to the Microsoft Teams and so on.%改行%*1:Can’t use password grant type flow for the Microsoft Account.%改行%*2:Set maximum API permissions to the Azure Active Directory application, then use minimum scope of the feature when request token.%改行%Categories:%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/microsoft-azure/azure-active-directory/）リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/microsoft-azure/）リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/microsoft-graph/）リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/slide-show/）リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/visual-studio/）リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/%e6%9c%aa%e5%88%86%e9%a1%9e/）%改行%Tags:%改行%No Tag%改行%Post navigation%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/2019/09/30/how-to-set-attributes-of-azure-active-directory-application-for-impersonated-users/）%改行%Post navigation%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/2019/11/30/download-a-client-app-includes-an-authentication-feature-for-azure-active-directory-account/）%改行%No responses yet%改行%Leave a Title: Create Azure Active Directory application – Processtune Blog
Categories: Azure Active Directory,Microservice,Microsoft Azure,Microsoft Graph,SlideShow,Visual Studio
BodyText: %改行%リンク（#content）%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/）%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/microsoft-azure/）%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/microsoft-azure/azure-active-directory/）%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/visual-studio/）%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/microservice/）%改行%リンク（#）%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/）%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/microsoft-azure/）%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/microsoft-azure/azure-active-directory/）%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/visual-studio/）%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/microservice/）%改行%Create Azure Active Directory application%改行%byリンク（https://blog.processtune.com/author/tetsuro-takao/）%改行%onリンク（https://blog.processtune.com/2019/08/31/）%改行%When we design an app, we start to decide that the app needs authentication or not. For example, the app shows user information, needs access to some resources or executes some functions of a specific user.%改行%If the app is for consumers, the app has to ready to authentication with Facebook, Twitter, Line, Google or Microsoft account, etc.%改行%For enterprise uses, it has to be that the app is able to authentication with an account of Azure Active Directory, Auth0, Google, AWS, VMware or Oracle and so on.%改行%In case the app uses resources that are managed by Azure Active Directory, using the Azure Active Directory application is an easy way to create an authentication feature for the app, as it provides an endpoint connected to the authorization server of an account registered on the Azure Active Directory.%改行%・OAuth 2.0%改行%When creating the Azure Active Directory application, several parameters of its are for a specific type of authentication, so understanding of types of authentication is important at first.%改行%Azure Active Directory application is used by the Microsoft Graph API.%改行%The Microsoft Graph API provides several authentication features for apps, there are WS-Fed, SAML, OAuth 2.0, and OpenID Connect.%改行%Particularly the OAuth 2.0 is supported by many identity providers such as Facebook, Twitter, LinkedIn, Google, Yahoo!, Salesforce.com, Github and so on, so this article describes how to set parameters of Azure Active Directory application for OAuth 2.0.%改行%There are four types of authentication ofリンク（https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc6749）.%改行%図（https://blog.processtune.com/wp-content/uploads/2020/03/OAuth20Top.png）図（https://blog.processtune.com/wp-content/uploads/2020/03/OAuth20Menu.png）%改行%The OAuth 2.0 Authorization Framework defines identity authentication steps and how to access resources use authorized identity. Accessing resources are the same in four types. Using the scope parameter for representing what resources the app use.%改行%The app has to request with access token when accessing resources, an access token is provided in a different way in four types.%改行%The types of authentication are defined by the grant type parameter, there are authorization code authentication type, implicit authentication type, password authentication type, and client credential authentication type. An authorization code authentication type is basic, so this article describes this type.%改行%An implicit authentication type is for the app using JavaScript to authenticate the user. Its type is described in the article ‘リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/?p=5101）‘.%改行%A password authentication type and a client credential authentication type are described in the article ‘リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/?p=5099）‘.%改行%・Create Azure Active Directory application%改行%In the authorization code authentication type, it is the first step that a user request authentication to an authorization server using a browser.%改行%Then the server requests identity and password to a user for authorizing identity, and if correct identity and password pair, responses authorization code.%改行%A user request login to resource server using acquired code. A resource server returns access token if the code represent authorized user identity that has authority to a scope that the user requested.%改行%These roles of the OAuth 2.0 Authorization Framework in Microsoft Graph API are below.%改行%A resource owner is the Azure Active Directory account.%改行%=>Login Azure portal and select Azure Active Directory, select Users and press New user.%改行%A resource server is the Microsoft Graph API which has Azure Active Directory resources and also Office 365 resources.%改行%=>This article uses user information of Azure Active Directory resources.%改行%A client is an app.%改行%=>Use [リンク（https://oauthdebugger.com/）] web site.%改行%図（https://blog.processtune.com/wp-content/uploads/2020/03/OAuth20Debugger.png）%改行%An authorization server is Azure Active Directory application endpoint.%改行%=>Login Azure portal and select Azure Active Directory, select Application registrations and press [New registration] tab.%改行%図（https://blog.processtune.com/wp-content/uploads/2020/03/new-registration.png）%改行%Go to [OAuth 2.0 <debugger/>] web site, then copy [Redirect URI (required)] section URL and back to Azure portal, then paste URL to [Redirect URI (optional)] and put the name into [Name] text box, press [Register] button.%改行%図（https://blog.processtune.com/wp-content/uploads/2020/03/register.png）%改行%When application created then an auto transition to [Overview] section, so press [Endpoints] tab.%改行%図（https://blog.processtune.com/wp-content/uploads/2020/03/Endpoints.png）%改行%Copy code of section [OAuth 2.0 authorization endpoint (v2)] and go to [OAuth 2.0 <debugger/>] web site, then paste to [Authorize URI (required)] section.%改行%図（https://blog.processtune.com/wp-content/uploads/2020/03/copy-endpoint.png）図（https://blog.processtune.com/wp-content/uploads/2020/03/paste-endpoint.png）%改行%Back to Azure portal and copy code of section [Application (client) ID], then go to [OAuth 2.0 <debugger/>] web site and paste to [Client ID (required)] section. Next, put ‘1234’ to section [State] and nothing does at [Nonce] section. Continue, put ‘openid’ to [Scope (required)] section and [Response type (required)] keeps stay [code] checked.%改行%図（https://blog.processtune.com/wp-content/uploads/2020/03/copy-clientID.png）図（https://blog.processtune.com/wp-content/uploads/2020/03/set-parameters-to-test.png）%改行%From scroll to the end of [OAuth 2.0 <debugger/>] page and press [SEND REQUEST] button, the page auto transitions to an explanation of the request that application name [Userinformation] argues to use user resources.%改行%This confirmation page says a user allows access permission for user resources to the app. If the user press [Accept] button, the page redirects to defined redirect URL and is sent authorization code which has the access permission for user resources as query string of URL.%改行%図（https://blog.processtune.com/wp-content/uploads/2020/03/sendrequest.png）図（https://blog.processtune.com/wp-content/uploads/2020/03/accessgrant.png）%改行%At the last, confirm the authorization code at [OAuth 2.0 <debugger/>] website defined as redirect URL at Azure Active Directory application.%改行%図（https://blog.processtune.com/wp-content/uploads/2020/03/loginsuccess.png）%改行%Categories:%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/microsoft-azure/azure-active-directory/）リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/microsoft-azure/）リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/microsoft-graph/）リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/slide-show/）リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/visual-studio/）%改行%Tags:%改行%No Tag%改行%Post navigation%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/2019/07/30/cognitive-services-qna-maker-create-bot-client-app/）%改行%Post Title: JavaScript colors SVG images that are acquired by Blazor WebAssembly – Processtune Blog
Categories: ASP.NET Core,Azure Active Directory,Blazor,JavaScript,Microservice,Microsoft Azure,SlideShow,Visual Studio,Visual Studio Code
BodyText:
%改行%リンク（#content）%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/）%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/microsoft-azure/）%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/microsoft-azure/azure-active-directory/）%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/visual-studio/）%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/microservice/）%改行%リンク（#）%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/）%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/microsoft-azure/）%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/microsoft-azure/azure-active-directory/）%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/visual-studio/）%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/microservice/）%改行%JavaScript colors SVG images that are acquired by Blazor WebAssembly%改行%図（https://blog.processtune.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/03/ContainerizationSeriesTitle.png）%改行%byリンク（https://blog.processtune.com/author/tetsuro-takao/）%改行%onリンク（https://blog.processtune.com/2021/11/01/）%改行%This article is continuous my previous article [リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/?p=6162）]. Modifyリンク（https://github.com/TetsuroTakao/SVGIconColoring_en_1）and add a feature that user can coloring SVG file select from a list.%改行%リンク（https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/aspnet/core/blazor/javascript-interoperability/?view=aspnetcore-6.0?WT.mc_id=DT-MVP-4029060）of Blazor WASM and JavaScript is described inリンク（https://blog.processtune.com/?p=6162）, so this article only describes how to modify SVG file with JavaScript, although it’s important that Blazor WASM can reuse JavaScript ecosystem.%改行%It is good that a feature that JavaScript creates not well will create using Blazor. And use JavaScript to control shadow DOM if it does not need send state to service, for exsample simulation or tempolary calculate that is before final decision of user.%改行%>>リンク（https://github.com/TetsuroTakao/QRCodeCreation）%改行%・Display user selected image%改行%Inリンク（https://blog.processtune.com/?p=6162）, Blazor creates HTML content in the [Pages/index.razor] file, this content lists SVG files that are acqired fromリンク（http://www.onlinewebfonts.com/icon）. And already are defined the [selectImg] JavaScript function inリンク（https://blog.processtune.com/?p=6162）. So create the [selectImg] JavaScript function and add to the [wwwroot/index.html] file. The feature clones selected SVG image and put into cloned image into a playgrounds for simulation that a user may color selected SVG image to prefered color. Then add several controls for coloring to the playgrounds same as clone of selected SVG image.%改行%[In the previous article, Blazor create HTML content in the [Pages/index.razor] file as below]
[So, Add below code to the [Pages/Index.razor] file, and ...]
[this function add to the [wwwroot/index.html] file. So the function runs when the SVG file is clicked then it recieves IMG tag DOM object.] function selectImg(img){ var placeholder = document.getElementById("selectedImage"); while(placeholder.children.length > 0){ placeholder.removeChild(placeholder.firstChild); } var container = document.createElement("div"); container.style.display = "inline-block"; var ctl = document.createElement("div"); var paintbrush = 128396; ctl.innerHTML = `&#${paintbrush};`; container.appendChild(ctl.cloneNode(true)); container.lastChild.style="color:#ff0000;font-size:5rem;cursor:pointer;"; container.lastChild.setAttribute('onclick','setColor(this);'); container.appendChild(ctl.cloneNode(true)); container.lastChild.style="color:#00ff00;font-size:5rem;cursor:pointer;"; container.lastChild.setAttribute('onclick','setColor(this);'); container.appendChild(ctl.cloneNode(true)); container.lastChild.style="color:#0000ff;font-size:5rem;cursor:pointer;"; container.lastChild.setAttribute('onclick','setColor(this);'); placeholder.appendChild(container); placeholder.appendChild(img.cloneNode(true)); placeholder.lastChild.id="currentImg"; placeholder.lastChild.style="width:200px;vertical-align:top;margin-top:3rem;margin-left:3rem;"; } [describe] HTML div tag that has id attribute [selectedImage] is the place holder that is an operating place for coloring selected SVG file, so if something in it, remove to have nothing. Create a container that is a playgrounds for coloring selected SVG file. The container includes three coloring selectors and selected SVG image. The coloring selectors are using HTML symbol defined the Template literals, and are equipped onclick handler (describes later). The selected image needs id attribute for colorting because it is cloned. These elements are defined several styles because they are dynamic injected as shadow DOM.%改行%>>リンク（https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/JavaScript/Reference/Template_literals）%改行%図（https://blog.processtune.com/wp-content/uploads/2022/03/ContainerOfSelectedSVG.png）%改行%図（https://blog.processtune.com/wp-content/uploads/2022/03/SVGImageSelectFeatureImplement.png）%改行%・Change color of selected image by user operation%改行%A user can select SVG images of the list, so change the cursol to a pointer.%改行% to %改行%The [setColor] JavaScript function accept argument coloring control (brush) as DOM object (the paintbrush). It is called when a user select any one of these paintbrushes.%改行%The style property of the control that accepted as an argument has a color property, so change the color of the clone of selected SVG image using it.%改行%On the other hand, the src attribute of the clone of selected SVG image has meta information and acctual SVG xml, thus to modify SVG xml of the clone of selected SVG image, remove meta information from value of the src attribute of selected SVG image.%改行%The [setColor] JavaScript function can find target DOM, because the clone of selected SVG image has the value [currentImg] of the [id] attribute that already defined when create it. Change SVG xml of the clone of selected SVG image to new color usingリンク（https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/API/DOMParser）. At last set the src attribute of the the clone of selected SVG image to new colored SVG xml with meta information.%改行%function setColor(brush){ var dataType = "data:image/svg+xml;utf8,"; var imgXML = document.getElementById("currentImg").getAttribute("src").replace(dataType,""); var dom_parser = new DOMParser(); var document_obj = dom_parser.parseFromString(imgXML, "text/xml"); document_obj.rootElement.getElementsByTagName("g")[0].setAttribute('style','fill:' + brush.style.color); document.getElementById("currentImg").setAttribute("src",dataType + document_obj.rootElement.outerHTML); }%改行%The following figures are scenes of selecting image then selecting brush.%改行%図（https://blog.processtune.com/wp-content/uploads/2022/03/SVGImageColoringFeatureImplement.png）%改行%The following figures are scenes of selecting image then selecting brush.%改行%>>リンク（https://github.com/TetsuroTakao/SVGIconColoring_en_2）%改行%Categories:%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/asp-net-core/）リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/asp-net-core/blazor/）リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/asp-net-core/javascript/）リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/slide-show/）リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/visual-studio/visual-studio-code/）%改行%Tags:%改行%No Tag%改行%Post navigation%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/2021/10/01/blazor-wasm-aquires-svg-images-that-will-be-colored-by-javascript-later/）%改行%Post navigation%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/2021/12/01/nginx-run-on-windows-subsystem-for-linux-2/）%改行%No responses yet%改行%Leave a Replyリンク（https://blog.processtune.com/2021/11/01/javascript-colors-svg-images-that-are-acquired-by-blazor-webassembly/#respond）%改行%Your email address will not be published.Required fields are marked*%改行%Comment*%改行%Name*%改行%Email*%改行%Website%改行%Save my name, email, and website in this browser for the next time I comment.%改行%© 2025 Processtune Blog. Title: Take token using 'authorized code' grant type of Microsoft Graph API – Processtune Blog
Categories: Azure Active Directory,Microservice,Microsoft Azure,Microsoft Graph,Office App,ReactJS.NET,SlideShow,Visual Studio,未分類
BodyText: %改行%リンク（#content）%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/）%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/microsoft-azure/）%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/microsoft-azure/azure-active-directory/）%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/visual-studio/）%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/microservice/）%改行%リンク（#）%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/）%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/microsoft-azure/）%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/microsoft-azure/azure-active-directory/）%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/visual-studio/）%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/microservice/）%改行%Take token using 'authorized code' grant type of Microsoft Graph API%改行%図（https://blog.processtune.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/03/AuthenticationAuthorizationSeriesTitle.png）%改行%byリンク（https://blog.processtune.com/author/tetsuro-takao/）%改行%onリンク（https://blog.processtune.com/2020/01/31/）%改行%Microsoft Graph API supports an authentication flow of The OAuth 2.0 Authorization Framework, there are four types of grant types of it (refer to the article). The ‘authorization code’ grant type is one of them, and Microsoft Graph API returns the authorization code when corresponding to the request of the authorizing the Azure Active Directory account.%改行%In the authentication flow, an app uses this authorization code to request a token which is used for accessing user resources.%改行%Microsoft Identity Platform provides a feature that these processes that are from calling authorization an account to taking a token complete automatically. And a token acquired is kept in the cache provided by a feature of the Microsoft Identity Platform.%改行%If the app wants to use correctly several tokens in an individual situation, it is good that the app interrupts a code or a token and changes to minimum scopes properly to access individual resources.%改行%For example, in case of the app login as an account of Azure Active Directory and sharing a resource owned by login user to another user and post a message to Microsoft Teams as a login user. In this case, the token acquired at the app login should have all scopes that permitted to execute. there are to post to Microsoft Teams, to read all groups of the domain, to access login user’s resources.%改行%It is not bad that the Azure Active Directory application has all scopes, but it is not good to request to acquire a token that has all scopes because the individual request is not needed for all scopes.%改行%Therefore, it is good to choose properly in case of using the Microsoft Identity Platform and in case of taking a token per minimum scopes.%改行%This article how to take authorization code and throw requests a token using the code.%改行%>>リンク（https://github.com/TetsuroTakao/GetUserInformation）%改行%・Setting configuration for development environment%改行%As first, this article usingリンク（https://github.com/TetsuroTakao/ShowUserInformation）ofリンク（https://blog.processtune.com/?p=5212）which implements already the ‘authorization code’ grant type authentication flow. Download the code sample, restore packages and run debug done, then modify ‘Sign In’ action in the ‘Account’ controller of [Controllers] folder at the Solution Explorer to usable for the development environment.%改行%using Microsoft.AspNetCore.Mvc; namespace React.Sample.Webpack.CoreMvc.Controllers { public class AccountController : Controller { [Route("Account/SignIn")] [ResponseCache(Location = ResponseCacheLocation.None, NoStore = true)] public void SignIn() { var tenant = "__YOUR TENANT__";//this grant_type allows common var clientId = "__APP CLIENT ID__"; var redirectUri = "http://localhost:9457/home"; Response.Redirect("https://login.microsoftonline.com/" + tenant + "/oauth2/v2.0/authorize?client_id=" + clientId + "&redirect_uri=" + redirectUri + "&grant_type=implicit&response_type=code&scope=User.Read"); } } }%改行%Modify lines of the variable ‘tenant’ and ‘clientId’ to useable for the development environment(11th line and 12th line).%改行%And open ‘Index’ action in the ‘Home’ controller of [Controllers] folder at the Solution Explorer, put a breakpoint at the 45th line. Then run debug and confirm acquiring ‘code’ and ‘state’ variables.%改行%図（https://blog.processtune.com/wp-content/uploads/2020/03/code.png）%改行%図（https://blog.processtune.com/wp-content/uploads/2020/03/state.png）%改行%・Get token%改行%Insert code below after the place that acquiring a ‘state’ variable, and modify lines of the variable ‘tenant’, ‘clientId’ and ‘secret’ to useable for the development environment.%改行%var signIn = false; var token = string.Empty; if (!string.IsNullOrEmpty(code)) { var tenant = "__YOUR TENANT__";//this grant_type allows common var clientId = "__APP CLIENT ID__"; var redirectUri = "http://localhost:9457/home"; var secret = "__CLIENT SECRET__"; token = new AccessGraph().GetToken(clientId, secret, redirectUri, "", code, tenant, "user.read"); if (!string.IsNullOrEmpty(token)) signIn = true; } ViewBag.IsSignin = signIn;%改行%Add a new class file name that is ‘AccessGraph.cs’ at the root of the solution, and create the ‘GetToken’ method of the ‘AccessGraph’ class. (リンク（https://github.com/TetsuroTakao/ShowUserInformation）includes it)%改行%図（https://blog.processtune.com/wp-content/uploads/2020/03/AccessGraph.png）%改行%namespace React.Sample.Webpack.CoreMvc { public class AccessGraph { public string GetToken(string clientid, string secret, string redirect, string refreshtoken, string authCode, string tenant, string resource = "user.read") { string result = string.Empty; MSGraphAuthTokens tokens = null; var url = $"https://login.microsoftonline.com/" + tenant + "/oauth2/v2.0/token";// AAD url = $"https://login.microsoftonline.com/common/oauth2/token";// live API using (var httpClient = new HttpClient()) { var properties = "client_id=" + clientid + "&client_secret=" + secret + "&scope=" + resource; properties += "&redirect_uri=" + redirect; if (!string.IsNullOrEmpty(refreshtoken)) { properties += "&refresh_token=" + refreshtoken + "&grant_type=refresh_token"; } if (!string.IsNullOrEmpty(authCode)) { properties += "&code=" + authCode + "&grant_type=authorization_code"; } var content = new StringContent(properties, Encoding.UTF8, "application/x-www-form-urlencoded"); var res = httpClient.PostAsync(url, content).Result; string resultJson = res.Content.ReadAsStringAsync().Result; if (res.IsSuccessStatusCode) { tokens = JsonConvert.DeserializeObject(resultJson); result = tokens.access_token; } } return result; } } public class MSGraphAuthTokens { public string access_token { get; set; } public string token_type { get; set; } public string expires_in { get; set; } public string scope { get; set; } public string refresh_token { get; set; } public string id_token { get; set; } } }%改行%Put a breakpoint after the line of putting the ‘signIn’ variable to the ‘ViewBag.IsSignin’ container (56th line) to confirm succeed acquiring token since the ‘sign’ variable is ‘true’.%改行%図（https://blog.processtune.com/wp-content/uploads/2020/03/signInConfirm.png）%改行%>>リンク（https://github.com/TetsuroTakao/GetUserInformation）%改行%Categories:%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/microsoft-azure/azure-active-directory/）リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/microsoft-azure/）リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/microsoft-graph/）リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/office-app/）リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/office-app/reactjs-net/）リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/slide-show/）リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/visual-studio/）リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/%e6%9c%aa%e5%88%86%e9%a1%9e/）%改行%Tags:%改行%No Tag%改行%Post navigation%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/2019/12/26/how-to-acquire-an-authorization-code-of-microsoft-graph-api-login-feature-with-asp-net-core-web-app/）%改行%Post navigation%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/2020/02/29/get-user-information-of-azure-active-directory-account-using-microsoft-graph-api/）%改行%No responses yet%改行%Leave a Replyリンク（https://blog.processtune.com/2020/01/31/programmable-authentication-flow-for-accessing-to-microsoft-graph/#respond）%改行%Your email address will not be published.Required fields are marked*%改行%Comment*%改行%Name*%改行%Email*%改行%Website%改行%Save my name, email, and website in this browser for the next time I comment.%改行%© 2025 Processtune Blog. Title: Local account creation – Processtune Blog
Categories: Active Directory,Azure Active Directory,Microservice,Microsoft Azure,SlideShow,Visual Studio,Windows 10 App,未分類
BodyText: %改行%リンク（#content）%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/）%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/microsoft-azure/）%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/microsoft-azure/azure-active-directory/）%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/visual-studio/）%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/microservice/）%改行%リンク（#）%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/）%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/microsoft-azure/）%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/microsoft-azure/azure-active-directory/）%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/visual-studio/）%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/microservice/）%改行%Local account creation%改行%byリンク（https://blog.processtune.com/author/tetsuro-takao/）%改行%onリンク（https://blog.processtune.com/2018/08/31/）%改行%This article explains how to create local account on a device which is not managed by Active Directory. These devices are assumed using at several dozens or hundreds places like Windows kiosk mode device.%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/?p=4590）explains [DataAccessLayer] class, this class provides logging feature. So the file name modify to ‘Logging.cs’. Then Visual Studio ask you “change refer to DataAccessLayer to Logging ?”, but it is ‘NOT’ need change. But you have to change declare of the [DataAccessLayer] class to ‘partial’.%改行%public partial class DataAccessLayer { ... }%改行%This article explain management feature of local account, so this feature is implemented at [DataAccessLayer] class too. And file name is ‘AccountManager.cs’%改行%リンク（https://github.com/TetsuroTakao/WPFLockdownSample3）%改行%図（https://blog.processtune.com/wp-content/uploads/2019/03/AccountManagement.png）%改行%This figure represents creating [DataAccessLayer] partial class of ‘AccountManager.cs’ added at ‘Features’ solution folder.%改行%・Two ways of account creation%改行%Perhaps, the most popular way to create local accounts is using cmdlets in Windows PowerShell, in C# programming uses ‘System.DirectoryServices.DirectoryEntry’ namespace and ‘Invoke’ method of ‘DirectoryEntry’ object, or use ‘PrincipalContext’ object of ‘System.DirectoryServices.AccountManagement’ namespace and ‘save’ method of ‘UserPrincipal’ object.%改行%These two ways are little bit different approaches. In case of ‘DirectoryEntry’ object, user object is com object and also group object is com object, it is both ‘DirectoryEntry’ object in C# aspect, and creation or deleting and so on are all using ‘Invoke’ method.%改行%In other hands, ‘System.DirectoryServices.AccountManagement’ namespace provides ‘UserPrincipal’ object and ‘GroupPrincipal’ object. ‘UserPrincipal’ object provides [DisplayName] property, [Name] property, [Description] property, [UserCannotChangePassword] property, [SetPassword] method and [Save] method. And ‘GroupPrincipal’ object provides [Members] property, it represents members collection of the group, so use [Add] method to add an user object to the member of a group, and then, use [Save] method of the object to commit operations of the group modifying. the steps using ‘System.DirectoryServices.AccountManagement’ is easy to understand because it is similar to the management UI of Windows OS.%改行%user.SetPassword(string.Empty); user.DisplayName = "somebody name"; user.Name = "somebody name"; user.Description = string.Empty; user.UserCannotChangePassword = false; user.PasswordNeverExpires = true; user.Save();%改行%図（https://blog.processtune.com/wp-content/uploads/2019/03/UserPropertyDialogGuest.png）%改行%リンク（https://github.com/TetsuroTakao/WPFLockdownSample4）%改行%・Create ‘CreateUsers’ method%改行%Partial class ‘DataAccessLayer’ aim to provide ‘CreateUsers’ method. The method argues user collection and responds logs collection.%改行%public partial class DataAccessLayer { PrincipalContext context { get { return new PrincipalContext(ContextType.Machine); } } public List<Log> CreateUsers(List<Tuple<string, List<string>>> users) { List<Log> result = new List<Log>(); Log log = null; UserPrincipal user = null; List<string> groups = null; try { foreach (var username in users) { user = UserPrincipal.FindByIdentity(context, username.Item1); if (user == null) { user = new UserPrincipal(context, username.Item1, username.Item1,true); } user.SetPassword(string.Empty); user.DisplayName = username.Item1; user.Name = username.Item1; user.Description = string.Empty; user.UserCannotChangePassword = false; user.PasswordNeverExpires = true; user.Save(); groups = username.Item2; if (groups == null) groups = new List<string>(); if (groups.Where(g => g == "Users").Count() == 0) groups.Add("Users"); SetUserGroups(user, groups); } //log "Users created" return result; } catch (Exception ex) { //log "Users creation fault reason : {0}", ex.Message return result; } } }%改行%The argument ‘users’ of above code includes a collection of ‘user’ object, this ‘user’ object has user name as key and groups name collection as value into ‘Tuple’ type, so argument ‘users’ enumelates for ‘FindByIdentity’ method to create ‘UserPrincipal’ object, then if it represents ‘null’, use ‘new’ to create target ‘UserPrincipal’. After correct ‘UserPrincipal’ object acquired, it is added to specific groups.%改行%・Create ‘SetUserGroups’ method%改行%The arguments of this method are ‘user’ object as ‘UserPrincipal’ type and groups name collection of the user. And responds log collection as execution result.%改行%public List<Log> SetUserGroups(UserPrincipal user, List<string> groups) { List<Log> result = new List<Log>(); Log log = new Log(); try { foreach (string groupname in groups) { log = new Log(); GroupPrincipal group = GroupPrincipal.FindByIdentity(context, groupname); group.Members.Add(user); group.Save(); //log "User[{0}] registered Group[{1}]", user.Name, groupname } //log "Groups of user[{0}] registering complete", user.Name return result; } catch (Exception ex) { //log "User[{0}] creation fault reason : {1}", user.Name, ex.Message return result; } }%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/?p=4513#scenariosOfThisArticle）is not create new group, So if your scenario needs new group, you should check ‘null’ with ‘group’ object as ‘GroupPrincipal’ type which acquired by using ‘FindByIdentity’ method. After correct ‘group’ object acquired, add user to the members of the group use ‘Add’ method of ‘Members’ property. Then use ‘Save’ method to commit the operations of the group.%改行%・Call account management with setting users object%改行%The last step is call ‘CreateUsers’ method of the [DtataAccessLayer] class.リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/?p=4513#scenariosOfThisArticle）aim to four account.%改行%図（https://blog.processtune.com/wp-content/uploads/2019/03/accountStructure.png）%改行%DataAccessLayer dataAccessLayer = new DataAccessLayer(); ... List<string> groups = new List<string>(); List<Tuple<string, List<string>>> users = new List<Tuple<string, List<string>>>(); groups.Add("Users"); groups.Add("Remote Desktop Users"); groups.Add("Administrators"); var user = new Tuple<string, List<string>>("superUser", groups); users.Add(user); groups = new List<string>(); groups.Add("Users"); groups.Add("Administrators"); user = new Tuple<string, List<string>>("maintenanceOperator", groups); users.Add(user); groups = new List<string>(); groups.Add("Users"); groups.Add("Remote Desktop Users"); user = new Tuple<string, List<string>>("appOperator", groups); users.Add(user); groups = new List<string>(); groups.Add("Users"); user = new Tuple<string, List<string>>("appUser", groups); users.Add(user); dataAccessLayer.CreateUsers(users);%改行%Categories:%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/technical-memo/active-directory/）リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/slide-show/）リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/visual-studio/）リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/winui/universal-apps/windows-store-app/windows-10-app/）リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/%e6%9c%aa%e5%88%86%e9%a1%9e/）%改行%Tags:%改行%No Tag%改行%Post navigation%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/2018/07/28/logging-file-save-to-microsoft-azure-part-1/）%改行%Post navigation%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/2018/10/27/desktop-ui-controlling/）%改行%No responses yet%改行%Leave a Replyリンク（https://blog.processtune.com/2018/08/31/local-account-creation/#respond）%改行%Your email address will not be published.Required fields are marked*%改行%Comment*%改行%Name*%改行%Email*%改行%Website%改行%Save my name, email, and website in this browser for the next time I comment.%改行%© 2025 Processtune Blog. Title: Should define layout at first if you choice atomic design – Processtune Blog
Categories: Azure Active Directory,Microservice,Microsoft Azure,Visual Studio,未分類
BodyText: %改行%リンク（#content）%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/）%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/microsoft-azure/）%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/microsoft-azure/azure-active-directory/）%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/visual-studio/）%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/microservice/）%改行%リンク（#）%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/）%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/microsoft-azure/）%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/microsoft-azure/azure-active-directory/）%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/visual-studio/）%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/microservice/）%改行%Should define layout at first if you choice atomic design%改行%byリンク（https://blog.processtune.com/author/tetsuro-takao/）%改行%onリンク（https://blog.processtune.com/2021/05/01/）%改行%Atomic design is useful for web app creating. Especially in microservices architecture design pattern that you might use atomic design to develop web app, because contents of web app needs to be mashed up an information from several individual domains. Even if information of contents is provided from single domain, it might to make controlled several divided transactions for managing a state that leads web users to completing their task.%改行%Though many of articles involving atomic design is focused in visual design, those describe how to create page from atomic element. Of course readers of those articles may be professional designers, so those articles not described about layout element tags and content element tags.%改行%This article does not describe atomic design itself, but describes process of team development using layout container for microservices or atomic design. I think it is very important that layout definition should be done at first, then atomic designed element that not includes layout definition will be injected into layout containers.%改行%>>リンク（https://github.com/TetsuroTakao/LayoutForAtomicDesign）%改行%・Define region and contents position%改行%CSS(Cascading Style Sheets) 4 and HTML 5 are supported by modern browsers. And many web developers are familiar to the JQuery.%改行%In process, HTML tags are defined with style attributes for region layout, and then define containers of contents, these containers map to resions. It is important that it not use tricky tactics but using these standerd technologies.%改行%図（https://blog.processtune.com/wp-content/uploads/2022/02/RegionDefinision.png）%改行%・Create web app%改行%Ok, create web app useリンク（https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/dotnet/core/tools/dotnet-new）command. You can use template input [dotnet new mvc] into comannd prompt or Windows tarminal, also tarminal in Visual Studio Code. So you can find several files auto generated at work(current) directory.%改行%図（https://blog.processtune.com/wp-content/uploads/2022/02/dotnetnewmvc.png）%改行%Open folder In Visual Studio Code, then prompt applicationUrl part of launchSettings.json(left below) at [Properties] in file explorer. Then put Ctrl+F5(without debug) to run web host only.%改行%if debug controller(right below) shown, it represents web already hosted, so you can use web browser for display applicationUrl value.%改行%図（https://blog.processtune.com/wp-content/uploads/2022/02/launchsettings.png）図（https://blog.processtune.com/wp-content/uploads/2022/02/debugController.png）%改行%図（https://blog.processtune.com/wp-content/uploads/2022/02/welcome.png）%改行%・_Layout.chtml and @RenderBody()%改行%After confirming template running, in case of MVC Web App, it is better that you modify _Layout.chtml to create container of atomic design elements.%改行%_Layout.chtml is changed to structed just only heaher tag, @RenderBody() and footer tag, this container(place holder of individual chtml files in Views folder) is full devided from shared CSS design(bootstrap) of _Layout.chtml. Delete div tag that has container class and main tag. Only @RenderBody() is between header tag and footer tag.%改行%図（https://blog.processtune.com/wp-content/uploads/2022/02/layoutchtml.png）%改行%Just only simple sample, some organization that controls main part of solution is charge main menu and footer, it mapped on 48 peaces of regions, and provide it for allience companies or development teams.%改行%It is important that these teams can create part as atomic design element that is not relay with layout, because main organization provides only pure layout without looks and feels of content elements.%改行%図（https://blog.processtune.com/wp-content/uploads/2022/02/48regions.png）%改行%・Coding description%改行%This is base of 48 compartments created in index.chtml.%改行%図（https://blog.processtune.com/wp-content/uploads/2022/02/regionbase.png）%改行%Container2, 3 and 4 are same as Container1.%改行%As body tag has only header tag, footer tag and @RenderBody, a row height is quoter of excludes footer height and header height. Although @RenderBody height is calculeted at first, because height property value of body tag is ‘auto’.%改行%@section scripts{ <script> window.addEventListener("load", function () { var bodyHeight = $(document).height() - ( $("footer").height() + parseInt($("footer").css("border-top-width").replace("px","")) + parseInt($("nav").css("margin-bottom").replace("px","")) ); $("body").css("height", bodyHeight + "px"); var rowHeight = ($("body").height() - $("header").height()) / 4; ... }); </script> }%改行%Define compartments height.%改行%The part of for loop of j index is for description of this article, it is not important, but code below is the part of definision of compartments height.%改行%@section scripts{ <script> window.addEventListener("load", function () { ...(code above) for(var i = 0; i < 4; i++){ for(var j = 10; j < 22; j++){ $("#Container" + (i + 1).toString() + (j + 1).toString()).html((i + 1).toString() + (j + 1).toString()); $("#Container" + (i + 1).toString() + (j + 1).toString()).css("text-align","center"); $("#Container" + (i + 1).toString() + (j + 1).toString()).css("vertical-align","middle"); $("#Container" + (i + 1).toString() + (j + 1).toString()).css("border", "thin solid #cceeee"); } $("#Container" + (i + 1).toString()).css("height", rowHeight.toString() + "px"); } }); </script> }%改行%At last, any contents regions are mapped on compartments by JavaScript. Contents are defined in div tag of ShelveContainer at first.%改行%For exsample, this ShelveContainer includes 4 contents, and contents regions are mapped on compartments respectively by JavaScript. Of course, borders and colors are for description of this article.%改行%図（https://blog.processtune.com/wp-content/uploads/2022/02/ShelveContainer.png）%改行%@section scripts{ <script> window.addEventListener("load", function () { ...(code above) ContentStructure($("#MainCompanyContainer"), 3, 4, $("#Container111"),"rgba(32, 201, 151, 0.3)"); ContentStructure($("#MicroService1"), 9, 2, $("#Container114"),"rgba(255, 192, 203, 0.3)"); ContentStructure($("#MicroService2"), 7, 2, $("#Container314"),"rgba(0, 255, 255, 0.3)"); ContentStructure($("#MicroService3"), 2, 2, $("#Container321"),"rgba(75, 0, 130, 0.3)"); }); function ContentStructure(container, w, h, base, color){ var cells = [w,h]; var borderWidth = cells[0] * 2; var borderheight = cells[1] * 2; container.css("width",($("#Container111").width() * cells[0] + borderWidth).toString() + "px"); container.css("height",($("#Container111").height() * cells[1] + borderheight).toString() + "px"); container.css("line-height",container.css("height")); container.css("text-align","center"); container.css("top",base.position().top); container.css("left",base.position().left); container.css("background-color",color); container.append(container.attr("id")); $("#RootContainer").append(container); } </script> }%改行%・Conclution%改行%Atomic design approach is useful for look and feel design or components aspect development, it affects to creating cost of microservice solution. If it started as bad approach, Atomic design approach consume more cost than common development process.%改行%When layout definition is devided from element look & feel completely, Atomic design approach gives you a lot of advantage%改行%>>リンク（https://github.com/TetsuroTakao/LayoutForAtomicDesign）%改行%Categories:%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/%e6%9c%aa%e5%88%86%e9%a1%9e/）%改行%Tags:%改行%No Tag%改行%Post navigation%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/2020/10/01/you-can-share-the-devices-profile-of-chrome-and-edge/）%改行%Post navigation%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/2021/07/01/qr-code-create-with-rust-language-runable-on-azure-functions/）%改行%No responses yet%改行%Leave a Replyリンク（https://blog.processtune.com/2021/05/01/should-define-layout-at-first-if-you-choice-atomic-design/#respond）%改行%Your email address will not be published.Required fields are marked*%改行%Comment*%改行%Name*%改行%Email*%改行%Website%改行%Save my name, email, and website in this browser for the next time I comment.%改行%© 2025 Processtune Blog.
