Title: How to acquire an authorization code for Microsoft Graph API with login feature of ASP.NET Core web app – Processtune Blog
Categories: Azure Active Directory,Microservice,Microsoft Azure,Microsoft Graph,Office App,Office Web App,SlideShow,Visual Studio
BodyText: %改行%リンク（#content）%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/）%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/microsoft-azure/）%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/microsoft-azure/azure-active-directory/）%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/visual-studio/）%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/microservice/）%改行%リンク（#）%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/）%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/microsoft-azure/）%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/microsoft-azure/azure-active-directory/）%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/visual-studio/）%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/microservice/）%改行%How to acquire an authorization code for Microsoft Graph API with login feature of ASP.NET Core web app%改行%図（https://blog.processtune.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/03/AuthenticationAuthorizationSeriesTitle.png）%改行%byリンク（https://blog.processtune.com/author/tetsuro-takao/）%改行%onリンク（https://blog.processtune.com/2019/12/26/）%改行%Microsoft Graph API provides a super-easy way to create an app that includes an authentication feature for the Azure Active Directory account. And Azure portal provides a code sample of various types of app. Its code sample has vast features such as login, logout or login state cache and so on using the Microsoft Identity Platform.%改行%This article describes how to acquire the authorization code in case of excepting using the Microsoft Identity Platform.%改行%>>リンク（https://github.com/TetsuroTakao/ShowUserInformation）(refer to the annotation at the end of this article)%改行%・Create client app with ReactJS.NET%改行%Please create the ASP.NET Core web app ofリンク（https://reactjs.net/）as the ‘reactnet-webpack’, if needs please refer toリンク（https://blog.processtune.com/?p=5032）which described creating the ASP.NET Core web app of ReactJS.NET as ‘reactnet-vanilla’. This article describes instead of it, how to create the ASP.NET Core web app of ReactJS.NET as ‘リンク（https://reactjs.net/features/react-helmet.html）‘.%改行%図（https://blog.processtune.com/wp-content/uploads/2020/03/reacthelmet.png）%改行%Create an app project a folder at any place and launch the PowerShell then put a command like a web page above.%改行%***> dotnet new -i React.Template ***> dotnet new reactnet-webpack ***> dotnet run%改行%***any place%改行%Wait for the BuildServiceProvider builds the service once for the first time.%改行%The hosting environment is launched(refer toリンク（https://blog.processtune.com/?p=5032）), press [Ctrl]+[C] to terminate it, and close the PowerShell.%改行%Launch windows explorer and go to the folder, then open created solution file with Visual Studio(open .sln file at second time or .csproj file at first time).%改行%図（https://blog.processtune.com/wp-content/uploads/2020/03/openSolution.png）%改行%At first, build a solution before do debug, when the build has completed, Visual studio can launch a host and a client both. So when Visual Studio can launch the app, Select [React.Sample.Webpack.CoreMvc] list item of [Start Debugging] box and press it. After building the project complete, Visual Studio launches the console as the host and the browser as the client app.%改行%図（https://blog.processtune.com/wp-content/uploads/2020/03/reactJShost.png）図（https://blog.processtune.com/wp-content/uploads/2020/03/reactjsDemoHome.png）%改行%Modify a view and a controller instead of using the React Component for now. Open a controller from the Solution Explorer, open the HomeController.cs in [Controllers] folder of [SampleApp] project and insert three rows above 40th ([return View…] ) row.%改行%図（https://blog.processtune.com/wp-content/uploads/2020/03/ModifyHomeController.png）%改行%Next, to modify a view, open the _Layout.cshtml in [Shared] folder of [Views] folder of [SampleApp] project. And insert the below code in the first child of [Body] tag (between the [Body] start tag and the [@RenderBody] row).%改行%図（https://blog.processtune.com/wp-content/uploads/2020/03/ModifyLayoutChtml.png）%改行%Pres F5 key to run the app.%改行%図（https://blog.processtune.com/wp-content/uploads/2020/03/setNavibar.png）%改行%Swap the 17th row ‘Signin’ character of the _Layout.cshtml to the below code.%改行% %改行%This SVG code created atリンク（https://editor.method.ac/）, so it is OK which using text or using SVG. It is important that parameters of [a] tag. In the case of using Method Draw, open the site and create some draw, select [View] menu item and select [Source…] after that, copy the code and paste to _Layout.cshtml.%改行%図（https://blog.processtune.com/wp-content/uploads/2020/03/methodDraw.png）%改行%図（https://blog.processtune.com/wp-content/uploads/2020/03/ViewSource.png）図（https://blog.processtune.com/wp-content/uploads/2020/03/CopySource.png）%改行%Pres F5 key to run the app.%改行%図（https://blog.processtune.com/wp-content/uploads/2020/03/setLogin.png）%改行%The next step is creating a controller and an action for [href] attribute of [a] tag. Create ‘SignIn’ action in the ‘Account’ controller which adds to [Controllers] folder at the Solution Explorer as AccountController.cs file.%改行%Right-click on the [Controllers] folder and select the [Add] item, continue to select the [Class] item.%改行%図（https://blog.processtune.com/wp-content/uploads/2020/03/addcontroller.png）%改行%図（https://blog.processtune.com/wp-content/uploads/2020/03/addAccountcontroller.png）%改行%Define the below code in the AccountController.cs file.%改行%using Microsoft.AspNetCore.Mvc; namespace React.Sample.Webpack.CoreMvc.Controllers { public class AccountController : Controller { [Route("Account/SignIn")] [ResponseCache(Location = ResponseCacheLocation.None, NoStore = true)] public void SignIn() { var tenant = "__YOUR TENANT__";//this grant_type allows common var clientId = "__APP CLIENT ID__"; var redirectUri = "http://localhost:9457/home"; Response.Redirect("https://login.microsoftonline.com/" + tenant + "/oauth2/v2.0/authorize?client_id=" + clientId + "&redirect_uri=" + redirectUri + "&grant_type=implicit&response_type=code&scope=User.Read" ); } } }%改行%Insert below code into the first line in [Index] action of the HomeController.cs file.%改行%var query = this.Url.ActionContext.HttpContext.Request.Query; var code = string.Empty; var state = string.Empty; if (query.ContainsKey("code")) code = query["code"]; if (query.ContainsKey("session_state")) state = query["session_state"];%改行%Set a breakpoint anywhere after the place that taking the ‘state’ string variable.%改行%図（https://blog.processtune.com/wp-content/uploads/2020/03/code.png）図（https://blog.processtune.com/wp-content/uploads/2020/03/state.png）%改行%>>リンク（https://github.com/TetsuroTakao/ShowUserInformation）%改行%This sample not includes the [npm] and the [Packeges].%改行%図（https://blog.processtune.com/wp-content/uploads/2020/03/nugetList.png）%改行%Categories:%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/microsoft-azure/azure-active-directory/）リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/microsoft-azure/）リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/microsoft-graph/）リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/office-app/）リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/office-app/office-web-app/）リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/slide-show/）リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/visual-studio/）%改行%Tags:%改行%No Tag%改行%Post navigation%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/2019/11/30/download-a-client-app-includes-an-authentication-feature-for-azure-active-directory-account/）%改行%Post navigation%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/2020/01/31/programmable-authentication-flow-for-accessing-to-microsoft-graph/）%改行%No responses yet%改行%Leave a Replyリンク（https://blog.processtune.com/2019/12/26/how-to-acquire-an-authorization-code-of-microsoft-graph-api-login-feature-with-asp-net-core-web-app/#respond）%改行%Your email address will not be published.Required fields are marked*%改行%Comment*%改行%Name*%改行%Email*%改行%Website%改行%Save my name, email, and website in this browser for the next time I comment.%改行%© 2025 Processtune Blog. Title: MVVM: Create some processings if these should execute before adding to a list items. – Processtune Blog
Categories: Azure Active Directory,Microservice,Microsoft Azure,SlideShow,Universal Apps,Visual Studio,Windows 8 App,Windows 8.1 App,Windows Store App,未分類
BodyText: %改行%リンク（#content）%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/）%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/microsoft-azure/）%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/microsoft-azure/azure-active-directory/）%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/visual-studio/）%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/microservice/）%改行%リンク（#）%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/）%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/microsoft-azure/）%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/microsoft-azure/azure-active-directory/）%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/visual-studio/）%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/microservice/）%改行%MVVM: Create some processings if these should execute before adding to a list items.%改行%byリンク（https://blog.processtune.com/author/tetsuro-takao/）%改行%onリンク（https://blog.processtune.com/2015/05/10/）%改行%When you provide any list, it’s items would not enough for user, not always. Accordingly, a ComboBox control is provided for developers. However, I want to include some processings before adding to list items occasionally. So, I use a ListBox( or ListView) control and a TextBox contorol with a Button control.%改行%This article describes these synchronization.%改行%リンク（http://1drv.ms/1PyKwdk）%改行%・Create Visual Studio Solution of MVVM design pattern type%改行%First, create visual studio solution from template of blank universal app (c#). And then, create class files in the shared project as a model( Artist model) and a view model( MainPage view model), a facade( Data access layer). See below.%改行%図（http://processtuneblog.azurewebsites.net/wp-content/uploads/2015/05/SolutionStructure.png）%改行%・Create XAML page%改行%Next, create XAML codes of a ListView control and a TextBox control, a Button control.%改行%<ListView x:Name="ArtistListView" Grid.Row="1" Grid.ColumnSpan="2" Margin="100,10,100,0" BorderBrush="White" BorderThickness="1" Header="Artist List" ItemsSource="{Binding ArtistListViewItemsSource}"> <ListView.ItemTemplate> <DataTemplate> <StackPanel Orientation="Horizontal"> <TextBlock Text="{Binding FirstName}"/> <TextBlock Text="{Binding LastName}" Margin="10,0,0,0"/> </StackPanel> </DataTemplate> </ListView.ItemTemplate> </ListView> <StackPanel Grid.Row="2" VerticalAlignment="Center" > <TextBlock Text="Separate by a space between firstname and lastname, if necessary." HorizontalAlignment="Right"/> <TextBox x:Name="AddContent" Text="{Binding AddContentText,Mode=TwoWay}" Width="300" Height="50" HorizontalAlignment="Right" PlaceholderText="Enter if it is not in the list."/> </StackPanel> <Button x:Name="AddToListButton" Content="Add to the list" Margin="10,0,0,0" Grid.Row="2" Grid.Column="1" Command="{Binding AddToListButtonCommand}" />%改行%図（http://processtuneblog.azurewebsites.net/wp-content/uploads/2015/05/XAMLView.png）%改行%・Create View Model%改行%A mechanism of those controls relaying each other is that a Command property of a Button control sets a Text property of a TextBox control to a ItemsSource property of a ListView. ICommand type property in this view model is extended by custom type that has “public Action ExecuteAction {get;set;}” property. This is a view model codes below.%改行%class addToListCommand : ICommand { // extends this property public Action ExecuteAction {get;set;} public bool CanExecute(object parameter) { return true; } public event EventHandler CanExecuteChanged; public void Execute(object parameter) { ExecuteAction(); } } addToListCommand addToListButtonCommand { get; set; } // bind to a Command property of the Button public ICommand AddToListButtonCommand { get { if (addToListButtonCommand == null) { addToListButtonCommand = new addToListCommand(); // This "AddContentText" is a property of the TextBox control. addToListButtonCommand.ExecuteAction = () => AddToArtistList(AddContentText); } return addToListButtonCommand; } }%改行%The command executes “AddToArtistList(AddContentText);” setted from external code via the ExecuteAction property. “AddContentText” argument is a property binding to a Text property of the TextBox. And “AddToArtistList” method do the process that separates a string given to two or three strings as first name or last name. The view model codes is below.%改行%public ObservableCollection<ArtistModel> ArtistListViewItemsSource { get; private set; } public void AddToArtistList(string name) { artistModel = new ArtistModel(); string[] names = name.Split(' '); artistModel.FirstName = names[0]; if (names.Length > 1) artistModel.LastName = names[names.Length - 1]; if (!string.IsNullOrEmpty(artistModel.FirstName)) { ArtistListViewItemsSource.Add(artistModel); RaisePropertyChanged("ArtistListViewItemsSource"); } }%改行%・Windows 8.1 or later%改行%An information inputted by user on button operation add to the list. It is easier to create on the platform as Windows 8.1 or later.%改行%<Button x:Name="AddToListFromFlyoutButton" Content="Enter if it is not in the list" HorizontalAlignment="Center" Margin="0,50,0,0"> <Button.Flyout> <Flyout> <StackPanel> <StackPanel Orientation="Horizontal"> <TextBox Header="First Name" Text="{Binding FirstNameTextBoxText, Mode=TwoWay, UpdateSourceTrigger=PropertyChanged}" Width="100"/> <TextBox Header="Last Name" Text="{Binding LastNameTextBoxText, Mode=TwoWay, UpdateSourceTrigger=PropertyChanged}" Width="100" Margin="10,0,0,0"/> </StackPanel> <Button x:Name="ListContentAddingButton" Content="{Binding ListContentAddingButtonContent}" HorizontalAlignment="Right" Margin="0,10,0,0" Click="ListContentAddingButtonContent_Click" IsEnabled="{Binding ListContentAddingButtonIsEnabled}"/> </StackPanel> </Flyout> </Button.Flyout> </Button>%改行%図（http://processtuneblog.azurewebsites.net/wp-content/uploads/2015/05/XAMLView2.png）%改行%A name input area is able to divide first name and last name using a Flyout control. And a IsEnabled property or a Content property of a Button control is able to change per inputting a character to a TextBox control because a Binding.UpdateSourceTrigger property of it execute setter method of the property that change a IsEnabled property or a Content property of a Button control.%改行%Consequently, create a process on a click event handler that closing a Flyout control and calling “AddToArtistList” method in the view model.%改行%private void ListContentAddingButtonContent_Click(object sender, RoutedEventArgs e) { Flyout f = this.AddToListFromFlyoutButton.Flyout as Flyout; if(f != null) f.Hide(); (DataContext as MainPageViewModel).AddToArtistList(null); }%改行%Modify “AddToArtistList” method in the view model to a code below.%改行%string firstNameTextBoxText { get; set; } public string FirstNameTextBoxText { get { return firstNameTextBoxText; } set { firstNameTextBoxText = value; RaisePropertyChanged("ListContentAddingButtonContent"); RaisePropertyChanged("ListContentAddingButtonIsEnabled"); } } string lastNameTextBoxText { get; set; } public string LastNameTextBoxText { get { return lastNameTextBoxText; } set { lastNameTextBoxText = value; RaisePropertyChanged("ListContentAddingButtonContent"); } } public string ListContentAddingButtonContent { get { string result = "O K"; if (!string.IsNullOrEmpty(FirstNameTextBoxText)) { result += ": " + FirstNameTextBoxText; if (!string.IsNullOrEmpty(LastNameTextBoxText)) result += " " + LastNameTextBoxText; } return result; } } public bool ListContentAddingButtonIsEnabled { get { return (!string.IsNullOrEmpty(FirstNameTextBoxText)); } } public void AddToArtistList(string name) { artistModel = new ArtistModel(); if (!string.IsNullOrEmpty(FirstNameTextBoxText)) { artistModel.FirstName = FirstNameTextBoxText; if (!string.IsNullOrEmpty(LastNameTextBoxText)) artistModel.LastName = LastNameTextBoxText; } if (!string.IsNullOrEmpty(artistModel.FirstName)) { ArtistListViewItemsSource.Add(artistModel); RaisePropertyChanged("ArtistListViewItemsSource"); } //Serialize and save to local. Refer to sample code about Dal. Dal.SetLocalArtistsData(ArtistListViewItemsSource); }%改行%図（http://processtuneblog.azurewebsites.net/wp-content/uploads/2015/05/AppView.png）%改行%Categories:%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/slide-show/）リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/winui/universal-apps/）リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/winui/universal-apps/windows-store-app/windows-8-app/）リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/winui/universal-apps/windows-store-app/windows-8-1-app/）リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/winui/universal-apps/windows-store-app/）リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/%e6%9c%aa%e5%88%86%e9%a1%9e/）%改行%Tags:%改行%No Tag%改行%Post navigation%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/2015/02/10/%e3%82%bf%e3%82%a4%e3%83%ab%e3%81%a8%e3%83%90%e3%83%83%e3%82%b8%e3%81%ae%e3%83%a9%e3%82%a4%e3%83%95%e3%82%b5%e3%82%a4%e3%82%af%e3%83%ab%e9%81%8b%e7%94%a8%ef%bc%88microsoft-azure-mobile-service/）%改行%Post navigation%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/2015/05/23/mvvm-creating-reusable-flyout-control-composite/）%改行%No responses yet%改行%Leave a Replyリンク（https://blog.processtune.com/2015/05/10/mvvm-create-some-processings-if-these-should-execute-before-adding-to-a-list-items/#respond）%改行%Your email address will not be published.Required fields are marked*%改行%Comment*%改行%Name*%改行%Email*%改行%Website%改行%Save my name, email, and website in this browser for the next time I comment.%改行%© 2025 Processtune Blog. Title: Design of solution with gRPC communication – Processtune Blog
Categories: ASP.NET Core,Azure Active Directory,C#,gRPC,Microservice,Microsoft Azure,Service,SlideShow,Technical Memo,Visual Studio
BodyText: %改行%リンク（#content）%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/）%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/microsoft-azure/）%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/microsoft-azure/azure-active-directory/）%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/visual-studio/）%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/microservice/）%改行%リンク（#）%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/）%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/microsoft-azure/）%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/microsoft-azure/azure-active-directory/）%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/visual-studio/）%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/microservice/）%改行%Design of solution with gRPC communication%改行%図（https://blog.processtune.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/03/SeriesTitle.png）%改行%byリンク（https://blog.processtune.com/author/tetsuro-takao/）%改行%onリンク（https://blog.processtune.com/2022/04/30/）%改行%リンク（https://github.com/grpc）is one of theリンク（https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Remote_procedure_call）, It was developed by Google. They needed a messaging protocol that can respond on the distributed computing environment in real-time when users operateリンク（https://www.cloudflare.com/learning/network-layer/what-is-the-control-plane/）of the Google Cloud. And it was leveraged for maintaining of the Control plane of Google Cloud also, to keep high quality cloud services.%改行%The gRPC usesリンク（https://cloud.google.com/blog/products/gcp/grpc-a-true-internet-scale-rpc-framework-is-now-1-and-ready-for-production-deployments?hl=en）they created: a framework of one of the RPC. Then the gRPC become to open source, and it has be the incubating project ofリンク（https://www.cncf.io/）since 2017.%改行%Microsoft supports many development environments for an application using the gRPC-Web and the gRPC-over-HTTP/2 (see Microsoft official document ‘リンク（https://docs.microsoft.com/ja-jp/aspnet/core/grpc/supported-platforms?view=aspnetcore-6.0&WT.mc_id=DT-MVP-4029060）‘).%改行%This article describe mechanism of .NET application using the gRPC architecture and an application that uses the gRPC works as the Message Oriented Middleware.%改行%The article ‘リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/?p=6649）‘ describes how to implement gRPC application service from Azure App Service.%改行%• gRPC-dotnet%改行%The gRPC-dotnet is implementation of .NET that is to use theリンク（https://grpc.io/blog/grpc-stacks/）. It’s structed with namespaces ofリンク（https://www.nuget.org/packages/Grpc.AspNetCore）andリンク（https://www.nuget.org/packages/Grpc.AspNetCore.Server.Reflection）. For creation of these stub, namespacesリンク（https://www.nuget.org/packages/Grpc.Net.Client）,リンク（https://www.nuget.org/packages/Grpc.Tools/2.51.0）, andリンク（https://www.nuget.org/packages/Google.Protobuf）architecture are used.%改行%The Grpc.AspNetCore namespace is the gRPC service itself, and the Grpc.AspNetCore.Server.Reflection namespace works for providing a description of the gRPC service to client apps just like theリンク（https://swagger.io/）.%改行%On the other hand, the Grpc.Tools namespace is auto generate a stub of client side that usesリンク（https://protobuf.dev/）. The Grpc.Tools namespace generates a stub with the Google.Protobuf architecture, The Google.Protobuf called from the Grpc.Tools namespace is pure C# implementation that does not use native component of the gRPC core stack.%改行%There was no HTTP/2 library of C# until gRPC-dotnet is developed, so gRPC apps of C# were developed with gRPC.Core that includes HTTP/2 library. The gRPC.Core namespace moved to the state of the Maintenance Only at May 2021, then at May 2022, it was in the state of the Deprecated (Please refer to the great article ‘リンク（https://grpc.io/blog/grpc-csharp-future/）‘).%改行%図（https://blog.processtune.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/02/gRPC-dotnet.png）%改行%• The gRPC is a protocol for the Message Oriented Middleware.%改行%The gRPC extends the Remote Procedure Call (RPC).リンク（https://grpc.io/）says, ‘A high performance, open-source universal RPC framework’.%改行%The first description is an aspect of universal RPC framework, it can be used in multiple languages and can enforce service contracts on multiple first- and third-party services using HTTP/2. It means that the gRPC can generate clients and servers in multiple languages, generated code is able to run on each language environment (C++, Java, Go, Node, Ruby, Python and C# across Linux, Windows and Mac). The gRPC as a framework is universal because it supports also development on many environment. For instance, mobile apps development of iOS and Android is supported with Objective-C and Android Java libraries, it can execute single-line installation in most languages.%改行%The RPC calls any functions on destination that are an object, in other words, service contracts have to describe the objects, so gRPC use the Protocol Buffers for run on universal environment. The Protocol Buffers are a language-neutral, platform-neutral extensible mechanism for serializing structured data (quote fromリンク（https://protobuf.dev/）). It can describe objects of services side and edge side both.%改行%The second description is an aspect of high performance of the gRPC, availability of object description affects streaming or unary flow control from each side of service and edge. In the case of using the forward error correction (FEC) mechanism for error handling, it is better that an object error is supported at communication level same as a connection error instead of at an application level. An access control of authentication & authorization typically uses JSON as a container of a security token, object description of the Protocol Buffers is useful for transfer of a security token too, also useful for serialization/deserialization. The RPC extends of the gRPC are also useful at other aspects such as distributed tracing, binding as synchronization/asynchronization, response of cancel/timeout, health checking, load balancing, logging and monitoring and tracing etc..%改行%The gRPC was developed for distributed computing. Distributed computing has the Message-Oriented Middleware, the Distributed Directory, the Service Discovery, the Stateful Middle Tier, the Stateless Actor Activator and so on. There are many types in the Message Oriented Middleware such as the Pub-Sub, the Message Queue, the Fan-out, the Request/Response, the Point to Point, the Remote Procedure Call and so forth. The Remote Procedure Call run on several type protocols like the HTTP, the HTTP/2, the TCP/IP, the Internet InterORB protocol. The gRPC is extends HTTP/2 with the Protocol Buffers. Thus, the gRPC has to be used in correct situation, in other words, what kind of a solution architecture, what part of the system or which direction of procedures call.%改行%図（https://blog.processtune.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/02/gRPCInDistributedComputing.png）%改行%The gRPC is like as the WCF or the DCOM that uses the Distributed Object in synchronize communication although, it can communicate asynchronously, and also can bidirectional communication between service and client because it transfer objects with the HTT/2 protocol.%改行%The Distributed Object of unary RPC makes a stub of a service side object on a client side, then a client executes a client-side object. In other hand, the gRPC transfers a description of service side object, then a client side calls service side procedure.%改行%図（https://blog.processtune.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/02/gRPCommunicationDirection.png）%改行%If you familiar with synchronize communication on the DCOM or the WCF architecture, aware consistency of a state of services on the gRPC communication. In the case of a unary RPC, a client-side application uses same object of service side as stub, thus typically the application care about consistency of only service side state although, in contrast a state of client side or service side is not guaranteed on the gRPC communication because the gRPC communication uses remotely individual object of service side or client side and usually communicate with asynchronously. Though the gRPC can communicate as synchronously for waiting remote procedure response like unary RPC, if use the Backend for Frontends pattern for design of cloud native solution, it needs design as distributed computing like Microservices. Please refer to the website of Sam Newman (‘リンク（https://samnewman.io/patterns/architectural/bff/）‘), and Microsoft official document (‘リンク（https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/architecture/patterns/backends-for-frontends?WT.mc_id=AZ-MVP-4029060）‘).%改行%図（https://blog.processtune.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/02/BackendForFrontends.png）%改行%ⓘ Recomended reference%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/?p=6649）%改行%Categories:%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/asp-net-core/）リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/c/）リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/microsoft-azure/grpc/）リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/asp-net-core/service/）リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/slide-show/）リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/technical-memo/）%改行%Tags:%改行%No Tag%改行%Post navigation%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/2022/02/01/node-mongodb-container-for-azure-static-web-apps/）%改行%Post navigation%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/2022/05/30/asp-net-core-grpc-grpc-communication-tools/）%改行%No responses yet%改行%Leave a Replyリンク（https://blog.processtune.com/2022/04/30/design-of-solution-with-grpc-communication/#respond）%改行%Your email address will not be published.Required fields are marked*%改行%Comment*%改行%Name*%改行%Email*%改行%Website%改行%Save my name, email, and website in this browser for the next time I comment.%改行%© 2025 Processtune Blog. Title: How to compile Rust assembly runnable on Linux for deployment to Azure Functions – Processtune Blog
Categories: Azure Active Directory,Azure Functions,Microservice,Microsoft Azure,Rust,SlideShow,Visual Studio,Visual Studio Code
BodyText: %改行%リンク（#content）%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/）%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/microsoft-azure/）%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/microsoft-azure/azure-active-directory/）%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/visual-studio/）%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/microservice/）%改行%リンク（#）%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/）%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/microsoft-azure/）%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/microsoft-azure/azure-active-directory/）%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/visual-studio/）%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/microservice/）%改行%How to compile Rust assembly runnable on Linux for deployment to Azure Functions%改行%図（https://blog.processtune.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/03/ContainerizationSeriesTitle.png）%改行%byリンク（https://blog.processtune.com/author/tetsuro-takao/）%改行%onリンク（https://blog.processtune.com/2021/08/01/）%改行%Azure Functions is one of great services of serverless computing. It is useful for microservices solution, event-driven messaging middleware and so on. Its functions run on Linux, use C standard library named ‘musl'(seeリンク（https://musl.libc.org/）) for system call of Linux.%改行%A runnable Rust assembly of Azure Functions is create use ‘cargo build’ command that compiles source code to assembly, compile process is structed with front-end compiler and back-end compiler.%改行%At first, front-end compiler generates intermidiate language from source code written on Windows, then back-end compiler use its intermidiate language to make runnable assemblyies that capable system call on Linux. This process is helped by toolchain management tool ‘rustup’.%改行%There is little bit complexity to struct these development enviromment, so this article describes it step by step.%改行%In the article [Rust QR code generator App of Azure Functions] describes how to modify the simple of this article (simple rust project) to QR code generator.%改行%Added at 17th Dec. 2021 :%改行%Please read Microsoft Official document [リンク（https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/azure-functions/create-first-function-vs-code-other?tabs=rust%2Cwindows?WT.mc_id=AZ-MVP-4029060）].%改行%>>リンク（https://github.com/TetsuroTakao/QRCodeGenerator）(created byリンク（https://blog.processtune.com/?p=5925）.)%改行%・Step 1. Rust install and rustup settings comformation%改行%Install Rust (seeリンク（https://www.rust-lang.org/tools/install）), then comfirm rustup toolchain conditions, it shown if put [rustup show] command in integrated terminal of Visual Studio Code (or Windows terminal).%改行%Runtime environment of Azure Functions is [x86_64-unknown-linux-musl], so if it needs, use [rustup target add stable-x86_64-unknown-linux-musl] command to install and use [cargo build] command to build it. Below figure is sample of [rustup show] result.%改行%Default host: x86_64-pc-windows-msvc rustup home: %User root%\.rustup installed toolchains -------------------- stable-x86_64-pc-windows-msvc (default) stable-x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu stable-x86_64-unknown-linux-musl installed targets for active toolchain -------------------------------------- wasm32-unknown-unknown x86_64-pc-windows-msvc x86_64-unknown-linux-musl active toolchain ---------------- stable-x86_64-pc-windows-msvc (default) rustc 1.56.1 (59eed8a2a 2021-11-01)%改行%This sample environment has installed [x86_64-unknown-linux-musl] target, and [stable-x86_64-pc-windows-msvc] toolchain is target default (default of Rust install on Windows). This sample development environment is Windows and use native ABI (Application Binary Interface) named MSVC (seeリンク（https://rust-lang.github.io/rustup/installation/windows.html）for Windows Rust developers). So developers who use Visual Studio (includes Visual Studio Code) for development have to do nothing. If not, useリンク（https://visualstudio.microsoft.com/visual-cpp-build-tools/?WT.mc_id=AZ-MVP-4029060）to compile Linux assembly easily.%改行%The musl is C standerd compiler of MIT license, it will be linked as statical, so should modify the [.cargo/config] file. The [.cargo/config] file is at [%User root%\.cargo\config] as global settings on Windows. However you don’t always want to generate Linux assembly use MSVC ABI, so I recommend that its file create at Rust project root folder.%改行%Toolchain uses rust-lld linker when compile for Musl libraly.%改行%ⓘ As additional information%改行%The cargo.exe reads [.cargo/config] file to compile source code use the rust-lld.exe, and the cargo.exe expect the file is written as TOML file format. The [\] string in TOML format is special string, so [.cargo/config] file should be written path strings as escaped file path use [”’] strings. Addition, cargo.exe needs full path infromation of its linker for compile.%改行%In case of without Microsoft C++ Build Tools, Visual Studio and Visual Studio Code%改行%[target.x86_64-unknown-linux-musl]%改行%linker = ”’%User root%\.rustup\toolchains\stable-x86_64-pc-windows-msvc\lib\rustlib\x86_64-pc-windows-msvc\bin\rust-lld.exe”’%改行%In case of using Microsoft C++ Build Tools, Visual Studio or Visual Studio Code%改行%[target.x86_64-unknown-linux-musl]%改行%linker = “rust-lld”%改行%It’s easy to set path of a linker using MSVC.%改行%・Step 2. Deployment%改行%At first, downloadリンク（https://github.com/TetsuroTakao/QRCodeGenerator）created byリンク（https://blog.processtune.com/?p=5925）and modify it to runnable on Linux. previous article. For it, create [.cargo/config] in the project and set rust-lld to the linker like above, then type [cargo build –release –target=x86_64-unknown-linux-musl] on the [Integrated terminal]. Addition, modify [.funcignore] file to prevent upload files of target folder.%改行%図（https://blog.processtune.com/wp-content/uploads/2022/03/CargoBuildLinuxAssembly.png）%改行%Next, the [%Project root%\target\x86_64-unknown-linux-musl\release\qrcode_generator] file is not uploaded, because Azure Functions Extension uploads a file at project root. Now the [qrcode_generator.exe] is at project root folder, it is execute file for local debug on Windows, so delete it and copy [%Project root%\target\x86_64-unknown-linux-musl\release\qrcode_generator] file to the project root.%改行%Azure Functions extension recognizes only one executable file which [%project name%.exe] file or [%project name%] file. So delete [%project name%.exe] file when upload the executable file to Azure Functions, and copy [%project name%] file to %project root%.%改行%For local debug on Windows, there is [qrcode_generator.exe] value in the [host.json] file to the project root. So modify value of the [defaultExecutablePath] property to [qrcode_generator] at the [description] property of the [customHandler] property for using on Azure Functions (Left figure).%改行%After these preparation of uploading to Azure Functions, Select the [Deploy to Functions App…] icon of the [Azure] extension. (Right figure).%改行%図（https://blog.processtune.com/wp-content/uploads/2022/03/PreperanceToAzureFunctions.png）図（https://blog.processtune.com/wp-content/uploads/2022/03/DeployToFunctionsApp.png）%改行%・Step 3. Test on Azure Functions%改行%The [Command palette] shows after select the [Deploy to Functions App…] icon of the [Azure] extension. And already created a Functions app when create local project using the [Azure] extension, so select the name of the Functions app from list. Then proceed confirmation dialog, if the process ended, show conformation of completing, so select the [View output] button to confirm the [OUTPUT] console (Left figure).%改行%図（https://blog.processtune.com/wp-content/uploads/2022/03/FunctionsAppUpload.png）図（https://blog.processtune.com/wp-content/uploads/2022/03/AzureFunctionsConsole1.png）%改行%Open Azure Functions on the [Azure portal] for the test (Right figure).%改行%Steps of the test (figure below)%改行%The display change when the [Functions] section selected, then select target function name (1. [QRCodeGenerationOnAzureFunctions] on the figure)%改行%Select the [Code + Test] section in displayed target function (2.)%改行%Select the [Test/Run] tab when navigated display to [Code + Test] (3.)%改行%Select the [GET] item of the [HTTP method] list on the [Input] tab area of [Code + Test], because uploaded Function app has only get method of warp (4.)%改行%Add parameter use the [Name] textbox and the [Value] textbox (6.) showed when select the [Add parameter] link (5.)%改行%At last, select the [Run] button for the test.%改行%図（https://blog.processtune.com/wp-content/uploads/2022/03/AzureFunctionsConsoleSteps.png）%改行%A status of the test process on console showed out, then the display is changed to the [Output] tab area of [Code + Test] in automate. So confirm result of the test is that the [HTTP response code] is [200 OK] and the [HTML response content] is outputted.%改行%図（https://blog.processtune.com/wp-content/uploads/2022/03/AzureFunctionsConsoleResult.png）%改行%>>リンク（https://github.com/TetsuroTakao/QRCodeGeneratorOnAzureFunctions）(this article created)%改行%Categories:%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/microsoft-azure/azure-functions/）リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/microsoft-azure/rust/）リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/slide-show/）リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/visual-studio/）リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/visual-studio/visual-studio-code/）%改行%Tags:%改行%No Tag%改行%Post navigation%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/2021/07/01/qr-code-create-with-rust-language-runable-on-azure-functions/）%改行%Post navigation%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/2021/09/01/rust-qr-code-generator-app-of-azure-functions/）%改行%No responses yet%改行%Leave a Replyリンク（https://blog.processtune.com/2021/08/01/how-to-compile-rust-assembly-runnable-on-linux-for-deployment-to-azure-functions/#respond）%改行%Your email address will not be published.Required fields are marked*%改行%Comment*%改行%Name*%改行%Email*%改行%Website%改行%Save my name, email, and website in this browser for the next time I comment.%改行%© 2025 Processtune Blog. Title: Logging file save to Microsoft Azure part 2 – Processtune Blog
Categories: Azure Active Directory,Microservice,Microsoft Azure,Visual Studio,未分類
BodyText: %改行%リンク（#content）%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/）%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/microsoft-azure/）%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/microsoft-azure/azure-active-directory/）%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/visual-studio/）%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/microservice/）%改行%リンク（#）%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/）%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/microsoft-azure/）%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/microsoft-azure/azure-active-directory/）%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/visual-studio/）%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/microservice/）%改行%Logging file save to Microsoft Azure part 2%改行%byリンク（https://blog.processtune.com/author/tetsuro-takao/）%改行%onリンク（https://blog.processtune.com/2019/03/28/）%改行%This article is follow ofリンク（https://blog.processtune.com/?p=4590）. This article explains how to upload file to Microsoft Azure. The file is application log file. So this article explains simple telemetry feature also.%改行%Microsoft Azure Blob Storage provides several type of data storage below.%改行%・Several type of data storage%改行%Even if you used to use log4net or NLog, this article ‘DOES NOT’ explains these formal logging tool, but implements super easy logging instead of it. Because I want to show you that MVC(in this case, MVVM) implementation model is easy to choice for your solution product. Then, it affects very important point in development scenes later for CD/CI or servicing your software as API.%改行%図（https://blog.processtune.com/wp-content/uploads/2019/03/log4.png）%改行%図（https://blog.processtune.com/wp-content/uploads/2019/03/NLog.png）%改行%At first, please download the solution file from GitHub to your local pc.%改行%リンク（https://github.com/TetsuroTakao/WPFLockdownSample2）%改行%And This is the sample code which applied contents of this article.%改行%リンク（https://github.com/TetsuroTakao/WPFLockdownSample3）%改行%・Create a model of log information%改行%Create solution folder named ‘Models’ at root of the solution. And create model class [Log].%改行%図（https://blog.processtune.com/wp-content/uploads/2019/03/solutionExplorer03.png）%改行%図（https://blog.processtune.com/wp-content/uploads/2019/03/solutionExplorer04.png）%改行%The model [Log] class has simple properties [Message],[OccurredTime],[OperatorName],[LogType] and create Type name [LogType] which has types [Default],[Information],[Error],[Operation]%改行%namespace WPFLockdownSample.Models { public class Log { public string Message { get; set; } public DateTime OccurredTime { get; set; } public string OperatorName { get; set; } public LogType LogType { get; set; } } public enum LogType { Default = 0, Information = 1, Error = 2, Operation = 4 } }%改行%As Next step, modify App_Startup method of App.xaml.cs to be able to logging. One point which you have to aware in this step, if some data has DateTime type data, you have to consider both when the data is used by local time and is used at Microsoft Azure programmatically. So the data is held as DateTime type better.%改行%void App_Startup() { void App_Startup(object sender, StartupEventArgs e) { Listlogs = new List(); Log log = new Log() { Message = "checkking app files ...", OccurredTime = DateTime.Now, OperatorName = typeof(App).Name, LogType = LogType.Information }; logs.Add(log); //Check existing of log file. //if (logging.ReadLogs().Count == 0) //{ // [Local account creation] explains how to create these four accounts. // And how to navigate use logon script // CoreApplication.Exit(); //} //else if (logFile.Exists) check current user //{ // [Desktop UI control] explains how to check current user // if (UserName == "maintenanceOperator") navigate to MaintenanceWindow // if (UserName == "appOperator") quiet this app. This account has specific automatic // run application when this account sign in. // if (UserName == "appUser") quiet this app. This account has specific automatic // run application when this account sign in. //} } }%改行%The type [Log] is in the Namespace [WPFLockdownSample.Models], so add ‘using WPFLockdownSample.Models;’ statement at top of App.xml.cs. ‘List<Log> logs’ holds several logs information while the logs will be written to log file( and the contents of the file will send to Microsoft Azure later).%改行%The [OperatorName] property is used for recording operations of application with [LogType] property. The [Operation]’s value in [LogType] type. In case of this sample code, the message is application information, so use [LogType.Information] and ‘typeof(App).Name’ for the value. if application message occurs in MainPage, use [LogType.Information] and ‘typeof(MainPage).Name’ value. So it will modified to ‘this.GetType().Name’ later, but it’s keep it so far.%改行%・Write log information to log file%改行%To create function of log writing, create solution folder named [Features] and create [DataAccessLayer] class, The class use Json, so please install Json serializer using NuGet. And then create method below.%改行%図（https://blog.processtune.com/wp-content/uploads/2019/03/jsonserialiser.png）%改行%図（https://blog.processtune.com/wp-content/uploads/2019/03/logging.png）%改行%public class DataAccessLayer { public FileInfo LogFile { get; private set; } const string appFilesPath = @"c:\WFPLockdownSample"; DirectoryInfo logFolder = new DirectoryInfo(appFilesPath); public DataAccessLayer() { LogFile = new FileInfo ( appFilesPath + @"\" + DateTime.Now.ToString("yyyyMMdd") + "logs.log" ); } public void AppendWriteLogs(List<Log> logs, bool reverse = true) { if(reverse) logs.Reverse(); string jsonstring = string.Empty; logs.AddRange(ReadLogs()); using (StreamWriter file = File.CreateText(LogFile.FullName)) { JsonSerializer serializer = new JsonSerializer(); serializer.Serialize(file, logs); } } public List<Log> ReadLogs() { List<Log> logs = new List<Log>(); string jsonstring = string.Empty; if (LogFile.Exists) jsonstring = File.ReadAllText(LogFile.FullName); if (!string.IsNullOrEmpty(jsonstring)) { logs = JsonConvert.DeserializeObject<List<Log>>(jsonstring); } return logs; } }%改行%This logging feature of [DataAccessLayer] class access to local folder, and in the future, other data sources or data service API, so it should be created as ‘partial’ class, it will be modified as partial class later, but it is normal class so far.%改行%This class has public property [LogFile], it set instance at constructor. And provides two features, one is [AppendWriteLogs] method and another is [ReadLogs] method.%改行%[AppendWriteLogs] method is a feature which outputs logs information to log file, [logs] argument is a container of log information which added when it occurred, so log information listed by time series order in [logs] argument, therefore most newest log information should be written at top most of log file. So order of logs information which accepted are reversed as default execution.%改行%Then logs of the log file append to [logs] argument and write back to the log file.%改行%On other hand, [ReadLogs] method provides a feature of read contents of the log file. If log file is not exists or log file has not any contents, it returns blank container of logs information. This state is first time execution of this application. So if log information count of this log container is zero, this application try to create four accounts for operation scenarios.%改行%・Use log functions%改行%To use write log function [AppendWriteLogs] method of the [DataAccessLayer] class, call the [DataAccessLayer] class at top of [App_Startup] method, then use [ReadLogs] method and count logs.%改行%And create log information which scenario was choosen, then write logs information use [AppendWriteLogs] method at end of all step.%改行%public partial class App : Application { DataAccessLayer dataAccessLayer = new DataAccessLayer(); void App_Startup(object sender, StartupEventArgs e) { Listlogs = new List(); Log log = new Log() { Message = "checkking app files ...", OccurredTime = DateTime.Now, OperatorName = typeof(App).Name, LogType = LogType.Information }; logs.Add(log); if (dataAccessLayer.ReadLogs().Count == 0) { log = new Log() { Message = "this exesution is first time.", OccurredTime = DateTime.Now, OperatorName = typeof(App).Name, LogType = LogType.Information }; logs.Add(log); //[Local account creation] explains how to create these four accounts. //And how to navigate use logon script //CoreApplication.Exit(); } else { log = new Log() { Message = "this execution has run on this device more than twice.", OccurredTime = DateTime.Now, OperatorName = typeof(App).Name, LogType = LogType.Information }; logs.Add(log); //[Desktop UI control] explains how to check current user //if (UserName == "maintenanceOperator") navigate to MaintenanceWindow //if (UserName == "appOperator") quiet this app. This account has specific automatic //run application when this account sign in. //if (UserName == "appUser") quiet this app. This account has specific automatic //run application when this account sign in. } log = new Log() { Message = "checkking app files is complete.", OccurredTime = DateTime.Now, OperatorName = typeof(App).Name, LogType = LogType.Information }; logs.Add(log); dataAccessLayer.AppendWriteLogs(logs); } }%改行%Go next stepリンク（https://blog.processtune.com/?p=4814）%改行%orリンク（https://blog.processtune.com/?p=4551）%改行%,リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/?p=4781）%改行%,リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/?p=4785）%改行%Categories:%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/%e6%9c%aa%e5%88%86%e9%a1%9e/）%改行%Tags:%改行%No Tag%改行%Post navigation%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/2018/10/27/desktop-ui-controlling/）%改行%Post navigation%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/2019/03/29/launch-correct-app-by-specific-account/）%改行%No responses yet%改行%Leave a Replyリンク（https://blog.processtune.com/2019/03/28/logging-file-save-to-microsoft-azure-part-2/#respond）%改行%Your email address will not be published.Required fields are marked*%改行%Comment*%改行%Name*%改行%Email*%改行%Website%改行%Save my name, email, and website in this browser for the next time I comment.%改行%© 2025 Processtune Blog. Title: Mind map creation with markdown notation on visual studio code – Processtune Blog
Categories: Azure Active Directory,Markdown,Microservice,Microsoft Azure,SlideShow,Uncategorized,Visual Studio
BodyText:
%改行%リンク（#content）%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/）%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/microsoft-azure/）%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/microsoft-azure/azure-active-directory/）%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/visual-studio/）%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/microservice/）%改行%リンク（#）%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/）%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/microsoft-azure/）%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/microsoft-azure/azure-active-directory/）%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/visual-studio/）%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/microservice/）%改行%Mind map creation with markdown notation on visual studio code%改行%図（https://blog.processtune.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/04/PreviewMindmapBothSides.png）%改行%byリンク（https://blog.processtune.com/author/takao/）%改行%onリンク（https://blog.processtune.com/2024/04/28/）%改行%Though there are many extensions to showリンク（https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Mind_map）on Visual Studio Code, it is better that the feature can use as possible as with common notation, and as simple installation for using it.リンク（https://plantuml.com/）can define various visuals of UML and mindmap from plane markdown, JSON data or YAML data and so on, these visual includes a legend, a title or a footer.%改行%And it’s my opinion that theリンク（https://marketplace.visualstudio.com/items?itemName=shd101wyy.markdown-preview-enhanced）extension of Visual Studio Code is one of good extension, because it has features of easy installing and operation to represent various diagrams including mindmap.%改行%Normally, to run preview of PlantUML needs the Java runtime although, the Markdown Preview Enhanced can define server URL for rendering. So it is not need complicate installing or settings that if it would have negative impact to existing development environment.%改行%This article describes how to use the Markdown Preview Enhanced extension and how to create several views using PlantUML.%改行%• Install Markdown Preview Enhanced and setting PlantUML rendering server URL%改行%Launch Visual Studio Code and select [Extension] icon from [Activate bar], then type ‘markdown’ in [Search box] and select Markdown Preview Enhanced for installing. After installing the extension, select [File] menu and drilled down to [Settings] sub menu using right arrow.%改行%図（https://blog.processtune.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/10/MarkdownPreviewEnhancedExtension.png）図（https://blog.processtune.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/10/VSCodeSettings.png）%改行%Type ‘markdownenhanced’ at search box of [Settings] window, and put ‘https://www.plantuml.com/plantuml’ into [Markdown-preview-enhanced: Plantuml Server].%改行%At following section, create new file and save as markdown file on Visual Studio Code, and define PlantUML then modify it actually.%改行%図（https://blog.processtune.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/10/savemdfile.png）%改行%• Write markdown first then it would be defined to PlantUML I recommend.%改行%Mindmap is useful for ordering items of meeting such as individual speech of minutes or actions until next meeting and so on. In these days, recording or creating transcription using AI is very helpful although, writing mindmap has helping organize thought of my own thinking for me. Same as memo that individual member wrote, generated summary of the meeting is only one of the memos wrote by one of attendees. Thus, I always write the memo as the Markdown file.%改行%# Agenda: AI Education contents for e-learning program ## 1) Assembling AI terms is for readers to be able to refer it always ### Models #### 1. Closed models *Please fulfil this level contents at **next meeting**.* #### 2. Open-source models #### 3. Open weight models #### 4. Mixture of Experts #### 5. Optimized model for hardware ### Training and Size #### 1. Parameters, Languages, Input window size and Specs of LLMs, SLMs #### 2. Quantization #### 3. Distillation(knowledge or Concept) #### 4. Low Rank Adaptation, RAGBench ### Runtime, Pipeline(Embeddings: tokenize + vectorize) #### 1. Ollama, LlamaIndex, Redis Vector Store, RedisVL, LangChain, Milvus, Postgres Vector Store, TensorFlow, Transformers #### 2. Llama Hub, Hugging Face Hub, Model Zoo, PyTorch Hub #### 3. OpenAI, AI managed services(AWS, Azure, Google Cloud, IBM, Oracle Cloud) #### 4. GGML, GGUF, GPTQ, ONNX #### 5. Byte pair encoding, Dimentions ### AI Agent, AI Orchestration, Multi-modal #### 1. RAG (Dictionary of bilingual: prefix and suffix, misspell and typo, task-specific word) ### Micro-services, Observability, Sidecar pattern and Service Mesh #### 1. Combine several AI services #### 2. Distributed tracing #### 3. Proxy, Service discovery, Logging, Distributed hash table, Connectors, Queue #### 4. Containers --- ## 2) Solutions structure sample of AI ### Deploy with AI agent services #### 1. AWS #### 2. Azure #### 3. Google cloud ### Development on-premise #### 0. BodyText: %改行%リンク（#content）%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/）%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/microsoft-azure/）%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/microsoft-azure/azure-active-directory/）%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/visual-studio/）%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/microservice/）%改行%リンク（#）%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/）%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/microsoft-azure/）%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/microsoft-azure/azure-active-directory/）%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/visual-studio/）%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/microservice/）%改行%WPF app that display Excel file.%改行%byリンク（https://blog.processtune.com/author/tetsuro-takao/）%改行%onリンク（https://blog.processtune.com/2013/08/26/）%改行%I tryed to display the Microsoft Excel file in a WPF app. but, I was not able to find it out by web about to do that. So I tryed next step which the Excel file converted to XPS file display in the DocumemtViewer of a WPF app.%改行%That was run well as I expected, so I report a detail as below.%改行%1. How to convert Excel file to XPS file (use PIA).%改行%2. How to display XPS file in WPF app (use a feature in Windows OS).%改行%This is the presentation file (Japanese ver. Microsoft PowerPoint file)リンク（http://t.co/KwMbkU96sI）%改行%This is the Visual Studio 2012 sample solution fileリンク（http://sdrv.ms/16fTtHr）%改行%1. How to convert Excel file to XPS file.%改行%Primary Interop Assemblies(PIA) can controls Excel file finely, but PIA depends on version of the Office. So we must decide on target version or use another architecture as VSTO.%改行%This time, use the Microsoft Office 2013 PIA, because of want to create Windows 8 desktop app. the Microsoft Office 2013 PIA is download from Office Developer Tools for Visual Studio 2012 page (リンク（http://www.microsoft.com/visualstudio/eng/office-dev-tools-for-visual-studio）).%改行%figure. Office Developer Tools for Visual Studio 2012.%改行%図（http://processtuneblog.azurewebsites.net/wp-content/uploads/2013/04/OfficeDeveloperToolsForVisualStudio2012.png）%改行%If you want to target the Microsoft Office 2010, use the 2013 PIA or download from below ( Office Developer Tools for Visual Studio 2012 includes dlls for 2010 and 2013 both ).%改行%リンク（http://www.microsoft.com/en-us/download/details.aspx?id=3508）%改行%figure. Microsoft Office 2010: Primary Interop Assemblies Redistributable.%改行%図（http://processtuneblog.azurewebsites.net/wp-content/uploads/2013/04/042613_0337_WPFappthatd1.png）%改行%図（http://processtuneblog.azurewebsites.net/wp-content/uploads/2013/04/VSToolsPIA.png）%改行%After download and installing that, create new WPF app solution from the Visual Studio file menu.%改行%figure. VS 2012 image.%改行%図（http://processtuneblog.azurewebsites.net/wp-content/uploads/2013/04/042613_0337_WPFappthatd2.png）図（http://processtuneblog.azurewebsites.net/wp-content/uploads/2013/04/042613_0337_WPFappthatd3.png）%改行%In order to enable drag and drop the Excel file to this app, open the project property window from right click at project name on the Solution Explorer. And put sample Excel file path of local PC into the Debug [Command line arguments] box.%改行%figure. the Command line arguments box.%改行%図（http://processtuneblog.azurewebsites.net/wp-content/uploads/2013/04/AddComanndlineArgs.png）%改行%Create start up event handler from the App.xaml. Defines the event to call the handler at the tag attribute place, it’s complementary when type “Startup = “.%改行%figure. the Startup attribute in the Application tag%改行%図（http://processtuneblog.azurewebsites.net/wp-content/uploads/2013/04/AddNewHandler.png）%改行%To catch an argument at the Startup event handler and open the sample Excel file. First, add reference the Excel name space from the [Assemblies] – [Extentions] of the Reference Manager how to show right click on the References folder of the Solution Explorer.%改行%figue. Add Microsoft.Office.Interop.Excel.dll reference.%改行%図（http://processtuneblog.azurewebsites.net/wp-content/uploads/2013/04/AddReferencePIAFor15Dll.png）%改行%Second, the Excel file open by Excel application object of PIA as below.%改行%varexcelApp =newMicrosoft.Office.Interop.Excel.Application()*%改行%excelApp.DisplayAlerts =false;%改行%excelApp.Visible =false;%改行%Microsoft.Office.Interop.Excel.WorkbookexcelWorkbook = excelApp.Workbooks.Open(path);%改行%*Perfect name space is in order to distinguish Microsoft.Office.Interop.Excel.Application and System.Windows.Application.%改行%*In the future, this app controls a contents of the Excel file by Open XML SDK, so perfect name space is in order to distinguish Microsoft.Office.Interop.Excel.Workbook and DocumentFormat.OpenXml.Spreadsheet.Workbook.%改行%“path” create from the argument of Application_Startup_1 event handler of App class defined in App.xaml.cs.%改行%public static stringExcelPath {get;set; }%改行%private voidApplication_Startup_1(objectsender,StartupEventArgse)%改行%{%改行%foreach(stringargine.Args)%改行%{%改行%ExcelPath = arg;%改行%}%改行%}%改行%In MainWindow.xaml.cs, Static variable of App class “ExcelPath” input to variable “path” of class MainWindow. And add the Window Loaded event handler at MainWindow class constructor%改行%private stringpath =App.ExcelPath;// add this line%改行%publicMainWindow()%改行%{%改行%InitializeComponent();%改行%Loaded += MainWindow_Loaded;// add this line%改行%}%改行%In MainWindow_Loaded event handler of MainWindow.xaml.cs, follow the code above, add code that the XPS file export from the Excel file..%改行%voidMainWindow_Loaded(objectsender,RoutedEventArgse)%改行%{%改行%varexcelApp =newMicrosoft.Office.Interop.Excel.Application();%改行%excelApp.DisplayAlerts =false;%改行%excelApp.Visible =false;%改行%Microsoft.Office.Interop.Excel.WorkbookexcelWorkbook = excelApp.Workbooks.Open(path);%改行%// add below%改行%ExportXPS(excelWorkbook);// XPS Exporter is explained at next description%改行%excelWorkbook.Close(false,null,null);%改行%excelApp.Quit();%改行%// release com object%改行%Marshal.ReleaseComObject(excelApp);%改行%excelApp =null;%改行%}%改行%Save the XPS file from the Excel file.%改行%stringxpsFileName =“”;%改行%voidExportXPS(Microsoft.Office.Interop.Excel.WorkbookexcelWorkbook)%改行%{%改行%xpsFileName = (newDirectoryInfo(path)).FullName;%改行%xpsFileName = xpsFileName.Replace(newFileInfo(path).Extension,“”) +“.xps”;%改行%excelWorkbook.ExportAsFixedFormat(XlFixedFormatType.xlTypeXPS,%改行%Filename: xpsFileName,%改行%OpenAfterPublish:false);%改行%}%改行%2. How to display XPS file in WPF app.%改行%To read XPS file for display to the DocumentViewer, placement the DocumentViewer component from the Tool box to MainWindow.xaml. If set the Window height size to 768 and width size to 1024, the DocumentViewer is stretched to same size by default.%改行%Drag the DocumentViewer component from the All WPF Controls of Toolbox and drop into the Grid area and set x:Name property to “DocumentViewer”.%改行%<Windowx:Class=”ExcelViewer.MainWindow”%改行%xmlns=”http://schemas.microsoft.com/winfx/2006/xaml/presentation”%改行%xmlns:x=”http://schemas.microsoft.com/winfx/2006/xaml”%改行%Title=”MainWindow”Height=”768″Width=”1024″>%改行%<Grid>%改行%<DocumentViewerx:Name=”DocumentWindow” />%改行%</Grid>%改行%</Window>%改行%figure. Stretch every direction to parent element is default property of the DocumentViewer.%改行%図（http://processtuneblog.azurewebsites.net/wp-content/uploads/2013/04/Documentviewer.png）%改行%Read the XPS file that create from the Excel file and display into the DocumentViewer control “DocumentWindow” by feature of the Windows OS. Add reference the ReachFremework, several using and create handler “DisplayXPSFile” for that the GetFixedDocumentSequence method of the XpsDocument object provide the FixedDocumentSequence to the DocumentViewer.%改行%figure. the ReachFramework for read XPS file.%改行%図（http://processtuneblog.azurewebsites.net/wp-content/uploads/2013/04/reachframework.png）%改行%usingMicrosoft.Office.Interop.Excel;%改行%usingSystem.IO;%改行%usingSystem.Runtime.InteropServices;%改行%usingSystem.Windows;%改行%usingSystem.Windows.Documents;%改行%usingSystem.Windows.Xps.Packaging;%改行%usingSystem.IO.Packaging;%改行%namespaceExcelViewer%改行%{%改行%public partial classMainWindow: System.Windows.Window%改行%{%改行%…%改行%voidMainWindow_Loaded(objectsender,RoutedEventArgse)%改行%{%改行%…%改行%excelApp =null;%改行%DisplayXPSFile();%改行%}%改行%…%改行%void DisplayXPSFile()%改行%{%改行%XpsDocumentxpsPackage =newXpsDocument(xpsFileName,FileAccess.Read,CompressionOption.NotCompressed);%改行%FixedDocumentSequencefixedDocumentSequence = xpsPackage.GetFixedDocumentSequence();%改行%DocumentWindow.Document = fixedDocumentSequence;%改行%}%改行%}%改行%}%改行%figure. Simple Excel file viewer created by WPF app.%改行%図（http://processtuneblog.azurewebsites.net/wp-content/uploads/2013/04/LaunchExcelViewer.png）%改行%Categories:%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/office-app/office-business-app/）リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/slide-show/）リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/winui/universal-apps/windows-store-app/windows-8-app/）%改行%Tags:%改行%No Tag%改行%Post navigation%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/2013/07/08/resource-of-store-app-for-globalization/）%改行%Post navigation%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/2014/01/20/view-the-processing-status-of-the-windows-runtime-component-to-windows-store-app/）%改行%2 Responses%改行%図（https://secure.gravatar.com/avatar/d759d0d17840f694fb2af5adcda1f013f195338f7da434330bcc14e61ea9d967?s=30&d=mm&r=g）リンク（http://www.alisajunior.com/）says:%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/2013/08/26/wpf-app-that-display-excel-file-2/#comment-6927）%改行%I simply want to say I’m beginner to blogs and seriously liked you’re blog site. Probably I’m likely to bookmark your blog post . You really come with really good articles. Regards for sharing your blog site.%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/2013/08/26/wpf-app-that-display-excel-file-2/?replytocom=6927#respond）%改行%Leave a Replyリンク（https://blog.processtune.com/2013/08/26/wpf-app-that-display-excel-file-2/#respond）%改行%Your email address will not be published.Required fields are marked*%改行%Comment*%改行%Name*%改行%Email*%改行%Website%改行%Save my name, email, and website in this browser for the next time I comment.%改行%© 2025 Processtune Blog. Add reference the ReachFremework, several using and create handler “DisplayXPSFile” for that the GetFixedDocumentSequence method of the XpsDocument object provide the FixedDocumentSequence to the DocumentViewer.%改行%figure. the ReachFramework for read XPS file.%改行%図（http://processtuneblog.azurewebsites.net/wp-content/uploads/2013/04/reachframework.png）%改行%usingMicrosoft.Office.Interop.Excel;%改行%usingSystem.IO;%改行%usingSystem.Runtime.InteropServices;%改行%usingSystem.Windows;%改行%usingSystem.Windows.Documents;%改行%usingSystem.Windows.Xps.Packaging;%改行%usingSystem.IO.Packaging;%改行%namespaceExcelViewer%改行%{%改行%public partial classMainWindow: System.Windows.Window%改行%{%改行%…%改行%voidMainWindow_Loaded(objectsender,RoutedEventArgse)%改行%{%改行%…%改行%excelApp =null;%改行%DisplayXPSFile();%改行%}%改行%…%改行%void DisplayXPSFile()%改行%{%改行%XpsDocumentxpsPackage =newXpsDocument(xpsFileName,FileAccess.Read,CompressionOption.NotCompressed);%改行%FixedDocumentSequencefixedDocumentSequence = xpsPackage.GetFixedDocumentSequence();%改行%DocumentWindow.Document = fixedDocumentSequence;%改行%}%改行%}%改行%}%改行%figure. Title: Steps of Introducing AI to companies – Processtune Blog
Categories: AI,Azure Active Directory,Microservice,Microsoft Azure,Visual Studio
BodyText: %改行%リンク（#content）%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/）%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/microsoft-azure/）%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/microsoft-azure/azure-active-directory/）%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/visual-studio/）%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/microservice/）%改行%リンク（#）%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/）%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/microsoft-azure/）%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/microsoft-azure/azure-active-directory/）%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/visual-studio/）%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/microservice/）%改行%Steps of Introducing AI to companies%改行%図（https://blog.processtune.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/03/blogAIheader.png）%改行%byリンク（https://blog.processtune.com/author/takao/）%改行%onリンク（https://blog.processtune.com/2024/03/26/）%改行%The previous article [リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/2024/02/07/introducing-artificial-intelligence-ai-in-companies/）] describes “AI has objectives depending on its scale”, “AI in companies is effective through orchestration using small language models” and “Information sources confidentiality also needs to be protected in AI”.%改行%Additionally, it describes “In the case of Microsoft 365 data is information sources, the confidentiality is best on Microsoft 365 rather than moving to other storages” and “In the case of the information sources being non-Microsoft 365 data, Information sources should be stored in individual storages that match access permissions, and divided schemes of several AI on orchestration foundation whenリンク（https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Federated_identity）is not complete”.%改行%• In the case of the information sources being non-Microsoft 365 data and In the case of the identity federation is complete.%改行%If the identity federation is aggregated to the Entra ID account, it is good that information sources are stored as Azure resources. Even if information sources are non-Microsoft 365 data, it is super easy to control access to information sources of AI.%改行%Information sources can be prepared or real time.リンク（https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/ai-services/openai/use-your-data-quickstart?wt.mc_id=DT-MVP-4029060）ofリンク（https://azure.microsoft.com/en-us/products/ai-studio?wt.mc_id=DT-MVP-4029060）supports various file types. Or using connector supports Azure AI Search, Azure Cosmos DB for MongoDB vCore, and URL/web address.%改行%If information sources could not be stored as Azure resources, Entra ID that aggregated as the identity federation should control access to AI.%改行%Azure provides various connection services such as provisioning or authorization delegation calls (if the service provider supports it) as the identity federation features.%改行%Thus in the case of AI that uses information sources outside of Azure, it is good that information sources are designed as single access permission.%改行%• Design steps for Introducing AI to companies%改行%When introducing AI to the company, consideration of The state of the user account of the organization and the purpose of AI in the company is the first step of design.%改行%It’s an important decision point whether the purpose of using AI in the company is to leverage Microsoft 365 data or not. Whether the user account of the organization has already completed the identity federation or not is also important because Microsoft 365 data is protected by the Microsoft 365 identity foundation.リンク（https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/entra/identity/hybrid/connect/four-steps?wt.mc_id=DT-MVP-4029060）describes that the identity foundation of Microsoft’s tremendous robustness and flexibility can address enterprise compliance and governance.%改行%The next step is considering the need to customize for using the company’s data. Then programming or No/Low code development is necessary or not. These steps lead to how to implement AI in the company.%改行%図（https://blog.processtune.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/02/DesignSteps.png）%改行%Most easiest way to introduce AI to the company is Microsoft 365 data leverage on the company.リンク（https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/microsoft-365/syntex/metadata-search?wt.mc_id=DT-MVP-4029060）of files of SharePoint, it classifies files as various aspects and then shows them on the SharePoint site. Furthermore,リンク（https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/microsoft-365/syntex/automate-document-generation?wt.mc_id=DT-MVP-4029060）. On creating UI,リンク（https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/microsoft-copilot-studio/advanced-connectors?wt.mc_id=DT-MVP-4029060）for using Microsoft 365 data and other data, creating cloud flow. These services will be described in my other articles.%改行%It is complex in the case of the information sources being non-Microsoft 365 data.%改行%One way to divide sources is an aspect of access permissions, it said above, and another way is to divide by use case that does not matter accessed identities.%改行%It is important how data is divided by permission when information sources of AI such as access accounting data or human resource data although, it is not important to divide data with identity to access IoT data unmanaged by id. These data might be divided into use cases for efficiency or agility.%改行%Furthermore, it is important to consider whether the identity federation is complete or not. Ifリンク（https://techcommunity.microsoft.com/t5/microsoft-mechanics-blog/manage-your-multi-cloud-identity-infrastructure-with-microsoft/ba-p/3709677?wt.mc_id=DT-MVP-4029060）and stores the whole of the data as Azure resources, it needs only implement the orchestration of AI.%改行%So this article describes how to implement AI using Microsoft 365 data with additional data. My other articles describe content other than AI orchestration.%改行%• Story of the sample of this article%改行%As a sample of AI using Microsoft 365 data with additional data, design levels of accessing information sources of AI that is like the following figure.%改行%図（https://blog.processtune.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/02/LevelsOfAccessingInformationSourcesOfAI.png）%改行%The additional data in the figure above is used in AI. For example, the travel expense data is shared only with the accounting division using SharePoint or Teams. Though the additional data is not shared with the company’s colleagues explicitly, It is better that colleagues who are not engaged accounting division can search related to the travel expense data such as heuristic knowledge that is assembled by the employee who applied travel expenses. This article creates the AI that orchestrates with Microsoft 365 using such data.%改行%• Preparing a Small Language Model for the company’s data%改行%There are several types of language models, types are such as foundation models, fine-tuned models, and quantization models. Now, the Trainer model such asリンク（https://www.microsoft.com/en-us/research/project/orca/）exists and creates small language models, training, and creating datasets, for foundation models.%改行%It is better to use fine-tuned models than foundation models because they are alreadyリンク（https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/ai-services/openai/how-to/fine-tuning?tabs=turbo%2Cpython&pivots=programming-language-studio&wt.mc_id=DT-MVP-4029060）if the usage of the additional data of the company on AI matches the task of fine-tuning.リンク（https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/microsoft-365-copilot/microsoft-365-copilot-overview?wt.mc_id=DT-MVP-4029060）,リンク（https://www.bloomberg.com/company/press/bloomberggpt-50-billion-parameter-llm-tuned-finance/）, orリンク（https://developer.amazon.com/en-US/alexa/alexa-skills-kit/asr）are representative.%改行%Additionally, ifリンク（https://arxiv.org/pdf/2210.17323.pdf）for fine-tuned models exists, it is better to use quantization models than fine-tuned models because quantization models are model compression using the one-shot weight quantization method, it can be used without GPU. Thus, the search for a model that aligns with the task atリンク（https://huggingface.co/）,リンク（https://www.tensorflow.org/hub?hl=en）, orリンク（https://pytorch.org/serve/model_zoo.html）at first. This article uses Hugging Face because using a model launcher distributed on Hugging Face. Model launcher is easy to search by typing “Ollama” at Hugging Face, Ollama is similar to LlamaIndex, LangChain, Semantic Kernel, Rust LLM, and Roboflow, they support many models.%改行%Model tools%改行%Support models%改行%Ollama%改行%Support models of more thanリンク（https://ollama.com/library）.%改行%LlamaIndex%改行%Several paid and open-sourceリンク（https://docs.llamaindex.ai/en/stable/module_guides/models/llms.html）are supported.%改行%LangChain%改行%Does not serve its own LLMs, but rather provides a standard interface for interacting with many differentリンク（https://python.langchain.com/docs/modules/model_io/llms/quick_start）.%改行%Semantic Kernel%改行%SDK that integrates Large Language Models (リンク（https://github.com/microsoft/semantic-kernel）) like OpenAI, Azure OpenAI, and Hugging Face.%改行%Rust LLM%改行%Support several models asリンク（https://github.com/rustformers/llm/blob/main/doc/known-good-models.md）.%改行%Roboflow%改行%Severalリンク（https://roboflow.com/models）are supported.%改行%Ollama also has an eco-system as Ollama’s official image at the docker hub or can be installed on Linux using WSL2 as well. Models that are not Quantarizationed can be compressed using a GGUF file.%改行%There are libraries to create a pipeline for C#, so it’s easy to implement local AI as figure below using Ollama.%改行%Categories:%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/ai/）リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/microsoft-azure/）%改行%Tags:%改行%No Tag%改行%Post navigation%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/2024/02/07/introducing-artificial-intelligence-ai-in-companies/）%改行%Post navigation%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/2024/04/28/mind-map-creation-with-markdown-notation-on-visual-studio-code/）%改行%No responses yet%改行%Leave a Replyリンク（https://blog.processtune.com/2024/03/26/steps-of-introducing-ai-to-companies/#respond）%改行%Your email address will not be published.Required fields are marked*%改行%Comment*%改行%Name*%改行%Email*%改行%Website%改行%Save my name, email, and website in this browser for the next time I comment.%改行%© 2025 Processtune Blog.
