Title: ASP.NET Core gRPC & gRPC communication tools – Processtune Blog
Categories: Azure Active Directory,gRPC,Microservice,Microsoft Azure,Visual Studio,未分類
BodyText: %改行%リンク（#content）%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/）%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/microsoft-azure/）%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/microsoft-azure/azure-active-directory/）%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/visual-studio/）%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/microservice/）%改行%リンク（#）%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/）%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/microsoft-azure/）%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/microsoft-azure/azure-active-directory/）%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/visual-studio/）%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/microservice/）%改行%ASP.NET Core gRPC & gRPC communication tools%改行%図（https://blog.processtune.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/03/SeriesTitle.png）%改行%byリンク（https://blog.processtune.com/author/tetsuro-takao/）%改行%onリンク（https://blog.processtune.com/2022/05/30/）%改行%Microsoft supports several development environments of the gRPC communication implementation, and provides many documents. I want to introduceリンク（https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/aspnet/core/grpc/test-tools?view=aspnetcore-6.0&WT.mc_id=AZ-MVP-4029060）and describe mechanism of HTTP/3 protocol, relation between the gRPC and HTTP/3 and so on. Design of gRPC communication was described at previous article ‘リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/?p=6651）‘, please refer to it.%改行%This article describes two-thirds steps of implementation of the gRPC service, at first, create the gRPC service with localhost, then query simple request use theリンク（https://github.com/fullstorydev/grpcurl/blob/master/README.md）tool. The last step is in the next article [リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/?p=6783）] that describes how to deploy the service to Azure Web App Service.%改行%And mechanism of HTTP/3 protocol and relation between the gRPC and HTTP/3 is described in the article [リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/?p=6804）].%改行%>>リンク（https://github.com/TetsuroTakao/gRPC2）%改行%• Implementation of the gRPC service.%改行%This article refers to Microsoft official document ‘リンク（https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/aspnet/core/tutorials/grpc/grpc-start?view=aspnetcore-6.0&tabs=visual-studio?WT.mc_id=DT-MVP-4029060）‘, so if lost a step of implementation of the gRPC service, please refer to it too. This article describe steps of the gRPC service with ASP.NET Core, so the service implementation with one of architectures below.%改行%Kestrel%改行%Cross-platform web server%改行%TestServer%改行%In-memory web server for unit test mainly%改行%Internet Information Service%改行%Standard web server of Windows, the constraints are .NET 5 and Windows 11 Build 22000 or Windows Server 2022 Build 20348 or later.%改行%HTTP.sys%改行%The Windows web service for ASP.NET Core includes several extending features that differ from Kestrel; constraints are same as IIS above.%改行%In the step 2, call it from the gRPCurl tool or the Postman, then after confirm runnable of the service, as last step, deploy to any of Azure Services below.%改行%Azure Kubernetes Service (AKS)%改行%Azure Container Apps%改行%Azure App Service (gRPC-Web)%改行%The gRPC service have to includes the gRPC reflection service for providing description of the gRPC service (works like as swagger). The gRPC client communicates with the gRPC reflection service for solving objects of the gRPC service before calls method of the service.%改行%If it needs only communication test, a gRPC service that does not implement the gRPC reflection service can also use the gRPC connection with loading the proto source file to the gRPCurl. Also, the Postman can use as gRPC client as same as the gRPCurl (useable the proto source file).%改行%It is easy that the gRPC reflection service implementation, Open Visual Studio Code, use the “dotnet new” command at target folder and use the “dotnet add package” command for implementation of service on the generic host of ASP.NET Core web app.%改行%図（https://blog.processtune.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/02/ImplementationProcess.png）%改行%1. Press the Ctrl key + @ key for open the terminal pain of Visual Studio Code. Then put the “dotnet new list” command to show all templates of dotnet sdk on development environment. The figure above shows .NET SDK 7.0 environment. There is the ASP.NET Core gRPC Service in the list.%改行%2. So, move to the target folder in the terminal pain, and put the “dotnet new grpc” command to generate project files at target folder.%改行%3. Install reflection server package use nuget dotnet command “dotnet add package Grpc.AspNetCore.Server.Reflection –version 2.51.0”.%改行%4. Next, open the Program.cs file from the EXPLORER pain, and put the code “builder.Services.AddGrpcReflection();” after the “var builder = WebApplication.CreateBuilder(args);” statement.%改行%5. After the “var app = builder.Build();” statement, add the “IWebHostEnvironment env = app.Environment;” code and the “if” section like code below.%改行%using gRPC2.Services; var builder = WebApplication.CreateBuilder(args); builder.Services.AddGrpcReflection(); // Additional configuration is required to successfully run gRPC on macOS. // For instructions on how to configure Kestrel and gRPC clients on macOS, visit https://go.microsoft.com/fwlink/?linkid=2099682 // Add services to the container. builder.Services.AddGrpc(); var app = builder.Build(); IWebHostEnvironment env = app.Environment; if (env.IsDevelopment()) { app.MapGrpcReflectionService(); } // Configure the HTTP request pipeline. app.MapGrpcService(); app.MapGet("/", () => "Communication with gRPC endpoints must be made through a gRPC client. To learn how to create a client, visit: https://go.microsoft.com/fwlink/?linkid=2086909"); app.Run();%改行%6. Put the “dotnet run” command to run gRPC service at local environment, then the “Now listening on: http://localhost:5008” statement displayed on the terminal pain. The HTTPS connection is able to use with the “dotnet dev-certs https” command in the terminal pane.%改行%At first, the Postman is easy to confirm the gRPC service connection than using the gRPCurl because the gRPCurl is command line application. So describes the Postman usage ahead, then the gRPCurl usage follows.%改行%• Call the gRPC service use the gRPCurl and the Postman.%改行%The “New…” menu item of the File menu item that displayed when press the hamburger icon of Postman has the gRPC connection feature, refer below figure.%改行%図（https://blog.processtune.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/02/PostmanCreateNewgRPC.png）%改行%From the terminal pain of Visual Studio Code, copy the part of the URL (localhost:5008) excluded the protocol part that is listening, and paste at the server address textbox of the gRPC Request tub. Then press the [Try again] link of the last item [Use server reflection] of the method select list that is shown when the method textbox selects. After pressing the [Try again] link, the list is changed to be able to select the method (SayHello).%改行%図（https://blog.processtune.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/03/PostmanInvoke.png）%改行%This “SayHello” method defined at the GreeterService class of the GreeterService.cs file, thus the reflection service create the proto file at the greet.proto file of the Protos folder.%改行%図（https://blog.processtune.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/03/FileStructure.png）%改行%Theリンク（https://github.com/fullstorydev/grpcurl/releases）is command line application for confirming the gRPC service communication. After installリンク（https://go.dev/doc/install）, install the gRPCurl (download the “grpcurl_1.8.7_windows_x86_64.zip” file and unzip it) and run the Windows Command Prompt at the gRPCurl install folder.%改行%図（https://blog.processtune.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/03/RungRPCurl.png）%改行%This figure is result of that acquire information of the gRPC service then call remote procedure.%改行%The describe command responds “greet.Greeter is a service” (package name and service name), and “rpc SayHello …” (method name). The Method is able to call with format “grpcurl -plaintext -d %value(s)% %server address% %package name%.%service name%/%methodname%”.%改行%Definition of argument(s) of the method of the service uses the “-d” command. Thus “grpcurl -plaintext -d %value(s)% localhost:5008 greet.Greeter/SayHello” warks well. Only in Windows development environment, argument(s) wrap with [” (double quate)] and JSON format: wrap key/value with [{} (Brace)] and wrap the key and the value each with [” (double quate)], [” (double quate)] in argument(s) needs escape.%改行%"grpcurl -plaintext -d "{\"name\": \"takao\"}" localhost:5008 greet.Greeter/SayHello"%改行%If there is Go development environment, it is able to connect to gRPC service with browser. If not, go to the page “Download and install” of the Go website and install it. After install it, install gRPC UI use the command “go install github.com/fullstorydev/grpcui/cmd/grpcui@latest” (move to the Go installed path if it needs). It is installed at the GOPATH of the system environment variables that is shown from the System Properties dialog’s [Environment Variables…] button select. The dialog is shown when select the [Advanced system settings] link of the [Related links] of the [System About] window that is displayed by selection the context menu item [System] of Windows icon (right click).%改行%Move to the gRPC UI installed path if it needs, type “grpcui -plaintext localhost:5008”, then the browser is opened and can test it.%改行%図（https://blog.processtune.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/03/EnvironmentVariables.png）図（https://blog.processtune.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/03/grpcui.png）%改行%ⓘ Recomended reference%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/?p=6649）%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/?p=6783）%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/?p=6804）%改行%Categories:%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/microsoft-azure/grpc/）リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/%e6%9c%aa%e5%88%86%e9%a1%9e/）%改行%Tags:%改行%No Tag%改行%Post navigation%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/2022/04/30/design-of-solution-with-grpc-communication/）%改行%Post navigation%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/2022/06/28/asp-net-core-options-pattern/）%改行%No responses yet%改行%Leave a Replyリンク（https://blog.processtune.com/2022/05/30/asp-net-core-grpc-grpc-communication-tools/#respond）%改行%Your email address will not be published.Required fields are marked*%改行%Comment*%改行%Name*%改行%Email*%改行%Website%改行%Save my name, email, and website in this browser for the next time I comment.%改行%© 2025 Processtune Blog. Title: Rust QR code generator App of Azure Functions – Processtune Blog
Categories: Azure Active Directory,Azure Functions,Microservice,Microsoft Azure,SlideShow,Visual Studio,Visual Studio Code,未分類
BodyText:
%改行%リンク（#content）%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/）%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/microsoft-azure/）%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/microsoft-azure/azure-active-directory/）%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/visual-studio/）%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/microservice/）%改行%リンク（#）%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/）%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/microsoft-azure/）%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/microsoft-azure/azure-active-directory/）%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/visual-studio/）%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/microservice/）%改行%Rust QR code generator App of Azure Functions%改行%図（https://blog.processtune.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/03/ContainerizationSeriesTitle.png）%改行%byリンク（https://blog.processtune.com/author/tetsuro-takao/）%改行%onリンク（https://blog.processtune.com/2021/09/01/）%改行%In the article [リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/?p=5925）], created Rust app uses warp library to listen HTTP GET request as web server.%改行%In this article, describes steps to add feature to listen POST request and to generate QR code and respond it. Addition, upload it and test it on Azure Functions.%改行%>>リンク（https://github.com/TetsuroTakao/QRCodeGenerator）.%改行%・Add feature of listen POST method%改行%Downloadリンク（https://github.com/TetsuroTakao/QRCodeGenerator）, and create function use Visual Studio Code Azure Functions extension.%改行%This article describes example as that the sample are downloaded to the [QRCodePOSTMethod] folder on local PC.%改行%Until show figure below, create local project like as the article [リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/?p=5925）] using the sample.%改行%図（https://blog.processtune.com/wp-content/uploads/2022/03/POSTMethodSample1.png）%改行%At first, define name of the Functions app using Azure Functions extension. This example name is [QRCodePOSTMethod].%改行%Though who read the article [リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/?p=5925）] can use same name used in its article, in this article, it is not good for my development environment that the Rust local project of GitHub is changed for this article. So changed a name using Azure Functions extension.%改行%Next, it’s flow of steps that add HTTP post method feature to main.rs, then the feature inject to the route definition. HTTP post method feature is code below, is similar as HTTP get method feature.%改行%let post_source = warp::post() .and(warp::body::json()) .map(|bodyjson: HashMap| { Response::builder() .header("content-type", "text/html") .status(StatusCode::OK) .body(format!("QR Code source [{}]", bodyjson.get("source").unwrap())) });%改行%Create a header and a body with status of the response using the warp response builder. While create the status, use the warp status object. So define using it at top of the [main.rs] file ([use warp::{http::{Response, StatusCode}, Filter};]).%改行%As last step, define routing. In case of the warp routing object, define the processing when HTTP get method is listened, then if the request is HTTP post method, executes the processing for it. The routing like it, needs allowing HTTP get method and HTTP post method.%改行%図（https://blog.processtune.com/wp-content/uploads/2022/03/AzureFunctionsCors.png）%改行%In addition, Azure Functions does not allowリンク（https://html.spec.whatwg.org/multipage/urls-and-fetching.html#cors-settings-attributes）as default, although it can change to allow. Evenifリンク（https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/logic-apps/logic-apps-azure-functions?tabs=consumption#find-functions-that-have-openapi-descriptions?WT.mc_id=AZ-MVP-4029060）aiming to accept request from JavaScript or otherリンク（https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Cross-origin_resource_sharing）, it not work well since the warp routing definition that does not allow as default. For that reason, create allowing of CORS using warp CORS object, as expand features in the future. The code below is definition of routing that flows from get request to post request, along with allowing them (CORS, creating header and acceptance of HTTP get/post method request).%改行%let cors = warp::cors() .allow_any_origin() .allow_headers(vec!["User-Agent", "Sec-Fetch-Mode", "Referer", "Origin", "Access-Control-Request-Method", "Access-Control-Request-Headers"]) .allow_methods(vec!["POST", "GET"]); let routes = warp::get().and(get_source) .or(warp::post().and(post_source)).with(cors); warp::serve(routes).run((Ipv4Addr::UNSPECIFIED, port)).await%改行%・Build and Confirming%改行%For confirming HTTP post method call result, build Rust project, copy created assembly to project root and Azure Functions Core Tools starting.%改行%図（https://blog.processtune.com/wp-content/uploads/2022/03/HttpPostRequestBuild.png）図（https://blog.processtune.com/wp-content/uploads/2022/03/HttpGetRequestInBrowser.png）%改行%Confirming for HTTP get method request without query string in browser is selection of the link of output starting result. In case of confirming for HTTP get method request with query string, input query string manually at the URL inputbox.%改行%Use Visual Studio Code extension named [Thunder Client] to confirming for HTTP get method request.%改行%図（https://blog.processtune.com/wp-content/uploads/2022/03/HttpPostRequestThunder.png）%改行%・Upload to Azure Functions and Test%改行%Like as the article [How to compile Rust assembly runnable on Linux for deployment to Azure Functions], it’s super easy to do it.%改行%» Basic step is below.%改行%Delete %projecr name%.exe that used for debugging on local environment.%改行%In the [Integrated terminal] >rm qrcode_generator.exe%改行%Build the project for x86_64-unknown-linux-musl platform that Azure Functions provided.%改行%In the [Integrated terminal] >cargo build –release –target=x86_64-unknown-linux-musl%改行%Copy builded the assembly from bild destination to project root.%改行%In the [Integrated terminal] >cp target\x86_64-unknown-linux-musl\release\qrcode_generator .%改行%Modify value [qrcode_generator.exe] of the [defaultExecutablePath] property to [qrcode_generator] on the [host.json] file.%改行%{…, “customHandler”: { “description”: { “defaultExecutablePath”: “qrcode_generator.exe”, …}, …}}%改行%Upload the assembly to Azure Functions using Azure Functions extention of Visual Studio Code.%改行%the [Integrated terminal] output…%改行%14:40:22 BlogSampleFunctions: Starting deployment…%改行%14:40:23 BlogSampleFunctions: Creating zip package…%改行%14:40:23 BlogSampleFunctions: Uploading zip package to storage container…%改行%14:40:24 BlogSampleFunctions: Zip package size: 2.13 MB%改行%14:40:26 BlogSampleFunctions: Deployment successful.%改行%14:40:36 BlogSampleFunctions: Syncing triggers…%改行%14:40:38 BlogSampleFunctions: Querying triggers…%改行%14:40:40 BlogSampleFunctions: HTTP Trigger Urls:%改行%QRCodePOSTMethod: https://blogsamplefunctions.azurewebsites.net/qrcodepostmethod%改行%Test Functions app at Azure portal.%改行%図（https://blog.processtune.com/wp-content/uploads/2022/03/AzureFunctionsPOSTTest.png）%改行%・Add QR Code generating feature%改行%There is the link to the Rust program as base code at the article [リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/?p=5925）].%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/?p=5925）リンク（https://github.com/TetsuroTakao/QRCodeCreation）%改行%The code has very simple (is two row code) function that generate QR code using a library of Rust ecosystem. The function argues string as source of QR code and return svg string (XML format). The XML is able to display on browser.%改行%pub fn create_qrcode_svg(_url: &str) -> String{ let code:QrCode = QrCode::new(_url).unwrap(); code.render::().build() }%改行%For now, the code is not need function. So as temporary, inject the code above inject into the HTTP post listener feature of main procedure.%改行%let post_source = warp::post() .and(warp::body::json()) .map(|bodyjson: HashMap| { let _url = bodyjson.get("source").unwrap(); let code:QrCode = QrCode::new(_url).unwrap(); let qrcode = code.render::().build(); Response::builder() .header("content-type", "image/svg+xml") .status(StatusCode::OK) .body(qrcode) });%改行%Injected two lines in the map process needs the [_url] varieble, so use the [bodyjson.get(“source”).unwrap()] statement of existing post listener feature. And render directly the result (strings of the [SVG] XML) of the [code.render::<:color>().build()] statement to the body. The warp filter generates XML content, so the [content-type] header value is [image/svg+xml]. Below figure is the result of the Thunder Client.%改行%図（https://blog.processtune.com/wp-content/uploads/2022/03/HttpPostQRRequestResult.png）%改行%ⓘ Additional information.%改行%If aim to response as HTML (does not response as API or RPC), does not use the [Response::builder] object, but also can use the [warp::reply::html] object for create filter feature more easly.%改行%Reply the body content to the request with the status code and the [content-type] header. For example in case of modifing the HTTP get listener feature, filter is returned as below.%改行%let url:&str = match p.get("source") { Some(q)=> q, None => "https://blog.processtune.com/" }; let code:QrCode = QrCode::new(url).unwrap(); let qr = code.render::().build(); let content = format!("{}
QR Code source [{}]", qr, &url); let reply = warp::reply::html(content); let reply = warp::reply::with_status(reply, StatusCode::OK); let reply = warp::reply::with_header(reply, "content-type", "text/html"); reply%改行%図（https://blog.processtune.com/wp-content/uploads/2022/03/HttpGetQRRequestResult.png）%改行%>>リンク（https://github.com/TetsuroTakao/QRCodePOSTMethod）.%改行%Categories:%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/microsoft-azure/azure-functions/）リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/slide-show/）リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/visual-studio/visual-studio-code/）リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/%e6%9c%aa%e5%88%86%e9%a1%9e/）%改行%Tags:%改行%No Tag%改行%Post navigation%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/2021/08/01/how-to-compile-rust-assembly-runnable-on-linux-for-deployment-to-azure-functions/）%改行%Post navigation%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/2021/10/01/blazor-wasm-aquires-svg-images-that-will-be-colored-by-javascript-later/）%改行%No responses yet%改行%Leave a Replyリンク（https://blog.processtune.com/2021/09/01/rust-qr-code-generator-app-of-azure-functions/#respond）%改行%Your email address will not be published.Required fields are marked*%改行%Comment*%改行%Name*%改行%Email*%改行%Website%改行%Save my name, email, and website in this browser for the next time I comment.%改行%© 2025 Processtune Blog. Title: Logging file save to Microsoft Azure part 1 – Processtune Blog
Categories: Active Directory,Azure Active Directory,kiosk mode,Microservice,Microsoft Azure,SlideShow,Universal Apps,Visual Studio,Windows 10 App,未分類
BodyText: %改行%リンク（#content）%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/）%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/microsoft-azure/）%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/microsoft-azure/azure-active-directory/）%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/visual-studio/）%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/microservice/）%改行%リンク（#）%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/）%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/microsoft-azure/）%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/microsoft-azure/azure-active-directory/）%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/visual-studio/）%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/microservice/）%改行%Logging file save to Microsoft Azure part 1%改行%byリンク（https://blog.processtune.com/author/tetsuro-takao/）%改行%onリンク（https://blog.processtune.com/2018/07/28/）%改行%This article is follow ofリンク（https://blog.processtune.com/?p=4562）. The article explains overall of several operation scenarios, and a flow control of the accounts use single operation scenario respectively of the operation scenarios. This article explains logging use log information model to make logging to more easily.%改行%This article continues toリンク（https://blog.processtune.com/?p=4814）which explains how to send log file to Azure Blob Storage. At first, this article explains how to implement logging.%改行%・It’s more better use formal logging tools%改行%Even if you used to use log4net or NLog, this article ‘DOES NOT’ explains these formal logging tool, but implements super easy logging instead of it. Because I want to show you that MVC(in this case, MVVM) implementation model is easy to choice for your solution product. Then, it affects very important point in development scenes later for CD/CI or servicing your software as API.%改行%図（https://blog.processtune.com/wp-content/uploads/2019/03/log4.png）%改行%図（https://blog.processtune.com/wp-content/uploads/2019/03/NLog.png）%改行%At first, please download the solution file from GitHub to your local pc.%改行%リンク（https://github.com/TetsuroTakao/WPFLockdownSample2）%改行%And This is the sample code which applied contents of this article.%改行%リンク（https://github.com/TetsuroTakao/WPFLockdownSample3）%改行%・Create a model of log information%改行%Create solution folder named ‘Models’ at root of the solution. And create model class [Log].%改行%図（https://blog.processtune.com/wp-content/uploads/2019/03/solutionExplorer03.png）%改行%図（https://blog.processtune.com/wp-content/uploads/2019/03/solutionExplorer04.png）%改行%The model [Log] class has simple properties [Message],[OccurredTime],[OperatorName],[LogType] and create Type name [LogType] which has types [Default],[Information],[Error],[Operation]%改行%namespace WPFLockdownSample.Models { public class Log { public string Message { get; set; } public DateTime OccurredTime { get; set; } public string OperatorName { get; set; } public LogType LogType { get; set; } } public enum LogType { Default = 0, Information = 1, Error = 2, Operation = 4 } }%改行%As Next step, modify App_Startup method of App.xaml.cs to be able to logging. One point which you have to aware in this step, if some data has DateTime type data, you have to consider both when the data is used by local time and is used at Microsoft Azure programmatically. So the data is held as DateTime type better.%改行%void App_Startup() { void App_Startup(object sender, StartupEventArgs e) { Listlogs = new List(); Log log = new Log() { Message = "checkking app files ...", OccurredTime = DateTime.Now, OperatorName = typeof(App).Name, LogType = LogType.Information }; logs.Add(log); //Check existing of log file. //if (logging.ReadLogs().Count == 0) //{ // [Local account creation] explains how to create these four accounts. // And how to navigate use logon script // CoreApplication.Exit(); //} //else if (logFile.Exists) check current user //{ // [Desktop UI control] explains how to check current user // if (UserName == "maintenanceOperator") navigate to MaintenanceWindow // if (UserName == "appOperator") quiet this app. This account has specific automatic // run application when this account sign in. // if (UserName == "appUser") quiet this app. This account has specific automatic // run application when this account sign in. //} } }%改行%The type [Log] is in the Namespace [WPFLockdownSample.Models], so add ‘using WPFLockdownSample.Models;’ statement at top of App.xml.cs. ‘List<Log> logs’ holds several logs information while the logs will be written to log file( and the contents of the file will send to Microsoft Azure later).%改行%The [OperatorName] property is used for recording operations of application with [LogType] property. The [Operation]’s value in [LogType] type. In case of this sample code, the message is application information, so use [LogType.Information] and ‘typeof(App).Name’ for the value. if application message occurs in MainPage, use [LogType.Information] and ‘typeof(MainPage).Name’ value. So it will modified to ‘this.GetType().Name’ later, but it’s keep it so far.%改行%・Write log information to log file%改行%To create function of log writing, create solution folder named [Features] and create [DataAccessLayer] class, The class use Json, so please install Json serializer using NuGet. And then create method below.%改行%図（https://blog.processtune.com/wp-content/uploads/2019/03/jsonserialiser.png）%改行%図（https://blog.processtune.com/wp-content/uploads/2019/03/logging.png）%改行%public class DataAccessLayer { public FileInfo LogFile { get; private set; } const string appFilesPath = @"c:\WFPLockdownSample"; DirectoryInfo logFolder = new DirectoryInfo(appFilesPath); public DataAccessLayer() { LogFile = new FileInfo ( appFilesPath + @"\" + DateTime.Now.ToString("yyyyMMdd") + "logs.log" ); } public void AppendWriteLogs(List<Log> logs, bool reverse = true) { if(reverse) logs.Reverse(); string jsonstring = string.Empty; logs.AddRange(ReadLogs()); using (StreamWriter file = File.CreateText(LogFile.FullName)) { JsonSerializer serializer = new JsonSerializer(); serializer.Serialize(file, logs); } } public List<Log> ReadLogs() { List<Log> logs = new List<Log>(); string jsonstring = string.Empty; if (LogFile.Exists) jsonstring = File.ReadAllText(LogFile.FullName); if (!string.IsNullOrEmpty(jsonstring)) { logs = JsonConvert.DeserializeObject<List<Log>>(jsonstring); } return logs; } }%改行%This logging feature of [DataAccessLayer] class access to local folder, and in the future, other data sources or data service API, so it should be created as ‘partial’ class, it will be modified as partial class later, but it is normal class so far.%改行%This class has public property [LogFile], it set instance at constructor. And provides two features, one is [AppendWriteLogs] method and another is [ReadLogs] method.%改行%[AppendWriteLogs] method is a feature which outputs logs information to log file, [logs] argument is a container of log information which added when it occurred, so log information listed by time series order in [logs] argument, therefore most newest log information should be written at top most of log file. So order of logs information which accepted are reversed as default execution.%改行%Then logs of the log file append to [logs] argument and write back to the log file.%改行%On other hand, [ReadLogs] method provides a feature of read contents of the log file. If log file is not exists or log file has not any contents, it returns blank container of logs information. This state is first time execution of this application. So if log information count of this log container is zero, this application try to create four accounts for operation scenarios.%改行%・Use log functions%改行%To use write log function [AppendWriteLogs] method of the [DataAccessLayer] class, call the [DataAccessLayer] class at top of [App_Startup] method, then use [ReadLogs] method and count logs.%改行%And create log information which scenario was choosen, then write logs information use [AppendWriteLogs] method at end of all step.%改行%public partial class App : Application { DataAccessLayer dataAccessLayer = new DataAccessLayer(); void App_Startup(object sender, StartupEventArgs e) { Listlogs = new List(); Log log = new Log() { Message = "checkking app files ...", OccurredTime = DateTime.Now, OperatorName = typeof(App).Name, LogType = LogType.Information }; logs.Add(log); if (dataAccessLayer.ReadLogs().Count == 0) { log = new Log() { Message = "this exesution is first time.", OccurredTime = DateTime.Now, OperatorName = typeof(App).Name, LogType = LogType.Information }; logs.Add(log); //[Local account creation] explains how to create these four accounts. //And how to navigate use logon script //CoreApplication.Exit(); } else { log = new Log() { Message = "this execution has run on this device more than twice.", OccurredTime = DateTime.Now, OperatorName = typeof(App).Name, LogType = LogType.Information }; logs.Add(log); //[Desktop UI control] explains how to check current user //if (UserName == "maintenanceOperator") navigate to MaintenanceWindow //if (UserName == "appOperator") quiet this app. This account has specific automatic //run application when this account sign in. //if (UserName == "appUser") quiet this app. This account has specific automatic //run application when this account sign in. } log = new Log() { Message = "checkking app files is complete.", OccurredTime = DateTime.Now, OperatorName = typeof(App).Name, LogType = LogType.Information }; logs.Add(log); dataAccessLayer.AppendWriteLogs(logs); } }%改行%Go next stepリンク（https://blog.processtune.com/?p=4814）%改行%orリンク（https://blog.processtune.com/?p=4551）%改行%,リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/?p=4781）%改行%,リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/?p=4785）%改行%Categories:%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/technical-memo/active-directory/）リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/technical-memo/kiosk-mode/）リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/microsoft-azure/）リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/slide-show/）リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/winui/universal-apps/）リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/visual-studio/）リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/winui/universal-apps/windows-store-app/windows-10-app/）リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/%e6%9c%aa%e5%88%86%e9%a1%9e/）%改行%Tags:%改行%No Tag%改行%Post navigation%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/2018/06/30/kiosk-mode-operation-design-2/）%改行%Post navigation%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/2018/08/31/local-account-creation/）%改行%No responses yet%改行%Leave a Replyリンク（https://blog.processtune.com/2018/07/28/logging-file-save-to-microsoft-azure-part-1/#respond）%改行%Your email address will not be published.Required fields are marked*%改行%Comment*%改行%Name*%改行%Email*%改行%Website%改行%Save my name, email, and website in this browser for the next time I comment.%改行%© 2025 Processtune Blog. Title: Information architecture for Windows Store app – Processtune Blog
Categories: Azure Active Directory,Microservice,Microsoft Azure,Visual Studio,Windows 8 App,Windows Store App
BodyText: %改行%リンク（#content）%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/）%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/microsoft-azure/）%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/microsoft-azure/azure-active-directory/）%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/visual-studio/）%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/microservice/）%改行%リンク（#）%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/）%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/microsoft-azure/）%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/microsoft-azure/azure-active-directory/）%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/visual-studio/）%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/microservice/）%改行%Information architecture for Windows Store app%改行%byリンク（https://blog.processtune.com/author/tetsuro-takao/）%改行%onリンク（https://blog.processtune.com/2014/06/08/）%改行%There is should be considered that Windows Store app is used in touch devices and mobile device. In order to create Windows Store app considering a use case like this, it is necessary to spend a lot of time for the design of information. Because organizing of information affects to a user operations of Windows Store app compared to past applications.%改行%In this article, the specific steps of the design of information is described.%改行%リンク（http://1drv.ms/PC62Ga）%改行%リンク（http://www.processtune.com/blog/?p=756）%改行%A characteristic of Windows Store app%改行%Used in touch devices%改行%Do not be an application that has a deep hierarchical structure.%改行%Should not provide information by many detail split area a screen.%改行%Used in mobile devices%改行%Require a mechanism of roaming data for offline.%改行%It’s easy to handle if roaming-data is maintained as an object model.%改行%It is necessary that information provided by the content is well organized.%改行%Information Architecture%改行%*Classification%改行%Richard Saul Wurman (1935.3.26 – ) , in his book “Information anxiety”, proposed concept of classification of information has become one of the design methods of information at the time of a contents providing.%改行%Five classification of information (Information anxiety 1)%改行%A lot of information can be classified by Location, Alphabet, Time, Category, Hierarchy (LATCH).%改行%Information architecture of recent years (Information anxiety 2)%改行%Information can be classified by Time, Alphabet, Category, Location, Continuous amount.%改行%*Reason why tagging and layering is not included in the information architecture.%改行%A layering is able to instead of a tagging. In addition, both a tagging and a layering depends on user-driven changing to handle an information.%改行%The classification like this depends on the place where the information is used. It means a layering can use to a unique classification, only if it is not changed frequently such as hierarchy of an organization. Also a tagging can use to a temporary classification, only if it is not aimed to shared with another user such as user tag.%改行%Example : The minutes of the meeting saved to a file server (layering of the left in the figure below) is representable by tagging. The right of figure below.%改行%図（http://processtuneblog.azurewebsites.net/wp-content/uploads/2014/06/FileDirectoryHierarchy.en_.png）図（http://processtuneblog.azurewebsites.net/wp-content/uploads/2014/06/TaggingImage.en_.png）%改行%Representation by tagging information can substitute the representation of information by hierarchy, but not vice versa.%改行%In order to know the location of the file by everyone.%改行%*Tiering in same scope of the content.%改行%If all tiers of a hierarchy is structured by one classification of the information architecture, anyone can reach purpose information easily (See figure below). A content should be navigated by same scope. If information classification is mixed in the hierarchy, a user must go to the tier below of the hierarchical structure to do the next operations. (Figure above left, In order to get to the minutes of the April 27 2011, a user can’t know that there is a folder called “Windows Phone app development projects” without opening the “NEC” folder.)%改行%*Navigate to the content using the menu or the like that leads in same scope.%改行%There is the most appropriate classification for information when providing it. (Time series classification is the best in the case of breaking news. To classify a minutes is same also).%改行%図（http://processtuneblog.azurewebsites.net/wp-content/uploads/2014/06/Hierarchy.en_.png）%改行%Classification of project or customer (gray area in figure above) is implements as menu items or tab items. An items of a menu or the like such as a tab is used for assist to navigate information by the other classification.%改行%In the case of breaking news, “Domestic, Abroad” (Location) or “Political, Crime” (Category) is an aid to navigate by other classification. The items of a menu or a tab navigate to last scope of the content, (time series).%改行%When offering the past news archive however, position or category is more important classification, compare to time series. Thus, time series is better to used as a menu or the like, and position or category is last scope of the content.%改行%Like this, a scheme to information is different by an aims for use.%改行%Therefore, navigation of information should be designed by which classification is main or aid.%改行%Anti-pattern of an object model%改行%Information group that is consistent scope of the content (classification of information architecture is not crossed) can be provided to an application, as the information properties by an object model that is implemented in MVVM or MVC design pattern.%改行%Consequence, an object model can be separated from a business logic that is a transfer of the object model. An object model as this type is domain model completely.%改行%*Disadvantage of providing an object model as tagging information or layering it.%改行%An object model is a container in order to create a combination of data for a form of business from data for a form of regularized to be persisted. And it to be transferred among application tiers as a refer of a data model properties.%改行%For example in case of providing songs data. Some songs is performed by many artists, and an artist performs many songs.%改行%図（http://processtuneblog.azurewebsites.net/wp-content/uploads/2014/06/DomainDesign.png）%改行%If a scope of the content for an object model is not consistency, It leads to a reduction in development efficiency because a developers must understand a form of all of the models (shape of the information difficult to predict).%改行%Because an object model is a fixed manner information characteristics, if the information would be changed frequently, as an object model should not be provided from an application.%改行%A tagging or a layering is better to be able to determine freely when user handle the information. Or it should be able to extending function by a programmer. Therefore those classification is not suitable to object model.%改行%*An object model can not change without it’s new release.%改行%Modification of the application is required in order to change the object model provided by an application.%改行%In the case of Windows Store app that provides UX, application changes due to an object model changes can not follow an evolution of user experience.%改行%As addition to viewpoint (inefficiency of the development) which described above, a tagging or a layering of information should be controled by end-user. It is one approach of keep an evolution of UX by an application that leave a portion of operate information by end-user.%改行%To provide high information interactivity by object model rather than application logic.%改行%A pattern of designing object model%改行%*A classification of temporary or unique.%改行%A function that create shape of tag or hierarchy is able to provide.%改行%Thereby an application becomes possible correspond to the evolution of an application that would born from a changes in the operation of the user.%改行%As other side, temporary information or unique information can provide as an object model.%改行%Example : Unique classification in the department management in the enterprise. And the temporary classification as a certain period music information.%改行%図（http://processtuneblog.azurewebsites.net/wp-content/uploads/2014/06/TemporaryHierarchy.en_.png）図（http://processtuneblog.azurewebsites.net/wp-content/uploads/2014/06/UniqueryHierarchy.en_.png）%改行%*The provision of business object for a particular%改行%The case of classifying the departments information in the enterprise can be provided as a hierarchy object model fixedly. The reason is below.%改行%1. Changes of the form of organizational structure is small.%改行%2. Changes of the form is not frequently.%改行%3. People who don’t know the form of organizational structure would not operate in-house systems.%改行%In addition, for a particular purpose, such as to send the weekly featured song information for applications can be implemented as a unique object model in the form of temporary information.%改行%Under conditions other than these particular usage, information classification should be able to freely set by the user.%改行%Design Considerations%改行%*Preparing five properties for a classification of the information architecture%改行%Fitting least one classification of information architecture to the properties of the object.%改行%Example as music information.%改行%Location%改行%Stored URL, Band formed at, Activity place%改行%Alphabet%改行%Initials, First letter of title in other language%改行%Time%改行%Release date, Data update%改行%Category%改行%Genre, Language, Region%改行%Continuous%改行%Number of sales, Number of broadcasts%改行%Example as the place of birth information of the members of the artists, by inheriting the position model which is a classification of information architecture.%改行%図（http://processtuneblog.azurewebsites.net/wp-content/uploads/2014/04/ObjectModelDiagram.png）%改行%図（http://processtuneblog.azurewebsites.net/wp-content/uploads/2014/04/LocationInformation.png）%改行%Categories:%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/winui/universal-apps/windows-store-app/windows-8-app/）リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/winui/universal-apps/windows-store-app/）%改行%Tags:%改行%No Tag%改行%Post navigation%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/2014/04/09/using-office-365-authentication-in-windows-8-store-apps/）%改行%Post navigation%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/2014/07/13/alm%e3%82%92%e6%8f%90%e6%a1%88%e3%81%97%e3%81%9f%e9%9a%9b%e3%81%ab%e8%8b%a6%e5%8a%b4%e3%81%97%e3%81%9f%e8%a9%b1%ef%bc%88%e5%85%a85%e5%9b%9e%ef%bc%89/）%改行%No responses yet%改行%Leave a Replyリンク（https://blog.processtune.com/2014/06/08/information-architecture-for-windows-store-app-2/#respond）%改行%Your email address will not be published.Required fields are marked*%改行%Comment*%改行%Name*%改行%Email*%改行%Website%改行%Save my name, email, and website in this browser for the next time I comment.%改行%© 2025 Processtune Blog. Title: You can share the devices profile of Chrome and Edge – Processtune Blog
Categories: Azure Active Directory,Browser,CSS,HTML,Microservice,Microsoft Azure,SlideShow,Technical Memo,Visual Studio,未分類
BodyText: %改行%リンク（#content）%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/）%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/microsoft-azure/）%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/microsoft-azure/azure-active-directory/）%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/visual-studio/）%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/microservice/）%改行%リンク（#）%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/）%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/microsoft-azure/）%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/microsoft-azure/azure-active-directory/）%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/visual-studio/）%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/microservice/）%改行%You can share the devices profile of Chrome and Edge%改行%byリンク（https://blog.processtune.com/author/tetsuro-takao/）%改行%onリンク（https://blog.processtune.com/2020/10/01/）%改行%Many devices will brows your web app. You may correspond to the situation with using media query or sometime using JavaScript.%改行%The devices profile used in the inspector feature of the Chrome browser is updated frequently, so we developers may not need modify it ( The profile for ‘Surface Duo’ or ‘Galaxy Fold’ are already included as default when I wrote this article on 1st Oct. 2020). Although, if you want to modify it since several reasons, such as that the main stream of device market of your country is not included in the emulators list, or you want to affect some visual impacts for your web app to target devices which is not included in the emulators list. You can modify and share the devices profile of the emulator launched from the inspector feature as the developer tool of Chrome and also Edge browser for team development more efficiently.%改行%This article describes how to modify and share the profile.%改行%図（https://blog.processtune.com/wp-content/uploads/2020/12/supportDevices.png）%改行%・Developer tools%改行%Modern browser such as the Chrome or the Edge and also the Safari and the Firefox provides the inspector feature for developers. It is useful to develop a web app especially when it runs, so web app developers use a emulator of the inspector feature of the developer tool to confirm how their app displayed in individual devices. Modern browser provides other features as the developer tool, this article focuses on the device emulator settings of the developer tool launch from the inspector feature of the browser.%改行%In the case of the Chrome browser (same as the Edge browser), right click at contents and select [Inspect] of the context menu. And then, select the icon of left top corner in the developer tool to show the device tool bar. The icon selecting means [Toggle device toolbar].%改行%図（https://blog.processtune.com/wp-content/uploads/2020/11/developerTool.png）図（https://blog.processtune.com/wp-content/uploads/2020/11/deviceEmulation.png）%改行%Since the browser shows contents in the [Responsive] mode, select prefer device name to confirm how the contents displayed on the device.%改行%The developer tool keeps menu items of the emulators list, you can modify and delete the device profile which you added.%改行%図（https://blog.processtune.com/wp-content/uploads/2020/11/iPhoneX.png）図（https://blog.processtune.com/wp-content/uploads/2020/11/iPhoneXView.png）%改行%・Add a device profile%改行%Select the [Edit] menu item most below in the devices list of the [Responsive] menu of the tool bar, then the [Settings] page is shown, select the [Add custom device] button at the [Device] section of the [Settings] page.%改行%Put a device name into the [Device Name] textbox, and put other settings, horizontal pixel size, vertical pixel size and user agent string. At last, confirm selected the [Mobile] item of the [User agent type] select list, and select the [Add] button to save it. This settings modifiable after saving. After figures bellow, detail of the [User agent type] select list and the [Device pixel ratio] textbox are described.%改行%図（https://blog.processtune.com/wp-content/uploads/2020/11/motoGPro.png）図（https://blog.processtune.com/wp-content/uploads/2020/11/motoGProView.png）%改行%・Calculate of the Device pixel ratio (DPR)%改行%The DPR calculate is little bit complicate.%改行%The ideal pixel density for distance from device to human eyes is 150 pixels per inch because if the distance from PC monitor to human eyes is 28 inch(71.12 cm) and the distance to smart phone display is 18 inch. This value is calculated from 96 pixel per inch for PC display.%改行%(28 divides 18) times 96 equals 149.33….%改行%The reason that the ideal pixel density is 96 pixel per inch for PC display is calculated from 133.33…% larger than 72 pixel per inch paper environment (one dot is similar to one pixel in legacy monitor). It was important for printing that a contents displayed in 72 pixel per inch to a monitor because the contents used to be out to144 dpi image printer in the early times of paper era.%改行%The article of link below describes deferent distance between reading paper and display.%改行%>>リンク（https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/archive/blogs/fontblog/where-does-96-dpi-come-from-in-windows?WT.mc_id=DT-MVP-4029060）%改行%The device pixel density provided smart phone brands will be able to figure out from calculate which the physical pixel density of smart phone divides by the ideal pixel density above.%改行%This article use Moto G7 Power for example. At first, go to the website of the brand to confirm display specification.%改行%It explains%改行%Resolution - HD+, 403 ppi%改行%図（https://blog.processtune.com/wp-content/uploads/2020/12/motog7specifications.png）%改行%so DPR is 403 divides 150 equals 2.6866….%改行%・Set DPR value, css width and css height%改行%Next step, calculate css width and css height value for settings of an emulation profile. The width of the device is 720 pixels divides 2.69 equals 268 pixels, set integer value to profile. The css height is that 1570 pixels divides 2.69 equals 584 pixels.%改行%Last step is seeking the user agent string from internet. I could find out the user agent string of the Moto G7 Power. Then set it to the profile.%改行%図（https://blog.processtune.com/wp-content/uploads/2020/12/motog7useragent.png）図（https://blog.processtune.com/wp-content/uploads/2020/12/motog7profilesettings.png）%改行%・Share the settings of devices profile%改行%Even if you create the devices profile in the Chrome browser, it can share with Edge team members because it is defined with same structure in JSON format as the devices profile of the Edge browser inspector feature.%改行%{ "account_id_migration_state": 2, "account_info": ..., "account_tracker_service_last_update": ..., "alternate_error_pages":..., "autocomplete":..., "autofill": ..., "bookmark_bar": ..., "browser":..., "browser_content_container_height": 0, "browser_content_container_width": 0, "browser_content_container_x": 0, "browser_content_container_y": 80, "caretbrowsing":..., "collections":..., "countryid_at_install": 19024, "custom_links":..., "data_reduction":..., "default_apps_install_state": 3, "devtools": { "adb_key":..., "preferences": { "Inspector.drawerSplitViewState": "{\"horizontal\":{\"size\":0,\"showMode\":\"OnlyMain\"}}", "InspectorView.splitViewState": "{\"vertical\":{\"size\":0}}", "closeableTabs": "{\"security\":true,\"heap_profiler\":true,\"resources\":true,\"lighthouse\":true}", "currentDockState": "\"right\"", "customEmulatedDeviceList": "[ { \"title\": \"Motorola moto g7 power\", \"type\": \"unknown\", \"user-agent\": \"Mozilla/5.0 (Linux; Android 9; moto g(7) power) AppleWebKit /537.36 (KHTML, like Gecko) Chrome/71.0.3578.99 Mobile Safari/537.36\", \"capabilities\": [ \"mobile\", \"touch\" ], \"screen\": { \"device-pixel-ratio\": 1.8, \"vertical\": { \"width\": 400, \"height\": 844 }, \"horizontal\": { \"width\": 844, \"height\": 400 } }, \"modes\": [ { \"title\": \"\", \"orientation\": \"vertical\", \"insets": { \"left\": 0, \"top\": 0, \"right\": 0, \"bottom\": 0 } }, { \"title\": \"\", \"orientation\": \"horizontal\", \"insets\": { \"left\": 0, \"top\": 0, \"right\": 0, \"bottom\": 0 } } ], \"show-by-default\": true, \"dual-screen\": false, \"show\": \"Default\" } ]", "elements.styles.sidebar.width": "{\"vertical\":{\"size\":0,\"showMode\":\"OnlyMain\"}}", "emulation.deviceModeValue": "{\"device\":\"\",\"orientation\":\"\",\"mode\":\"\"}", "emulation.showDeviceMode": "true", "emulation.showDeviceOutline": "false", "inspectorVersion": "30", "releaseNoteVersionSeen": "27", "standardEmulatedDeviceList":..., "undefined-tabOrder": ... } }, "domain_diversity":..., "download": ..., "dual_engine": ..., "edge": ..., "extensions":..., "family_safety": ..., "google":..., "http_original_content_length": "3375462", "http_received_content_length": "3375462", "invalidation":..., "language_model_counters":..., "media":..., "media_router": ..., "ntp":..., "open_save_as_awareness_ui":..., "password_manager":..., "plugins":..., "previews": ..., "profile":..., "reset_prepopulated_engines": false, "safebrowsing":..., "settings":..., "signin": ..., "spellcheck":..., "sync": ..., "translate_accepted_count": ..., "translate_blocked_languages": ..., "translate_denied_count_for_language":..., "translate_last_denied_time_for_language":..., "translate_site_blacklist_with_time": {}, "translate_too_often_denied_for_language": ..., "unified_consent":..., "web_apps":... }%改行%If Microsoft Edge browser team create the profile, the emulator profile is at “\%AppData%\Local\Microsoft\Edge\User Data\Default\Preferences” of the profile creator folder in the “C:\Users\” directory. So, share segment of “devtools-preferences-customEmulatedDeviceList” array JSON.%改行%In the case that Chrome team creates the profile, it is at “\%AppData%\Local\Google\Chrome\User Data\Default\Preferences”.%改行%One point I have to say, “devtools-preferences-customEmulatedDeviceList-type” property is not saved to the profile, I’ll ask product team of Microsoft.%改行%Categories:%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/technical-memo/browser/）リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/technical-memo/css/）リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/technical-memo/html/）リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/slide-show/）リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/technical-memo/）リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/%e6%9c%aa%e5%88%86%e9%a1%9e/）%改行%Tags:%改行%No Tag%改行%Post navigation%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/2020/09/01/how-to-set-attributes-of-azure-active-directory-application-for-an-app-using-javascript-to-authenticate-users/）%改行%Post navigation%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/2021/05/01/should-define-layout-at-first-if-you-choice-atomic-design/）%改行%No responses yet%改行%Leave a Replyリンク（https://blog.processtune.com/2020/10/01/you-can-share-the-devices-profile-of-chrome-and-edge/#respond）%改行%Your email address will not be published.Required fields are marked*%改行%Comment*%改行%Name*%改行%Email*%改行%Website%改行%Save my name, email, and website in this browser for the next time I comment.%改行%© 2025 Processtune Blog. Title: Deploy the gRPC service to the Azure App Service – Processtune Blog
Categories: Azure Active Directory,gRPC,Microservice,Microsoft Azure,Visual Studio,未分類
BodyText: %改行%リンク（#content）%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/）%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/microsoft-azure/）%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/microsoft-azure/azure-active-directory/）%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/visual-studio/）%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/microservice/）%改行%リンク（#）%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/）%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/microsoft-azure/）%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/microsoft-azure/azure-active-directory/）%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/visual-studio/）%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/microservice/）%改行%Deploy the gRPC service to the Azure App Service%改行%図（https://blog.processtune.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/03/SeriesTitle.png）%改行%byリンク（https://blog.processtune.com/author/tetsuro-takao/）%改行%onリンク（https://blog.processtune.com/2022/09/30/）%改行%Microsoft Azure provides hosting feature of the gRPC service as Azure App Service, Azure Container Apps and Azure Kubernetes Service.%改行%This article describes how to deploy the gRPC service that is created in the article [リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/?p=6649）] to Azure App Service.%改行%>>リンク（https://github.com/TetsuroTakao/gRPCAzureDeploySample）%改行%• Create the Web App Service using Azure portal%改行%Open Azure portal and select the [Create a resource] then select the Web App [Create] link, and set values as figure below.%改行%図（https://blog.processtune.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/03/AzureAppServiceCreation.png）%改行%Set values of settings with select the [Configuration] of the [Settings] section of the left pane after selecting the Web App Service (or select the [Go to resource] when complete creating the service) created it.%改行%Though there are application settings and general settings in the [Configuration], set value “2.0” to the [HTTP version] property and set value “On” to the [HTTP 2.0 Proxy] property in the [General settings] tab.%改行%Select the [+ New application settings] link after the [Application settings] link, then the [Add/Edit application setting] blade displayed, set value “HTTP20_ONLY_PORT” to the [Name] property and set value “5243” to the [Value] property.%改行%図（https://blog.processtune.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/03/AzureAppServiceSettings.png）%改行%• Execute the gRPC service with HTTPS%改行%Created gRPC service uses TSL 1.2 (figure below left), therefore several modifying.%改行%There are two settings in the [launchsettings.json] file of the [Properties] folder of the sample project that was described in the article ‘ASP.NET Core gRPC & gRPC communication tools’. Execute the project using the setting [https]. Please download the sample project from GitHub, or create the gRPC service with referring of the article.%改行%The figure below right is type the [dotnet run –launch-profile https] command in the [Ternimal] of Visual Studio Code.%改行%図（https://blog.processtune.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/03/TSLSettings.png）図（https://blog.processtune.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/03/ExecuteHTTPS.png）%改行%The gRPC communication provided Azure Web App Service needs TSL communication. It needs certificate to communicate while TSL handshake. Therefor create certificate, in many cases, there is the certificate for development already in the PC. For confirming local certificates in development environment use the command [dotnet dev-certs https -v] in the [Terminal] pane of Visual Studio Code. Find the certificate that is in vailed term and copy the certificate id. The certificate is also able to create as self signed with Go language that install in previous article [リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/?p=6649）]. Put the command below in the [Terminal] pane of Visual Studio Code (at current directory that moved project root folder).%改行%//In the case of self signed $Go installed path$\bin\go run $Go installed path$\src\crypto\tls\generate_cert.go --rsa-bits=2048 --host=localhost%改行%In the case of certificate for development, check the [ASP.NET Core HTTPS development certificate] is trusted using the [certmgr.msc] command input to Windows [Run] menu item. If not, put the [dotnet dev-certs https –check –trust] in the [Terminal] pane of Visual Studio Code.%改行%図（https://blog.processtune.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/03/CheckCertTrusted.png）%改行%There is also able to check using the command [dotnet dev-certs https -v] in the [Terminal] pane of Visual Studio Code. And copy collect one certificate to use at C# code (refer to figure below). Select certificate that includes value [localhost] as the [CN] property and also has value of the [IsHttpsDevelopmentCertificate] property is [true] and valid period. If not, use the command [dotnet dev-certs https] to create the development certificate (detail is refer to theリンク（https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/dotnet/core/tools/dotnet-dev-certs?WT.mc_id=DT-MVP-4029060）).%改行%図（https://blog.processtune.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/03/devcert.png）%改行%The code copied is used for the gRPC client app that is able to download fromリンク（https://github.com/TetsuroTakao/gRPC/tree/main/GrpcGreeterClient）.%改行%using System.Security.Cryptography.X509Certificates; ... X509Store store = new X509Store(StoreName.My, StoreLocation.CurrentUser); store.Open(OpenFlags.ReadOnly | OpenFlags.OpenExistingOnly); var cert = store.Certificates.Where(c => c.GetCertHashString() =="E73488EF2CD742DE44A31ECA660F9FD8AD3D46C6").FirstOrDefault(); ...%改行%There is displayed error when execute the gRPC service use HTTPS and execute client-side app as simple [dotnet run] command with leave downloaded client-side app sample code. Thus, open the [Program.cs] file of the client-side app project and modify an argument of the [ForAddress] method to [“http://localhost:5008”].%改行%図（https://blog.processtune.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/03/gRPCServiceExecuteOnLocal.png）図（https://blog.processtune.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/03/gRPCClientExecuteOnLocal.png）%改行%Confirm certificate for development of localhost using the code above.%改行%using Grpc.Net.Client; // using GrpcGreeterClient; using System.Security.Cryptography.X509Certificates; //using var channel = GrpcChannel.ForAddress("https://localhost:8585"); using var channel = GrpcChannel.ForAddress("http://localhost:5008"); X509Store store = new X509Store(StoreName.My, StoreLocation.CurrentUser); store.Open(OpenFlags.ReadOnly | OpenFlags.OpenExistingOnly); var cert = store.Certificates.Where(c => c.GetCertHashString() =="E73488EF2CD742DE44A31ECA660F9FD8AD3D46C6").FirstOrDefault(); // var client = new Greeter.GreeterClient(channel); // var reply = await client.SayHelloAsync(new HelloRequest { Name = "GreeterClient" }); // Console.WriteLine("Greeting: " + reply.Message); Console.WriteLine(string.Format("{0}, Press any key to exit...",cert!.FriendlyName)); Console.ReadKey();%改行%• Deploy the gRPC service to Azure Web App Service%改行%It is able to use Visual Studio Code to deploy the gRPC service to Azure Web App service. Download the sample project that was described in the article ‘リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/?p=6649）‘ fromリンク（https://github.com/TetsuroTakao/gRPC2）, or create the gRPC service refer to the article.%改行%The gRPC service has to be able to listen on only port 5243 that set at Azure Web App Service with HTTP 2.0, so modify configuration of the Program.cs file as below. This code needs theリンク（https://www.nuget.org/packages/Microsoft.AspNetCore.Authentication.Certificate）.%改行%using Microsoft.AspNetCore.Server.Kestrel.Https; using Microsoft.AspNetCore.Authentication.Certificate; ... var builder = WebApplication.CreateBuilder(); builder.Services.AddAuthentication( CertificateAuthenticationDefaults.AuthenticationScheme) // use Microsoft.AspNetCore.Authentication.Certificate .AddCertificate(options => { options.AllowedCertificateTypes = CertificateTypes.All; // use Microsoft.AspNetCore.Authentication.Certificate }); builder.Services.AddAuthorization(); builder.WebHost.ConfigureKestrel(options => { options.ListenAnyIP(5008); options.ListenAnyIP(7116, listenOptions => { listenOptions.Protocols = Microsoft.AspNetCore.Server.Kestrel.Core.HttpProtocols.Http2; listenOptions.UseHttps(options => { options.ClientCertificateMode = ClientCertificateMode.RequireCertificate; // use Microsoft.AspNetCore.Server.Kestrel.Https options.ClientCertificateValidation = (certificate, chain, errors) => { options.ServerCertificate = certificate; return true; }; } ); }); });%改行%This code adds the Authentication feature and the Authorization feature, also needs the Routing feature, the Authentication feature and the Authorization feature for Web application, thus add code below before the [app.MapGrpcService();] statement.%改行%app.UseRouting(); app.UseAuthentication(); app.UseAuthorization();%改行%Figure below is all code of service and client, therefore can deploy the service part to Azure.%改行%図（https://blog.processtune.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/03/gRPCAllCodeServiceAndClient.png）%改行%Sign in Azure from Visual Studio Code before proceeding to the next step. Press the key [Ctrl] + [Shift] + [P] to open the [Command palette], and input ‘azure sign’ and select the menu item [Azure Sign In] from the list of the palette. This operation needs the [Azure Tools] Visual Studio Code extension, thus install it if the extension is not installed (Figure left).%改行%Confirm sign in information at the [Azure Account Status] of the [Status bar] and select the [Azure Tools] extension at the [Activity Bar]. Then can select subscription of Azure when select the [RESOURCES] section of the [Azure Tools] extension (Figure right).%改行%図（https://blog.processtune.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/03/VisualStudioCodeAzureToolsExtension.png）図（https://blog.processtune.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/03/SelectSubscription.png）%改行%Press the key [Ctrl] + [Shift] + [P] in Visual Studio Code to open the [Command palate], And input ‘Azure App Service’ to the palate. Select the Subscription at the [Command palette] and select the Resource (Azure Web App Service) that created in the section above (‘Create the Web App Service using Azure portal’) too. Select the [OK] button after shown the confirm dialog.%改行%図（https://blog.processtune.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/03/DeployAzureAppService.png）%改行%Categories:%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/microsoft-azure/grpc/）リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/microsoft-azure/）リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/%e6%9c%aa%e5%88%86%e9%a1%9e/）%改行%Tags:%改行%No Tag%改行%Post navigation%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/2022/08/18/net-core-options-pattern/）%改行%Post navigation%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/2022/10/30/http-3-and-the-grpc-service-in-net-framework/）%改行%No responses yet%改行%Leave a Replyリンク（https://blog.processtune.com/2022/09/30/deploy-the-grpc-service-to-the-azure-app-service/#respond）%改行%Your email address will not be published.Required fields are marked*%改行%Comment*%改行%Name*%改行%Email*%改行%Website%改行%Save my name, email, and website in this browser for the next time I comment.%改行%© 2025 Processtune Blog. Title: Change JsonConvert to JsonSerializer – Processtune Blog
Categories: .NET Core,Adaptive Cards,Azure Active Directory,Microservice,Microsoft Azure,Office App,SlideShow,Visual Studio,WinUI
BodyText: %改行%リンク（#content）%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/）%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/microsoft-azure/）%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/microsoft-azure/azure-active-directory/）%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/visual-studio/）%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/microservice/）%改行%リンク（#）%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/）%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/microsoft-azure/）%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/microsoft-azure/azure-active-directory/）%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/visual-studio/）%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/microservice/）%改行%Change JsonConvert to JsonSerializer%改行%byリンク（https://blog.processtune.com/author/tetsuro-takao/）%改行%onリンク（https://blog.processtune.com/2020/05/23/）%改行%When using a web API, a program has to implement serialize and deserialize features in many cases.%改行%the ‘Newtonsoft.Json.JsonConvert.Deserialize(_json_)’ and the ‘Newtonsoft.Json.JsonConvert.SerializeObject(_object_)’ is useful so far, and if a program unknowns a json text as type, the ‘Newtonsoft.Json.JsonConvert.Deserialize(_json_)’ and the ‘Newtonsoft.Json.Linq.JArray[“_propertyname_”]’ is useful.%改行%Microsoft provides new docs ‘リンク（https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/dotnet/standard/serialization/system-text-json-migrate-from-newtonsoft-how-to）‘.%改行%Here this article describes how to deserialize JSON text with a type definition using System.Text.Json.JsonSerializer, and also how to deserialize unknown type JSON text. The ‘System.Text.Json.JsonSerializer.Serialize(_object_)’ and the ‘System.Text.Json.JsonDocument.Parse(_json_).RootElement.GetProperty(“_propertyname_”).GetString()’ are mentioned too.%改行%図（https://blog.processtune.com/wp-content/uploads/2020/05/NewtonToSystem.png）%改行%>>リンク（https://github.com/TetsuroTakao/NewtonToSystem）%改行%・Create WinUI App%改行%リンク（https://github.com/TetsuroTakao/NewtonToSystem）is created as the Windows UI 3 application(Hereinafter referred to as WinUI). The WinUI 3 app is described at the Microsoft Docs article ‘リンク（https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/windows/apps/winui/winui3/）‘ as setting the environment to create Windows UI app. And also the reference to the ‘リンク（https://github.com/microsoft/Xaml-Controls-Gallery/tree/winui3preview）‘ is mentioned.%改行%図（https://blog.processtune.com/wp-content/uploads/2020/05/msdocs.png）%改行%I have decided to change the ‘NewtonSoft’ namespace provides serialization features (that I had used a long time), to System namespace at this opportunity.%改行%The WinUI app is created from a template at first.%改行%図（https://blog.processtune.com/wp-content/uploads/2020/05/createproject.png）%改行%The below figure is the structure of the solution(left). And this article describes compared with the ‘Newtonsoft’ namespace and the ‘System’ namespace, thus the code sample defined a reference to the library through the NuGet(rihgt).%改行%図（https://blog.processtune.com/wp-content/uploads/2020/05/solutionstructure.png）図（https://blog.processtune.com/wp-content/uploads/2020/05/nuget.png）%改行%・Create View%改行%At first, it is good to define simple title of the window and put one or two buttons at the view then run the application press F5 key, because the WinUI 3 app is still a preview that has not a design feature for Visual Studio. The figures below define the title of the window.%改行%図（https://blog.processtune.com/wp-content/uploads/2020/05/appxaml.png）図（https://blog.processtune.com/wp-content/uploads/2020/05/appxamlcs.png）%改行%If it is able to launch, continue to design complex layouts at the next.%改行%The text box above will hold something JSON string, and the text box below will generate formatted text of the object that analyzed from JSON string above.%改行%図（https://blog.processtune.com/wp-content/uploads/2020/05/mainwindowxaml.png）%改行%This sample is omitted visual design, actually, the WinUI 3 can implement the Fluent Design using the Fluent Design system of Windows 10. For instance, can apply shadow to the controls, can use modern controls such as the hiding scroll bars(thin bar), and can use the dark mode theme(see Microsoft Build 2020 session ‘リンク（https://mybuild.microsoft.com/sessions/7d5b56db-d367-43ac-9163-44de4e3919e8?source=sessions）11:43 -‘).%改行%図（https://blog.processtune.com/wp-content/uploads/2020/05/appexecute.png）%改行%In the code behind, when the button pressed, the app will acquire the JSON string from web API through the facade. This JSON string will be put into the public property of the facade as the ‘original JSON string’. then in this UI thread, put it to the text property of the text box that means the ‘original JSON string’. As the same, the web API URL also set to the public property of the facade at now, in the future it might be able to set from out of the facade.%改行%The button sends the argument that means which namespaces will be used to deserialize the JSON string. Then acquire a formatted string as a result that will be put to the text property of the text box that means the ‘Formatted string’.%改行%図（https://blog.processtune.com/wp-content/uploads/2020/05/mainwindowxamlcs.png）%改行%On the original JSON string as a sample will acquire from the web API that is provided from an adaptive card template list. It is a good sample because it provides not a standard typed JSON string.%改行%This JSON string contains all templates of the adaptive card, so the first layer elements of the root element have not property name. This kind type of JSON string is impossible to deserialize through prepared types. So this web API provides search features of organization information. Please refer to the Microsoft document ‘リンク（https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/adaptive-cards/templating/service）‘.%改行%{ "cdm": { "templates": [ { "file": "Categories.json", "fullPath": "cdm/Categories.json" } ] }, "finos.org": { "templates": [ { "file": "Bond.json", "fullPath": "finos.org/Bond.json" } ] }, "github.com": { "templates": [ { "file": "issue_webhook.data.json", "fullPath": "github.com/issue_webhook.data.json" }, { "file": "issue_webhook.json", "fullPath": "github.com/issue_webhook.json" } ] }, ... }%改行%・Flow per conditions%改行%In the facade, if the argument means using the ‘Newtonsoft’ namespace, the method deserializes the JSON string using correct namespace(in the left figure). In the case of the ‘System’ namespace, the method throws the part that picked github.com of the JSON string to the method that parses the JSON(in the right figure). Not use the ‘Newtonsoft’ namespace from now, thus it divided from the method using the ‘System’ namespace.%改行%This type of JSON string can not deserialize through prepared type, because the property that wants to except is only one(organization name such as the ‘github.com’ in above JSON string figure) and it can not useリンク（https://docs.microsoft.com/ja-jp/dotnet/api/system.text.json.serialization.jsonextensiondataattribute?view=netcore-3.1）(see Microsoft document ‘リンク（https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/dotnet/standard/serialization/system-text-json-how-to）‘).%改行%In Case 3, It does not use the ‘CardModel’ type but use the ‘templates’ property of the ‘RootElement’ that parse by the ‘JsonDocument’, because as aforementioned, a prepared type can not define using the ‘JsonExtensionData’ attribute.%改行%図（https://blog.processtune.com/wp-content/uploads/2020/05/facadenewton.png）図（https://blog.processtune.com/wp-content/uploads/2020/05/facadesystem.png）%改行%・Convert from the Newtonsoft namespace to the System namespace%改行%The comment ‘Case 1’ of the ‘GetCard’ method is converted to the comment ‘Case 3’ of the ‘GetJsonString’ method at the ‘TextController’ class. These processes are sample code of using prepared type to deserialize an object.%改行%// Case 1. Using the Newtonsoft namespace to parse an object as deserializing through prepared type. var cardTemplates = JsonConvert.DeserializeObject<CardModel>(cards); results.Add("Result of the Case 1."); foreach (var t in cardTemplates.templates) { results.Add(string.Format("file = [{0}], fullPath = [{1}]", t.file, t.fullPath)); } result = string.Join(Environment.NewLine, results); to // Case 3. Using the System namespace to parse an object as deserializing through prepared type. var cards = JsonDocument.Parse(json).RootElement.GetProperty("templates"); var templates = JsonSerializer.Deserialize<List<Template>>(cards.ToString()); result.Add("Result of the Case 3."); foreach (var t in templates) { result.Add(string.Format("file = [{0}], fullPath = [{1}]", t.file, t.fullPath)); }%改行%The type ‘CardModel’ has the ‘templates’ property as a list of the ‘Template’ type. The ‘Template’ type has the ‘file’ property and the ‘fullPath’ property as string type. The name of these properties can define the camel-type because these properties have the ‘JsonPropertyName’ attribute. These attributes have to coincident with the name of the property name of the JSON string.%改行%図（https://blog.processtune.com/wp-content/uploads/2020/05/textcontroller.png）図（https://blog.processtune.com/wp-content/uploads/2020/05/cardmodel.png）%改行%In Case 3, It does not use the ‘CardModel’ type but use the ‘templates’ property of the ‘RootElement’ that parse by the ‘JsonDocument’, because as aforementioned, a prepared type can not define using the ‘JsonExtensionData’ attribute.%改行%On the other hand, instead of using a prepared type, using the ‘JsonElement’ that created by the ‘EnumerateArray’ method of the parsed object.%改行%// Case 2. To parse an object as deserializing that through the 'JObect' of the Newtonsoft namespace. // Use this process if you can't prepare the type of the JSON, or the type is not a complex structure. var templates = JsonConvert.DeserializeObject<JObject>(cards); var templatesArray = templates["templates"].ToArray(); results.Add("Result of the Case 2."); foreach (var t in templatesArray) { results.Add(string.Format("file = [{0}], fullPath = [{1}]", t["file"].ToString(), t["fullPath"].ToString())); } result = string.Join(Environment.NewLine, results); to // Case 4. To parse an object as deserializing that through the 'JsonDocument' of the System namespace. // Use this process if you can't prepare the type of the JSON, or the type is not a complex structure. result.Add("Result of the Case 4."); foreach (var t in cards.EnumerateArray().ToList()) { result.Add(string.Format("file = [{0}], fullPath = [{1}]", t.GetProperty("file").GetString(), t.GetProperty("fullPath").GetString())); } return string.Join(Environment.NewLine,result);%改行%図（https://blog.processtune.com/wp-content/uploads/2020/05/appexecuted.png）%改行%Categories:%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/winui/net-core/）リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/office-app/adaptive-cards/）リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/office-app/）リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/slide-show/）リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/visual-studio/）リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/winui/）%改行%Tags:%改行%No Tag%改行%Post navigation%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/2020/04/20/appsettings-json-in-c-net-core-mvc-web-app/）%改行%Post navigation%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/2020/05/27/create-net-core-library/）%改行%No responses yet%改行%Leave a Replyリンク（https://blog.processtune.com/2020/05/23/change-jsonconvert-to-jsonserializer/#respond）%改行%Your email address will not be published.Required fields are marked*%改行%Comment*%改行%Name*%改行%Email*%改行%Website%改行%Save my name, email, and website in this browser for the next time I comment.%改行%© 2025 Processtune Blog.
