Title: QR code create with Rust language runnable on Azure Functions – Processtune Blog
Categories: Azure Active Directory,Azure Functions,Microservice,Microsoft Azure,Rust,SlideShow,Visual Studio,Visual Studio Code
BodyText: %改行%リンク（#content）%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/）%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/microsoft-azure/）%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/microsoft-azure/azure-active-directory/）%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/visual-studio/）%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/microservice/）%改行%リンク（#）%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/）%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/microsoft-azure/）%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/microsoft-azure/azure-active-directory/）%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/visual-studio/）%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/microservice/）%改行%QR code create with Rust language runnable on Azure Functions%改行%図（https://blog.processtune.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/03/ContainerizationSeriesTitle.png）%改行%byリンク（https://blog.processtune.com/author/tetsuro-takao/）%改行%onリンク（https://blog.processtune.com/2021/07/01/）%改行%Azure Functions is functions runtime that is able to launch a function created by multiple language, as serverless on cloud (see Microsoft Docs [リンク（https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/azure-functions/supported-languages?WT.mc_id=AZ-MVP-4029060）]). What it is serverless means less code, less maintainance of infrastructure, thus developers are able to focus on code for only business process, managers can save on costs as result. Furthermore, when procuring members of some project as using microservices architecture, managers can orchestrate several teams of various language programmers.%改行%In this article, createリンク（https://github.com/TetsuroTakao/QRCodeGenerator）for that will deploy to Azure Functions later.%改行%In the article [リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/?p=5958）] describes creation of assembly to deploy Azure Functions.%改行%In the article [リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/?p=5958）] describes how to modify the simple of this article (simple rust project) to QR code generator.%改行%Added at 9th Feb. 2022 :%改行%Please read Microsoft Docs [リンク（https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/azure-functions/functions-run-local?tabs=v4%2Cwindows%2Ccsharp%2Cportal%2Cbash?WT.mc_id=AZ-MVP-4029060）] and install it, you can execute command [func start] on console (or tarminal of Visual Studio code, Windows and so on.) when restart PC.%改行%>>リンク（https://github.com/TetsuroTakao/QRCodeCreation）%改行%・Azure Functions Visual Studio Code Extensions%改行%This article uses Visual Studio Code and it’s extensions and Rust (seeリンク（https://www.rust-lang.org/tools/install）).%改行%For debugging on local development environment and upload to Azure Functions, it needs Azure Functions Core Tools. This tool installed along with other tools of Azure if install Azure Tools Extension (left figure), or if aim to just install Azure Functions extension, install individual(right figure).%改行%図（https://blog.processtune.com/wp-content/uploads/2022/02/AzureToolsExtension.png）図（https://blog.processtune.com/wp-content/uploads/2022/02/VSCodeExtensions.png）%改行%・Create local Rust project for Azure Functions%改行%It is super easy to create Rust project that run on Windows using Visual Studio Code extension of Azure Functions.%改行%At first, open work folder in Visual Studio Code. Work folder is able to create using the [Open Folder] dialog box. Select the [Open folder…] of the [File] menu, then right click on any directory and select the [New] then select the [Folder] (Left figure).%改行%While open work folder, create Rust project using extension of Azure Functions. Select the [Create New Project…] icon of the [FUNCTIONS] area of the [Azure] extension. (Right figure)%改行%図（https://blog.processtune.com/wp-content/uploads/2022/02/OpenFolderDialog.png）図（https://blog.processtune.com/wp-content/uploads/2022/02/CreateAzureFunctionsProject.png）%改行%[Command Palette] is shown when select [Create New Project…] icon. there are several steps to create new project, exclude step of ‘Function name’ (This setting is used for URL of Functions) and step of ‘Folder of the project’, put values of table below. These values are for sample project.%改行%Folder of the project%改行%QRCodeGeneratorOnAzureFunctions(default : Opened folder from Visual Studio Code. This setting make URL of Azure Functions to figure below)%改行%図（https://blog.processtune.com/wp-content/uploads/2022/02/AzurePortalFunctionsFunction.png）%改行%Language%改行%Custom Handler(default : Last used, If first Azure Functions function creation, select this.)%改行%Project template%改行%Http trigger(default : Last used, If first Azure Functions function creation, select this.)%改行%Function name%改行%BlogSampleFunctions(This setting create URL : https://blogsamplefunctions.azurewebsites.net/api/QRCodeGeneratorOnAzureFunctions)%改行%Authorization%改行%Anonymous(default : no authentication)%改行%図（https://blog.processtune.com/wp-content/uploads/2022/02/FunctionsNewSteps.png）%改行%Return to the [File Explorer], and type [Ctrl] + [Shift] + [`] key for open the [Integrated terminal] (use [Ctrl] + [K] + [S] for comfirming conbination of keys for your keybord type). Then type [ cargo init –name qrcode_generator ] on the [Integrated terminal] to create Rust project package (this name value is not relate a name of Azure Functions). After package creation, type [ cargo run ] to run project. It is OK that it shows [ Hello, world! ] in the [Integrated terminal].%改行%This Rust project package name [qrcode_generator] is same name as Rust assembly file name (and the [exe] extension), so the [cargo run] command create assembly file at [%project root%\target\debug\qrcode_generator.exe] and launch the file. And this value is defined at the [name] value of [package] definition in the [Cargo.toml] file.%改行%[ cargo init ] comand generates rust project files such as the [src/main.rs] file and the [Cargo.toml] file and so on, the [src/main.rs] file is startup file, so [ cargo run ] command executes [ println!(“Hello, world!”) ] statement of the [src/main.rs] file (see figure below).%改行%図（https://blog.processtune.com/wp-content/uploads/2022/02/InitialHelloworld.png）%改行%・Create web server%改行%Create sample projest with modifying [src/main.rs] file that the [ cargo init ] comand generated.%改行%TCP listener is standard function of Rust, although HTTP lisner have to process asyncronous task flow. asyncronous feature is provided with async_std or tokio and so on.%改行%This article select tokio and warp as sample because there is popular and there are a lot of code samples on web. warp is web server framework.%改行%Since warp is for creation of web server, it can listen HTTP request from Azure Functions as HTTP listener. If the setting of Azure Functions environment (host.json at root of project, figure below) to enable forwarding HTTP request, Azure Functions accepts Http request and throw it to the function of Azure Functions that served by warp.%改行%--host.json file (part of the file) ... "customHandler": { "description": { "defaultExecutablePath": "qrcode_generator.exe", "workingDirectory": "", "arguments": [] }, //here is the setting (All figure of host.json is shown later) "enableForwardingHttpRequest": true } }%改行%At first, modify the [Cargo.toml] file as below to use these eco-systems. And define to use them at main.rs of the [src] folder.%改行%--Cargo.toml file [dependencies] warp = "0.3" tokio = { version = "1", features = ["rt", "macros", "rt-multi-thread"] } --src/main.rs file use std::env; use std::collections::HashMap; use std::net::Ipv4Addr; use warp::{http::Response, Filter}; fn main() { println!("Hello, world!"); }%改行%These defines are used as table below.%改行%std::env%改行%Acquire port number that Azure Functions provided.%改行%std::collections::HashMap%改行%Acquire query string of HTTP request.%改行%std::net::Ipv4Addr%改行%Acquire IPv4 address of HTTP request.%改行%warp::{http::Response, Filter}%改行%Create HTTP response, and serve function that defines process of create response.%改行%Next, acquire port number of incomming stream (distination port number of the stream Azure Functions forwards) using definitioned term (FUNCTIONS_CUSTOMHANDLER_PORT).%改行%fn main() { let port: u16 = match env::var("FUNCTIONS_CUSTOMHANDLER_PORT") { Ok(val) => val.parse().expect("Custom Handler port is not a number!"), Err(_) => 3000, }; ... }%改行%Continues, creating respond in case when HTTP GET message listened. Sample code for POST message is described in the article [リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/?p=5958）].%改行%async fn main() { ... let get_source = warp::path!("api" / "QRCodeGeneratorOnAzureFunctions") .and(warp::query::>()) .map(|q:HashMap| match q.get("source") { Some(s) => Response::builder().body(format!("QR Code source [{}]",s)), None => Response::builder().body(String::from("Source does not requested")) }); ... }%改行%ⓘ As additional information%改行%Azure Functions provides global URL as format below (as default).%改行%https://%Function name%.azurewebsites.net/api/%Folder name of project root%%改行%So listening HTTP request use [ warp::path!(“api” / “QRCodeGeneratorOnAzureFunctions”) ] statement. If want to omit path [api], modify host.json as below.%改行%–host.json file (root parameter [extensions], All figure of host.json is shown later)%改行%“extensions”: {“http”: {“routePrefix”: “”}}%改行%At last, create listening part of HTTP GET message. Below is all code of the [src/main.rs] file.%改行%use std::env; use std::collections::HashMap; use std::net::Ipv4Addr; use warp::{http::{Response}, Filter}; #[tokio::main] async fn main() { let port: u16 = match env::var("FUNCTIONS_CUSTOMHANDLER_PORT") { Ok(val) => val.parse().expect("Custom Handler port is not a number!"), Err(_) => 3000, }; let get_source = warp::path!("QRCodeGeneratorOnAzureFunctions") .and(warp::query::>()) .map(|q:HashMap| match q.get("source") { Some(s) => Response::builder().body(format!("QR Code source [{}]",s)), None => Response::builder().body(String::from("Source does not requested")) }); let routes = warp::get().and(get_source); warp::serve(routes).run((Ipv4Addr::UNSPECIFIED, port)).await }%改行%・Run on local%改行%Azure Functions Core Tools has web hosting feature, so build Rust project at first, then define builded assembly path to this tool. Build path is [target/relese] folder that are created when type [cargo build –release] command in the [Integrated terminal]. This tool use execute file at project root folder to host client or upload to Azure Functions. So copy the file from build path to project root.%改行%cp .\target\release\qrcode_generator.exe%改行%This tool hosts the Rust assembly file use [defaultExecutablePath] value in [host.json], the value is defined at description property of customHandler property in the file.%改行%図（https://blog.processtune.com/wp-content/uploads/2022/02/hostJson.png）%改行%ⓘ As additional information (8th Feb. 2022)%改行%リンク（https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/azure-functions/functions-bindings-register#extension-bundles?WT.mc_id=AZ-MVP-4029060）makes Azure Function project enable runable the project of other language that .NET runtime does not support.%改行%Type [ func start ] command in the [Integrated terminal] after put value [qrcode_generator.exe] in the [description] property of the[customHandler] property in this file.%改行%図（https://blog.processtune.com/wp-content/uploads/2022/02/HttpGetRequest.png）%改行%Termination is type [Ctrl] + [C] key at terminal.%改行%Next >>リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/?p=5958）%改行%Categories:%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/microsoft-azure/azure-functions/）リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/microsoft-azure/）リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/microsoft-azure/rust/）リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/slide-show/）リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/visual-studio/）リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/visual-studio/visual-studio-code/）%改行%Tags:%改行%No Tag%改行%Post navigation%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/2021/05/01/should-define-layout-at-first-if-you-choice-atomic-design/）%改行%Post navigation%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/2021/08/01/how-to-compile-rust-assembly-runnable-on-linux-for-deployment-to-azure-functions/）%改行%No responses yet%改行%Leave a Title: Blazor WASM aquires SVG images that will be colored by JavaScript later – Processtune Blog

As addition, the Blazor webassembly acquires some icons from external web app by browser of local.%改行%In next article [リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/?p=6189）], these icons rendered by the Razor template then JavaScript colors them.%改行%>>リンク（https://github.com/TetsuroTakao/SVGIconColoring_en_1）%改行%・Create Blazor Webassembly project%改行%.NET framework provides many templates variety for creation of apps, there are several kind of web apps, native apps and so on. it is also provided for Blazor WebAssembly and it has host type and stand alone type (as default).%改行%The [dotnet new –list] command shows all of templates and the [dotnet new blazorwasm -h] shows parameters of Blazor WebAssembly. So put the command [dotnet new blazorwasm] create base files of Blazor WebAssembly project that is not hosted by Blazor server. Blazor server provides progressive web apps feature.%改行%図（https://blog.processtune.com/wp-content/uploads/2022/03/dotnetNewList.png）%改行%図（https://blog.processtune.com/wp-content/uploads/2022/03/dotnetnewblazorwasmhost.png）%改行%図（https://blog.processtune.com/wp-content/uploads/2022/03/dotnetnewblazorwasm.png）%改行%The directory of created project includes many files. There is the [Program.cs] file as application entrypoint, and the [App.razor] file as routing deefinition.%改行%HTTP request is tranfer from the [App.razor] file to the [Shared\MainLayout.razor] file then the [Shared\MainLayout.razor] makes output that combines the [Shared\NavMenu.razor] file with requested ([/] : as root) page of the [Pages] folder.%改行%The page files of the [Pages] folder hasリンク（https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/aspnet/core/blazor/components/prerendering-and-integration?view=aspnetcore-6.0&pivots=server#use-routable-components-in-a-razor-pages-app?WT.mc_id=DT-MVP-4029060）. It is routing mechanizm of Razor syntax.%改行%@page "/" Index

Hello, world!

Welcome to your new app. %改行%At first as one fifths, put command [dotnet run] in the [Integrated terminal] (put the [ctrl] key + the [@] key for show the [Integrated terminal] : to confirm shortcut key on your keybord type, show shortcut key list using the [ctrl] key + the [k] key + the [s] key) . When the result output after compile, put the [ctrl] key + click the link (http://localhost:5078 for below figure) to launch browser with display index page content.%改行%図（https://blog.processtune.com/wp-content/uploads/2022/03/dotnetrun.png）図（https://blog.processtune.com/wp-content/uploads/2022/03/helloworld.png）%改行%・Acquiring SVG images from external web%改行%As next : two fifths step, create temporaly contents as whole structure at the [Index.razor] file. Create some content in case of that some value is null ([

Loading…

] for below figure), and create some content when some value is not null.%改行%Prepare the [SVGIcon] object for SVG image. Use the [MarkupString] type to a property that is used for output string for HTML. The [image] tag of HTML has the [src] attribute that is able to accept inline SVG.%改行%図（https://blog.processtune.com/wp-content/uploads/2022/03/structuresource.png）図（https://blog.processtune.com/wp-content/uploads/2022/03/structureview.png）%改行%Three fifths step, acquire one svg file from external web site [Icon Fonts], and display it. Modify only in the [@code] statements.%改行%図（https://blog.processtune.com/wp-content/uploads/2022/03/OneSVGSource.png）図（https://blog.processtune.com/wp-content/uploads/2022/03/OneSVGView.png）%改行%⚜ Code%改行%for HTML(same as temporaly contents as whole structure above)%改行%

List of the [Icon Fonts]

Icon made from Icon Fonts is licensed by CC BY 3.0
@if (SVGs.URL == null) {

Loading...

} else {
}%改行%for Code%改行%private SVGIcon SVGs = new SVGIcon(); protected override async Task OnInitializedAsync() { HttpClient httpClient = new HttpClient(); Uri requestURL = new Uri("http://cdn.onlinewebfonts.com/svg/download_155117.svg"); var res = await httpClient.GetStringAsync(requestURL); if (string.IsNullOrEmpty(res)) { @* some code *@ } else { var xml = new System.Xml.XmlDocument(); xml.LoadXml(res); var content = ""; if(xml.LastChild != null){ content = xml.LastChild.OuterXml; } SVGs = new SVGIcon() { URL="http://cdn.onlinewebfonts.com/svg/download_155117.svg", XML= new MarkupString(content) }; } }%改行%[describe]%改行%Use the [OnInitializedAsync] task to run code when HTML on loading.%改行%The step of four fifths, modify the [OnInitializedAsync] that is task to run code when HTML on loading. Change feature that acquires one SVG file to acquire ten SVG files as sampling. So change the [SVGs] variable from single object to the List objects.%改行%private SVGIcon SVGs = new SVGIcon(); to private List SVGs = new List();%改行%Since change the [SVGs] variable to the List objects, HTML part also change to render List objects.%改行%@if (SVGs.URL == null) {

Loading...

} else {
} to @if (SVGs.FirstOrDefault() == null) {

Loading...

} else { @foreach (var svg in SVGs) {
} } [describe] The [SVGs.FirstOrDefault()] statement is return null when the [SVGs] variable items count is zero, on otherhand the [SVGs.First()] statement return the [InvalidOperationException] type exception. If create exception handler that corresponds all exceptions, it is better use this [System.Linq.Enumerable.First Method].%改行%>>リンク（https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/dotnet/api/system.linq.enumerable.first?view=net-6.0?WT.mc_id=DT-MVP-4029060）%改行%図（https://blog.processtune.com/wp-content/uploads/2022/03/ListingSVGsCode.png）図（https://blog.processtune.com/wp-content/uploads/2022/03/ListingSVGsView.png）%改行%・Call JavaScript from Blazor WebAssembly%改行%Five fifths step is modifing the [wwwroot/index.html] file because JavaScript has to define in HTML file. However, this contents is vavigated by the [Shared/MainLayout.razor] file and includes the [Pages/Index.razor] component. So add the [SignIn] part on the [Shared/MainLayout.razor] file as simple procedures of calling JavaScript from Blazor. The Blazor method [SignIn()] is called use injected razor syntax of HTML [A] tag (@SignedText), then the Blazor method [SignIn()] calls the JavaSript method [SetSignInText] as implimentation of the [リンク（https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/dotnet/api/microsoft.jsinterop.ijsruntime?view=aspnetcore-6.0?WT.mc_id=DT-MVP-4029060）] Interface.%改行%The below code is whole of the [Shared/MainLayout.razor] file.%改行%@inherits LayoutComponentBase @inject IJSRuntime JS
@SignedText | About
@Body
@code{ public MarkupString SignedText { get; set; } = (MarkupString)"SignIn"; public async Task SignIn() { SignedText = new (await JS.InvokeAsync("SetSignInText")); } } [describe] It's important that define the [@inject IJSRuntime JS] statement at head part of the [Shared/MainLayout.razor] file. As addition, implementation of the [IJSRuntime] interface is asyncronise, so the task define as async and await calling the [SetSignInText] JavaScript method. The [IJSRuntime] interface has also the [InvokeVoidAsync] method, thus if JavaSctipt returns some value, use the [InvokeAsync] method. These methods create an instance of ValueTask value type that is able to convert to Task reference type. So it is good that await a method returns value type, then construct new the MarkupString using it.%改行%ⓘ As additional information%改行%If theリンク（https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/dotnet/api/system.threading.tasks.valuetask-1?view=net-6.0?WT.mc_id=DT-MVP-4029060）value type is awaited, the instanse of Task reference type is executed, it is onetime. This mechanism effects to decleasing number of instance allocating times.%改行%A reference type needs explicit instancing, and await end of instancing, furthermore, await end of processing that throw value of the processing to Blazor object. Long time and external processting of reference type task as await ansyncronously needs instancing a struct that has multiple fields.%改行%The [SetSignInText] JavaScript has to defines as HTML, so it is good which on the [Shared/MainLayout.razor] file or on the [wwwroot/index.html] file (excludes the [Pages/Index.razor] file). If the reason is that it is share function for all pages, so define on the [Shared/MainLayout.razor] file. Or the reason is that want to bunch JavaScript functions at head of HTML file, define on the [Pages/Index.razor] file.%改行%In this article, JavaScript functions are defined on the [wwwroot/index.html] file.%改行% [describe] Though this code sample is dummy code of JavaScript that it does not login really, it is able to confirm that Blazor calls JavaScript method real. And can confirm that Blazor can await JavaScript method and acquire string that JavaScript object take and transfer.%改行%図（https://blog.processtune.com/wp-content/uploads/2022/03/SigninCode.png）図（https://blog.processtune.com/wp-content/uploads/2022/03/SigninView.png）%改行%ⓘ As additional information%改行%If want to save the project until so far of this article to GitHub temporarily, create gitignore file use .NET template, and initialise git repository use the [Integrated terminal] of Visual Studio Code.%改行%Put [dotnet new gitignore] command in the [Integrated terminal] to create gitignore file, and Put [git init] command to initialise git repository.%改行%図（https://blog.processtune.com/wp-content/uploads/2022/03/dotnetgitignore.png）%改行%Categories:%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/winui/net-core/）リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/asp-net-core/）リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/asp-net-core/javascript/）リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/slide-show/）リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/visual-studio/）リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/visual-studio/visual-studio-code/）%改行%Tags:%改行%No Tag%改行%Post navigation%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/2021/09/01/rust-qr-code-generator-app-of-azure-functions/）%改行%Post navigation%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/2021/11/01/javascript-colors-svg-images-that-are-acquired-by-blazor-webassembly/）%改行%No responses yet%改行%Leave a Replyリンク（https://blog.processtune.com/2021/10/01/blazor-wasm-aquires-svg-images-that-will-be-colored-by-javascript-later/#respond）%改行%Your email address will not be published.Required fields are marked*%改行%Comment*%改行%Name*%改行%Email*%改行%Website%改行%Save my name, email, and website in this browser for the next time I comment.%改行%© 2025 Processtune Blog. It is a useful mechanism as substitute a ComboBox control. Because some processings are able to run before an item is added to a ListView control.%改行%I want to use it many times, so make the mechanism to generic reusable composite. This article describe how to create reusable Flyout control composite.%改行%リンク（http://1drv.ms/1BhSxwH）%改行%・Outline%改行%Basic mechanism is same as the sample code ofリンク（http://www.processtune.com/blog/?p=1661）. A difference about XAML is a Name property of the controls set nothing for it would be reused many times.%改行%図（http://processtuneblog.azurewebsites.net/wp-content/uploads/2015/05/XAMLArtistList.png）図（http://processtuneblog.azurewebsites.net/wp-content/uploads/2015/05/XAMLGenreList.png）%改行%図（http://processtuneblog.azurewebsites.net/wp-content/uploads/2015/05/XAMLSongList.png）%改行%The side of a view model, a Flyout control is showed from a FlyoutBase in order to divide from individual XAML controls. though a button control in the Flyout control should close the Flyout that includes self.%改行%図（http://processtuneblog.azurewebsites.net/wp-content/uploads/2015/05/OverView.png）%改行%To do it, use a Command property and a CommandParameter property of a Button control. The value bind to the Command property of a Button control should extend by the ICommand interface. An Execute method of it take an argument represented by the CommandParameter property of the Button control. When the CommandParameter is set a RelativeSource with the “Mode=Self” condition, it transfer the button itself as the argument of the method.%改行%Two ways following is how to close a Flyout control from a button which the Flyout control includes.%改行%1. When a Button control show a Flyout control, the Button control set itself to the private variable of a view model. When the Button control close the Flyout control, the Button control use the variable to determines the Flyout control.%改行%2. The button close the flyout aquire the flyout from the VisualTreeHelper class.%改行%The sample code of this article is includes the first way.%改行%図（http://processtuneblog.azurewebsites.net/wp-content/uploads/2015/05/MechanismOfViewModel.png）%改行%These two method are below.%改行%1. The sample solution includes.%改行%XAML%改行%<StackPanelDataContext=”{BindingGenreDataContext}”Grid.Row=”1″Grid.Column=”1″Margin=”10,10,0,0″ >%改行%<ListViewBorderBrush=”White”BorderThickness=”1″Header=”Genre List”ItemsSource=”{BindingGenericListViewItemsSource}”Height=”600″>%改行%<ListView.ItemTemplate>%改行%<DataTemplate>%改行%<StackPanelOrientation=”Horizontal”>%改行%<TextBlockText=”{BindingFirstItemText}”/>%改行%</StackPanel>%改行%</DataTemplate>%改行%</ListView.ItemTemplate>%改行%</ListView>%改行%<ButtonContent=”Enter if it is not in the list”HorizontalAlignment=”Center”Command=”{BindingShowFlyoutButtonCommand}”CommandParameter=”{BindingRelativeSource={RelativeSourceMode=Self}}”Margin=”0,10,0,0″>%改行%<FlyoutBase.AttachedFlyout>%改行%<Flyout>%改行%<StackPanel>%改行%<StackPanelOrientation=”Horizontal”>%改行%<TextBoxHeader=”{BindingFirstTextBoxHeader}”Text=”{BindingFirstTextBoxText,Mode=TwoWay,UpdateSourceTrigger=PropertyChanged}”Width=”300″/>%改行%</StackPanel>%改行%<ButtonContent=”O K”HorizontalAlignment=”Right”Margin=”0,10,0,0″Command=”{BindingCloseFlyoutButtonCommand}”/>%改行%</StackPanel>%改行%</Flyout>%改行%</FlyoutBase.AttachedFlyout>%改行%</Button>%改行%</StackPanel>%改行%View model( This code is a part of the view model that binds to the Command property of the Button control.)%改行%ButtoncurrentButton {get;set; Title: .NET Core Options pattern – Processtune Blog
Categories: .NET Core,ASP.NET Core,Azure Active Directory,C#,Microservice,Microsoft Azure,SlideShow,Visual Studio,Visual Studio Code,WPF App,未分類
BodyText: %改行%リンク（#content）%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/）%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/microsoft-azure/）%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/microsoft-azure/azure-active-directory/）%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/visual-studio/）%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/microservice/）%改行%リンク（#）%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/）%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/microsoft-azure/）%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/microsoft-azure/azure-active-directory/）%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/visual-studio/）%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/microservice/）%改行%.NET Core Options pattern%改行%図（https://blog.processtune.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/03/OptionsPatternSeriesTitle.png）%改行%byリンク（https://blog.processtune.com/author/tetsuro-takao/）%改行%onリンク（https://blog.processtune.com/2022/08/18/）%改行%The Options Pattern can build a hierarchical settings values structure. In the previous article [リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/?p=6364）], a settings values of The .NET Generic Host that created by the host builder were registered to the host as a service as it is, and were used in the UI layer although, the Options Pattern in .NET Core must be applied the Options Pattern as the configuration service before registered to the host.%改行%In the WPF application, customizing startup flow at the App.xaml.cs can apply the Options pattern to the service before the MainWindow showed.%改行%>>リンク（https://github.com/TetsuroTakao/GenericHostWPF）%改行%・Development steps for .NET Core Options Pattern%改行%[Step 1] The .NET Generic Host built with the host builder can be used for both web applications and desktop native applications, so settings values as configuration service that applies the Options Pattern is able to use on each screen with using the Generic Host that define in the constructor of the App class.%改行%図（https://blog.processtune.com/wp-content/uploads/2022/08/developmentSteps.png）%改行%[Step 2] On each screen, the configuration service that the Options Pattern is applied is able to use in the constructor of the screen. This mechanism is the same as when passing the settings values as configuration service that is applied the Options Pattern to the constructor of the controller in the ASP.NET Core Options Pattern, so this article means also recap of the ASP.NET Core Options Pattern.%改行%The App class has to service the configuration service with the Options Pattern applied before calling the MainWindow class, so stop calling the MainWindow.xaml in the App.xaml and call MainWindow after building the configuration service in the App.xaml.cs will do so.%改行%[Step 3] Define a startup method name to the Startup attribute in App.xaml, and create the Startup method in App.xaml.cs, then show the MainWindow screen in the Startup method. The MainWindow screen is registered as a service when the host is constructed, and the MainWindow service is displayed in the Startup method.%改行%This article explains this flow.%改行%・ASP.NET Core Options Pattern recap%改行%In the case of Program.cs of ASP.NET Core, the settings values of the JSON file is able to specify as configurations of the host. So it can register to the Generic Host as the configuration service directly. In the case of WPF, this creation of host builder to read JSON file part is a little troublesome.%改行%図（https://blog.processtune.com/wp-content/uploads/2022/08/asp.netCoreOptionsPatternRecap.png）%改行%First, for the JSON that was automatically loaded by the Generic Host in ASP.NET Core must be loaded explicitly, since files other than JSON can also be handled, please refer to the official document [リンク（https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/dotnet/core/extensions/configuration-providers?WT.mc_id=DT-MVP-4029060）] also.%改行%Another thing is that the application startup flow is customized and insert the service registration process before show the MainWindow. Let’s go into a little more detail.%改行%The Generic Host in ASP.NET Core was defined in Program.cs, on the other hand, in the case of the WFP application is defined in App.xaml.cs.%改行%There are two steps to registering the Generic Host configuration as a service.%改行%Use the ‘Microsoft.Extensions.Hosting’ namespace, loading a JSON file into the Generic Host configuration using the ‘ConfigureAppConfiguration’ method of the Generic Host is the one, and another, registering the Generic Host configuration as a service using ‘ConfigureServices’ method of the Generic Host.%改行%As for the flow of processing, use the ‘Microsoft.Extensions.Hosting’ namespace, clear the source of the configuration of the host obtained by the ‘ConfigureAppConfiguration’ method of the Generic Host, and load the appsettings.json file.%改行%the ‘optional’ argument is a flag for appsettings.json reading, and if ‘true’ is specified, it will work even if there is no file.%改行%the ‘reloadOnChange’ argument is ‘true’ and reloads appsettings.json when it changes.%改行%The configuration constructed in this way can be obtained with the ‘Configuration’ property of the ‘context’ argument in the ConfigureServices’ method of the host, so register it as a configuration service to the ‘services’ argument that was also obtained.%改行%Use the ‘Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection’ namespace, the Options Pattern is able to use while the type specification of the ‘Configure’ method of the service of the host that can be received by the ‘ConfigureServices’ method of the host. This mechanism is the ‘IOptions’ interface of the ‘TOptions’ type of the configuration service provides Dependency Injection feature to insert customised type into the configuration service. In the time, specifying the type of the ApplicationContext class that has the same structure as the JSON file.%改行%App.xaml.cs public partial class App : Application { private IHost _host; public App() { _host = Host.CreateDefaultBuilder() .ConfigureAppConfiguration((hostingContext, configuration) => { configuration.Sources.Clear(); IHostEnvironment env = hostingContext.HostingEnvironment; configuration .AddJsonFile("appsettings.json", optional: true, reloadOnChange: true) .AddJsonFile($"appsettings.{env.EnvironmentName}.json", true, true); }) .ConfigureServices((context, services) => { services.Configure<ApplicationContext>(context.Configuration); services.AddSingleton<MainWindow>(); // services.AddTransient<MainWindow>(); }) .Build(); }%改行%ⓘ Application lyfecycle%改行%The ‘AddSingleton’ method of services of the Generic Host and the ‘AddTransient’ method( commented) controls lyfecycle of the service.%改行%リンク（https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/aspnet/core/fundamentals/dependency-injection?WT.mc_id=DT-MVP-4029060&view=aspnetcore-6.0#lifetime-and-registration-options）introduces how to validate ‘AddTransient’, ‘AddScoped’, and ‘AddSingleton’ method with an operation ID attached to the service instance.%改行%The ‘AddTransient’ method adds a unique instance per session, the ‘AddScoped’ method adds a per-request instance, and the ‘AddSingleton’ method adds an application-unique service instance to the application host.%改行%In this time, the ‘MainWindow’ service uses an instance unique to the application, but the data context and configuration service described later have a strong dependency on MainWindow, so use AddTransient when serving multiple users.%改行%・Customize startup flow%改行%The flow up to MainWindow display can be customized by defining App.xaml and App.xaml.cs.%改行%In App.xaml, remove the ‘StartupUri’ attribute and specify the method name that operates at startup to the ‘Startup’ attriute. In this example, it is ‘Application_Startup’, so the ‘Application_Startup’ method create in the ‘App.xaml.cs’ file later. The ‘Application_Exit’ method as the application exit method at the time of termination also create in the ‘App.xaml.cs’ file.%改行%Next, in order to display the ‘MainWindow’ screen in the ‘Application_Startup’ method of App.xaml.cs, register the ‘MainWindow’ class as the host service in advance.%改行%The ‘MainWindow’ class registered as a service in the host can be obtained as one of the services of host that are holded in the ‘_host’ variable of the App class global scope in App.xaml.cs.%改行%The “GetRequiredService” method returns InvalidOperationException (if there is no service) or ObjectDisposedException (if the service has been destroyed), unlike “GetService” which returns null in an exception, so support flow can set in subsequent processing. Used when such operations are expected. At that time, it is the scope of the service that must be considered that described above (refer to [Application lyfecycle]).%改行%In the ‘Application_Startup’ method, the MainWindow screen is displayed by the “Show” method of the service that is obtained from the ‘GetRequiredService’ method of the ‘_host’ variable.%改行%App.xaml.cs private async void Application_Startup(object sender, StartupEventArgs e) { await _host.StartAsync(); _host.Services.GetRequiredService().Show(); } private async void Application_Exit(object sender, ExitEventArgs e) { await _host.StopAsync(TimeSpan.FromSeconds(5)); _host.Dispose(); }%改行%・Use settings values as the Options Pattern%改行%To confirm startup flow, start Visual Studio Code in any folder and display the terminal with CTRL + @.%改行%Create a project with ‘dotnet new wpf’ command in the terminal.%改行%To install each extension of Microsoft.Extensions.Hosting, Microsoft.Extensions.Configuration and Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection from Nuget, copy .NET CLI command below and paste it into the terminal.%改行%Microsoft.Extensions.Hosting%改行%https://www.nuget.org/packages/Microsoft.Extensions.Hosting/%改行%Microsoft.Extensions.Configuration%改行%https://www.nuget.org/packages/Microsoft.Extensions.Configuration/%改行%Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection%改行%https://www.nuget.org/packages/Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection/%改行%The setting values ​​can use in all of methods of MainWindow.xaml.cs, because the configuration service of the host is stored into the variable of the global scope of the MainWindow class at the constructor of the class in this sample. Although, for convenience of explanation, every statement is defined in the constructor.%改行%Thus that it can be used later for clicks, mouseovers, select list selections, etc. if it needs.%改行%In this article, we want to complete everything in the constructor, so binding using INotifyPropertyChanged is performed, but before explaining the ViewModel, explains the structure of using service of the host first.%改行%Set the data context in MainWindow.xaml. Here we are using a class named ‘MainWindowViewModel’. Since the data context specification on the xaml side is set to Window, the property is bound as it is. Application works without the attribute “UpdateSourceTrigger=PropertyChanged”. If the data context inherits from INotifyPropertyChanged, the PropertyChangedEventHandler’s delegate is executed by default.%改行%MainWindow.xaml <Window x:Class="GenericHostWPF.MainWindow" xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/winfx/2006/xaml/presentation" xmlns:x="http://schemas.microsoft.com/winfx/2006/xaml" xmlns:d="http://schemas.microsoft.com/expression/blend/2008" xmlns:mc="http://schemas.openxmlformats.org/markup-compatibility/2006" xmlns:local="clr-namespace:GenericHostWPF" mc:Ignorable="d" Title="MainWindow" Height="450" Width="800"> <Window.DataContext> <local:MainWindowViewModel /> </Window.DataContext> <Grid> <TextBlock x:Name="Message" Margin="10,10,500,0" TextWrapping="Wrap" Text="{Binding Message, UpdateSourceTrigger=PropertyChanged}" VerticalAlignment="Top"/> <TextBox x:Name="MessageArea" HorizontalAlignment="Left" Height="312" Margin="12,100,12,12" TextWrapping="Wrap" Text="{Binding Log}" VerticalAlignment="Top" Width="773" Background="Black" Foreground="White"/> </Grid> </Window>%改行%Store this in the global scope variable ‘datacontext’ of the class in the constructor of MainWindow.xaml.cs and put the setting value into the property of this view model.%改行%For the setting value, bind the Japanese section of the ‘config’ argument obtained in the constructor to the global scope variable ‘applicationContext’ so that it can be used in other methods.%改行%The ‘applicationContext’ variable provides layered properties using the property ‘Brand’ or the ‘Title’ property of each ‘Page’ object and so on.%改行%MainWindow.xaml.cs public partial class MainWindow : Window { private MainWindowViewModel dataContext = new (); private ApplicationContext applicationContext = new(); private IConfiguration configRoot; public MainWindow(IConfiguration config) { configRoot = config; InitializeComponent(); config.GetSection(ApplicationContext.English).Bind(applicationContext); dataContext = (MainWindowViewModel)this.DataContext; dataContext.Message = applicationContext.Brand; dataContext.Log = applicationContext.Pages.First().Title; } }%改行%ⓘ INotification in a ViewModel%改行%図（https://blog.processtune.com/wp-content/uploads/2022/08/viewmodel.png）%改行%On the ViewModel side, extend INotifyPropertyChanged to declare a delegate for PropertyChangedEventHandler and define the delegate’s methods. Since the ‘CallerMemberName’ attribute is used in the method, the ‘System.Runtime.CompilerServices’ namespace is used.%改行%Each property stores a value in a private variable and takes it out. If the value is the same, do nothing and call the delegate’s method if there is a change.%改行%Categories:%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/winui/net-core/）リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/asp-net-core/）リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/c/）リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/slide-show/）リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/visual-studio/visual-studio-code/）リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/winui/wpf-app/）リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/%e6%9c%aa%e5%88%86%e9%a1%9e/）%改行%Tags:%改行%No Tag%改行%Post navigation%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/2022/07/23/encapsulation-of-information-affects-an-app-flexibility-asp-net-core-options-pattern/）%改行%Post navigation%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/2022/09/30/deploy-the-grpc-service-to-the-azure-app-service/）%改行%No responses yet%改行%Leave a Replyリンク（https://blog.processtune.com/2022/08/18/net-core-options-pattern/#respond）%改行%Your email address will not be published.Required fields are marked*%改行%Comment*%改行%Name*%改行%Email*%改行%Website%改行%Save my name, email, and website in this browser for the next time I comment.%改行%© 2025 Processtune Blog. Title: Encapsulation of information affects an app flexibility – ASP.NET Core Options Pattern – Processtune Blog
Categories: ASP.NET Core,Azure Active Directory,C#,Microservice,Microsoft Azure,Service,SlideShow,Visual Studio,Visual Studio Code
BodyText:
%改行%リンク（#content）%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/）%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/microsoft-azure/）%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/microsoft-azure/azure-active-directory/）%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/visual-studio/）%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/microservice/）%改行%リンク（#）%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/）%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/microsoft-azure/）%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/microsoft-azure/azure-active-directory/）%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/visual-studio/）%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/microservice/）%改行%Encapsulation of information affects an app flexibility – ASP.NET Core Options Pattern%改行%図（https://blog.processtune.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/03/OptionsPatternSeriesTitle.png）%改行%byリンク（https://blog.processtune.com/author/tetsuro-takao/）%改行%onリンク（https://blog.processtune.com/2022/07/23/）%改行%Previous article [リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/?p=6364）] describes overview ofリンク（https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/aspnet/core/fundamentals/configuration/options?WT.mc_id=DT-MVP-4029060&view=aspnetcore-6.0）that enables information encapsulation and separation of business logic interest. This feature makes that an application extends some features without affecting existing features.%改行%This article using previous sample code to describe a process of extending feature withリンク（https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/aspnet/core/fundamentals/configuration/options?WT.mc_id=DT-MVP-4029060&view=aspnetcore-6.0）.%改行%Please select the link [リンク（https://github.com/TetsuroTakao/GlobalizationUseSettings）] below to download it to refer to code of this article.%改行%>>リンク（https://github.com/TetsuroTakao/GlobalizationUseSettings）%改行%>>リンク（https://github.com/TetsuroTakao/GlobalizationUseSettings3）%改行%• Preparation of this article%改行%In previous article, did not describe steps of implementation, but describe overview of process of development. So, this article describes extending application using the Options Pattern with steps of implementation.%改行%The sample of implementation uses the ‘appsettings.json’ file as a settings that includes two sections of languages the ‘English’ section and the ‘Japanese’ section create screens of an application. This article describes that adding an item of the sections of settings does not affect to another section of the settings.%改行%As concretely, a navigation menu of sample application is created from the settings. Even if adding menu item of the navigation menu in the English screen does not affect to the Japanese screen.%改行%As the screen that navigate from the ‘Index’ screen, this sample includes the ‘Search’ screen made from the ‘Search.cshtml’ file and the ‘Search.cshtml.cs’ file of the ‘Pages’ folder.%改行%Implementation steps are1.Create .NET ‘webapp’ project,2.Prepare settings JSON,3.Prepare a class that has same structure as settings JSON to use the Options Pattern,4.Add configuration service to the host usingリンク（https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/dotnet/core/extensions/generic-host?WT.mc_id=DT-MVP-4029060）, the service has same structure as settings JSON, because the Options Pattern creates a service that uses the class that made at step [3] asリンク（https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/dotnet/core/extensions/dependency-injection?WT.mc_id=DT-MVP-4029060）,5.Use the settings values through the service at each controller (or non-MVC Razor pages).%改行%• Create .NET ‘webapp’ project - Step 1 >Start Visual Studio Code ([code .] at any directory to create the project in Windows terminal) >Open the terminal window in Visual Studio Code (Press [CTRL + @] in terminal pain) >Create new 'webapp' project(Put [dotnet new webapp] in terminal pain) • Prepare settings JSON - Step 2 >Copy from the Sample project, or type below in appsettings.json of the project [%project root directory%\appsettings.json] { "Japanese": { "Brand": "管理画面", "Pages": [ { "ActionKey": "Index", "Title": "ホーム" }, { "ActionKey": "Privacy", "Title": "個人情報" } ] }, "English": { "Brand": "Management Console", "Pages": [ { "ActionKey": "Index", "Title": "Home" }, { "ActionKey": "Privacy", "Title": "Privacy" } ] } }%改行%• A class that has same structure as settings JSON – Step 3%改行%The ‘appsettings.json’ file has the ‘Japanese’ section and the ‘English’ section, the Options Pattern represents it as constant string. The ‘ApplicationContext’ class below provides two objects that identifyed using name ‘English’ and ‘Japanese’, each object has the ‘Brand’ property, and the ‘Pages’ property that is collection of the ‘Page’ object which has the ‘ActionKey ‘ property and the ‘Title’ property.%改行%This procedure for specification is the Options Pattern specific that reduces same statement of the class to define same structure. Please compare the ‘appsettings.json’ above and the ‘ApplicationContext’ class below.%改行%[%project root directory%\Models\ApplicationContext.cs] public class ApplicationContext { public const string Japanese = "Japanese"; public const string English = "English"; public string Brand{ get; set; } = String.Empty; public ListPages{ get; set; } = new List(); } public class Page { public string ActionKey { get; set; } = String.Empty; public string Title { get; set; } = String.Empty; }%改行%The ‘ApplicationContext’ class as the ‘ApplicationContext.cs’ file create in the ‘Models’ folder that create new at the project.%改行%図（https://blog.processtune.com/wp-content/uploads/2022/08/ImplementationStep1.png）%改行%• Add configuration service to the host – Step 4%改行%In the ‘Program.cs’ file of the project root, adds statement ‘builder.Services.Configure(builder.Configuration.GetSection(“Japanese”));’ at before the statement ‘var app = builder.Build();’. This statement provides a service that is structed as the ‘ApplicationContext’ type, includes settings value.%改行%[%project root directory%\Program.cs] var builder = WebApplication.CreateBuilder(args); builder.Services.AddRazorPages(); // Add this code builder.Services.Configure(builder.Configuration); // Only this one code var app = builder.Build(); if (!app.Environment.IsDevelopment())... ... app.Run();%改行%• Use the settings values at each controller (or non MVC razor pages) – Step 5%改行%Each method of a Controller if MVC, or OnGet method of the Razor pages if it’s not MVC could provides settings values to client UI.%改行%Sample is non MVC Razor page, so move to Pages directory using [cd pages] command in the ‘terminal’ pain and type [dotnet new pages -na %namespace%] to add a page in Pages folder of the project, make its page able to navigate from Index in follow step. Replace the part [%namespace%] to the namespace of development environment that can copy from the ‘Index.cshtml.cs’.%改行%Modify a name of the model ‘PagesModel’ to ‘SearchModel’ in the ‘Pages.cshtml’ file, and file name to ‘Search.cshtml’. And modify class name ‘PagesModel’ to ‘SearchModel’ in the ‘Pages.cshtml.cs’ and file name to ‘Search.cshtml.cs'(refer to the figure below left).%改行%Next step is modifying ‘Index’ screen, because of this web app starts from the ‘Index’ screen.%改行%Put configuration service into class-scoped variable at class constructor of the ‘Index.cshtml.cs’, to use in OnGet method. Put settings values to ViewData at OnGet method using class-scoped variable (refer to the figure below right).%改行%図（https://blog.processtune.com/wp-content/uploads/2022/08/ImplementationStep2.png）図（https://blog.processtune.com/wp-content/uploads/2022/08/ImplementationStep3.png）%改行%The ‘GetSection’ method of configuration service provides settings value of specific section (this sample is ‘English’ section) to bind to class-scoped variable that structed as same with the ‘appsettings.json’. Then put settings values in ViewData using a type, in OnGet method. Thus, this coding style is very effectively.%改行%[%project root directory%\Pages\Index.cshtml.cs] public SearchModel(ILoggerlogger, IConfiguration configuration) { Context = new ApplicationContext(); configuration.GetSection(ApplicationContext.English).Bind(Context); // Use below code if you want show Japanese settings values. // configuration.GetSection(ApplicationContext.Japanese).Bind(Context); } public void OnGet() { ViewData["Brand"] = Context.Brand; ViewData["Pages"] = Context.Pages; var current = Context.Pages.Where(p => p.ActionKey == "Index") .FirstOrDefault(); if(current != null){ ViewData["Title"] = current.Title; } } [%project root directory%\Pages\Index.cshtml]

@ViewData["Brand"]!

%改行%図（https://blog.processtune.com/wp-content/uploads/2022/08/ImplementationStep4.png）%改行%• Navigate from Index to Search%改行%Definition in the ‘Index.cshtml.cs’ file is lost affect when navigate to the ‘Search’ screen, thus definition like this has to set to global scope of application. For it, create new file ‘PageBase.cs’ at the ‘Shared’ folder of the ‘Pages’ folder of project root, then copy all code of the ‘Index.cshtml.cs’ file to it. After it, modify as below code. Let literal code ‘Index’ that represents current screen as it is, only modify ‘IndexModel’ to ‘PageBaseModel’ here.%改行%[%project root directory%\Pages\Shared\PageBase.cs] using Microsoft.AspNetCore.Mvc; using Microsoft.AspNetCore.Mvc.RazorPages; namespace GlobalizationUseSettings3.Pages { // public class IndexModel:PageModel public class PageBaseModel:PageModel { private readonly ILogger_logger; private readonly ApplicationContext Context; // public IndexModel(ILogger logger, IConfiguration configuration, string language) public PageBaseModel(ILogger logger, IConfiguration configuration) { _logger = logger; Context = new ApplicationContext(); configuration.GetSection(language).Bind(Context); } public void OnGet() { ViewData["Brand"] = Context.Brand; ViewData["Pages"] = Context.Pages; var current = Context.Pages.Where(p => p.ActionKey == "Index").FirstOrDefault(); if(current != null){ ViewData["Title"] = current.Title; } } } }%改行%In the ‘Index.cshtml.cs’ file, modify the ‘IndexModel’ class that is extend of the ‘PageModel’ class to extend of the ‘PageBaseModel’ class as below code. And let’s change the screen language to Japanese in this time.%改行%[%project root directory%\Pages\Index.cshtml.cs] using Microsoft.AspNetCore.Mvc; using Microsoft.AspNetCore.Mvc.RazorPages; namespace GlobalizationUseSettings3.Pages; // public class IndexModel : PageModel public class IndexModel : PageBaseModel { // private readonly ILogger_logger; // private readonly ApplicationContext Context; // public IndexModel(ILoggerlogger, IConfiguration configuration) public IndexModel(ILoggerlogger, IConfiguration configuration) : base(logger, configuration, ApplicationContext.Japanese) { // _logger = logger; // Context = new ApplicationContext(); // configuration.GetSection(ApplicationContext.English).Bind(Context); } // public void OnGet() // { // ViewData["Brand"] = Context.Brand; // ViewData["Pages"] = Context.Pages; // var current = Context.Pages.Where(p => p.ActionKey == "Index").FirstOrDefault(); // if(current != null){ // ViewData["Title"] = current.Title; // } // } }%改行%図（https://blog.processtune.com/wp-content/uploads/2022/08/ImplementationStep5.png）%改行%Next step is control of the current page title and adding navigation menu item for the ‘Search’ screen.%改行%To obtain current screen, get the ‘HttpContext’ object of HTTP request and get values of the ‘RouteData’ object of the ‘HttpContext’ object. This ‘RouteData’ object includes URL address string as relatively of current screen. On the other hands, strings for title of screens set in the ‘appsettings.json’ file is mapped to the ‘ApplicationContext’ class. Thus the ‘Title’ property of the ‘Page’ object use for current screen is able to identify in the ‘Pages’ collection of the ‘ApplicationContext’ class if acquired URL address string match to the ‘ActionKey’ property of the ‘Page’ object. Delete ‘/’ string to compare acquired URL address string with the ‘ActionKey’ property of the ‘Page’ object use the ‘RegularExpression’ object.%改行%Modify the ‘OnGet()’ method of the ‘PageBase.cs’ file like as below code.%改行%[%project root directory%\Pages\Shared\PageBase.cs] using System.Text.RegularExpressions; ... public void OnGet() { ViewData["Brand"] = Context.Brand; ViewData["Pages"] = Context.Pages; // var current = Context.Pages.Where(p => p.ActionKey == "Index").FirstOrDefault(); // if(current != null){ // ViewData["Title"] = current.Title; // } var p = Request.HttpContext.GetRouteData()!.Values["Page"]!.ToString(); var rx = new Regex(@"\w.*",RegexOptions.Compiled | RegexOptions.IgnoreCase); var m = rx.Matches(p!).FirstOrDefault()!.Value; ViewData["Title"] = Context.Pages.Where(p => p.ActionKey == m).FirstOrDefault()!.Title; }%改行%Last step of navigate to the ‘Search’ scree is Modify the ‘_Layout.cshtml’ file to create navigation menu item for ‘Search’ screen.%改行%Like as a title of current screen, This menu item is also created from the ‘Page’ object defined in the ‘ApplicationContext’ class. The ‘Page’ class has the ‘ActionKey’ property and the ‘Title’ property, use this ‘ActionKey’, set at the ‘asp-page-handler’ attribute of HTML anchor tag as the URI of the ‘Search’ screen.%改行%@item.Title%改行%Settings values set to the ‘Pages’ of ViewData in constructor of the ‘PageBase’ class are collection of a pair the ‘ActionKey’ property and the ‘Title’ property. Thus, modify the ‘_Layout.cshtml’ file as below code creates list items of HTML unordered list tag.%改行%
    @*
  • Home
  • Privacy
    • *@ @foreach (var item in pages!) {
  • @if(ViewData["Title"]!.ToString()==item.Title) { @item.Title } else{ @item.Title }
    • }
%改行%In this mechanism, modifying the ‘appsettings.json’ file changes navigate menu immediately. Add ‘Search’ page settings only in the ‘English’ section, it does not affect to the ‘Japanese’ section.%改行%図（https://blog.processtune.com/wp-content/uploads/2022/08/ImplementationStep6.png）図（https://blog.processtune.com/wp-content/uploads/2022/08/ImplementationStep7.png）図（https://blog.processtune.com/wp-content/uploads/2022/08/ImplementationStep5.png）%改行%ⓘ Recommended Reference%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/?p=6428）%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/?p=6364）%改行%Categories:%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/asp-net-core/）リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/c/）リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/asp-net-core/service/）リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/slide-show/）リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/visual-studio/visual-studio-code/）%改行%Tags:%改行%No Tag%改行%Post navigation%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/2022/06/28/asp-net-core-options-pattern/）%改行%Post navigation%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/2022/08/18/net-core-options-pattern/）%改行%No responses yet%改行%Leave a Replyリンク（https://blog.processtune.com/2022/07/23/encapsulation-of-information-affects-an-app-flexibility-asp-net-core-options-pattern/#respond）%改行%Your email address will not be published.Required fields are marked*%改行%Comment*%改行%Name*%改行%Email*%改行%Website%改行%Save my name, email, and website in this browser for the next time I comment.%改行%© 2025 Processtune Blog. Title: Nginx run on Windows Subsystem for Linux 2 – Processtune Blog
Categories: ASP.NET Core,Azure Active Directory,Blazor,Docker,Microservice,Microsoft Azure,Nginx,Reverce Proxy,SlideShow,vim,Visual Studio,Visual Studio Code,WSL2
BodyText: %改行%リンク（#content）%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/）%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/microsoft-azure/）%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/microsoft-azure/azure-active-directory/）%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/visual-studio/）%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/microservice/）%改行%リンク（#）%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/）%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/microsoft-azure/）%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/microsoft-azure/azure-active-directory/）%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/visual-studio/）%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/microservice/）%改行%Nginx run on Windows Subsystem for Linux 2%改行%図（https://blog.processtune.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/03/ContainerizationSeriesTitle.png）%改行%byリンク（https://blog.processtune.com/author/tetsuro-takao/）%改行%onリンク（https://blog.processtune.com/2021/12/01/）%改行%On design fase ofリンク（https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/architecture/guide/architecture-styles/microservices?WT.mc_id=DT-MVP-4029060）, several experts of various knowledge designsリンク（https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/service-fabric/service-fabric-reliable-services-communication-aspnetcore?WT.mc_id=AZ-MVP-4029060）,リンク（https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/dotnet/architecture/microservices/container-docker-introduction/?WT.mc_id=DT-MVP-4029060）or entities implimentation ofリンク（https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/dotnet/architecture/microservices/microservice-ddd-cqrs-patterns/ddd-oriented-microservice?WT.mc_id=DT-MVP-4029060）and so on.%改行%As detail, define data flow from some usecases that structs user story when designing stateful middleware, because individual service of common microservices may map to a single usecase of the user story and they have specific persistence layer. And they acquire a state from their specific persistence layer for marge with some states that keeped by other services, or save a state to their specific persistence layer until it will be used.%改行%This article describes mechanism and creating processes of typically microservices, using reverse proxy that developed withリンク（https://www.nginx.com/）as the sample of microservices endpoint. This reverse proxy server is primitive prototype ofリンク（https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/dotnet/architecture/microservices/architect-microservice-container-applications/direct-client-to-microservice-communication-versus-the-api-gateway-pattern?WT.mc_id=DT-MVP-4029060）of microservices. The sample is structed with reverse proxy service that create on here as service mesh endpoint, and SVG coloring service that created in before article as sample of individual service.%改行%>>リンク（https://github.com/TetsuroTakao/QRCodeCreation）%改行%・Objective%改行%Service mesh endpoint provides an entrance of service mesh, service mesh is structured with several individual service of microservices.%改行%One of reason why Service mesh design pattern was born is for assembling a bunch of sidecar feature of individual services (the endpoint have to provides service discovery, load balancing, encryption, observability, traceability, authentication and authorization, and the circuit breaker pattern).%改行%This entrance is able to provide using also Azure Application Gateway although, it is able to create manually. Microsoft official document [リンク（https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/dotnet/architecture/microservices/architect-microservice-container-applications/direct-client-to-microservice-communication-versus-the-api-gateway-pattern?WT.mc_id=DT-MVP-4029060）] describes to consider processes of development real enterprise solution. Its article describes creating only one gateway of enterprise microservices is not good for performance, user experience, maintenance or security and so on.%改行%Before creation of vast of service mesh as microservices solution, creating service mesh endpoint manually once is useful for design skill of microservices.%改行%・Download sample application.%改行%Typically endpoint of service mesh is inner of cloud and communicate to load balancer of HTTP gateway. This pattern is for service mesh endpoints redundancy. Notice the sample of this article aims to expose directly single endpoint for development. In the word, it is sample of one mesh endpoint of several meshes. This endpont have to provide http host feature.%改行%This article only describes developing reverse proxy for development environment. Logging, service discovery, load balancing, encryption, observability, traceability, authentication & authorization, and the circuit breaker pattern are ommitted in this article, because the sample of this article assumes to provide these features on control plane of a cloud.%改行%図（https://blog.processtune.com/wp-content/uploads/2022/04/sampleoverview.png）%改行%This sample use the port number [8080] as outside port of development environment, and pass through to the port number [8081] for inside. This [リンク（https://github.com/TetsuroTakao/SVGIconColoring_en_2）] application container in the figure has to accept request from the port number [8081] and has to response to the port number [8081]. The source of the application is on GitHub and canリンク（https://github.com/TetsuroTakao/SVGIconColoring_en_2）although, it is not containerise yet, thus containerise it from now. As details, read the article [リンク（https://docs.docker.com/samples/dotnetcore/）].%改行%ⓘ As additional information%改行%Each persistence layer of individual service may create usingリンク（https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/architecture/guide/technology-choices/data-store-overview?WT.mc_id=DT-MVP-4029060）design pattern mixed several types of data store such as MongoDB or Azure Blob storage and so on that excludes file storage (for example, Azure Files, Azure NetApp Files). this design pattern represents usage of a data store types of purposes of the table below.%改行%Assumed information type%改行%Example%改行%Store type%改行%Implementations%改行%Unstructured, Semistructured%改行%Product catalog, Invoice, License%改行%Document-oriented database%改行%MongoDB, Azure Blob storage, Amazon DocumentDB%改行%Structured%改行%Investor Relations, Customer master,%改行%Merchantise master%改行%Relational database%改行%MySQL, Azure SQL database, Oracle databese%改行%Dynamic growing%改行%Intarest graph%改行%(Entertainment, Social network, E-commerce),%改行%Cyber-security threats analysis%改行%Graph structures data store%改行%OrientDB, Azure Cosmos DB, Amazon Neptune%改行%Pair type%改行%(includes shallow tier tree type)%改行%Task list, Address list,%改行%Recording (Transcript etc.) of meeting%改行%Key/Value data store%改行%Apache Ignite, Azure Table Storage,%改行%Google Cloud Datastore, AWS DynamoDB%改行%Only if some use case (in other words, the term [boundary context] in Domain-driven design) is mapped individual service and the case needs various data models can choose theリンク（https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/azure-sql/multi-model-features?WT.mc_id=DT-MVP-4029060）as persistence layer.%改行%To launch the sample project downloaded at the port number [8081], modify the value of the [applicationUri] property that is set at the [%project name%] ([SVGIconColoring_en] in this sample) settings of the [profiles] section of the [launchSettings.json] file, it is under the [Properties] folder of the Project root.%改行%The [Properties\launchSettings.json] file Define port value [8081] of the [profiles/SVGIconColoring_en/applicationUrl] { "iisSettings": { "windowsAuthentication": false, "anonymousAuthentication": true, "iisExpress": { "applicationUrl": "http://localhost:48877", "sslPort": 44388 } }, "profiles": { "SVGIconColoring_en": { "commandName": "Project", "dotnetRunMessages": true, "launchBrowser": true, "inspectUri": "{wsProtocol}://{url.hostname}:{url.port}/_framework/debug/ws-proxy?browser={browserInspectUri}", "applicationUrl": "https://localhost:7270;http://localhost:8081", "environmentVariables": { "ASPNETCORE_ENVIRONMENT": "Development" } }, "IIS Express": { "commandName": "IISExpress", "launchBrowser": true, "inspectUri": "{wsProtocol}://{url.hostname}:{url.port}/_framework/debug/ws-proxy?browser={browserInspectUri}", "environmentVariables": { "ASPNETCORE_ENVIRONMENT": "Development" } } } }%改行%Though launch from Visual Studio Code in this article, it will be modify to use Azure Functions Core Tools later. This article describes containerization with docker with follow.%改行%図（https://blog.processtune.com/wp-content/uploads/2022/03/LaunchSample.png）%改行%・Preparation of development on Windows subsystem for Linux 2.%改行%Reverse proxy is created with Nginx, Nginx is provided asリンク（https://hub.docker.com/_/nginx）from Docker Hub. This image run onリンク（https://www.docker.com/get-started）orリンク（https://hub.docker.com/search?offering=community&operating_system=linux&q=&type=edition）, Docker Desktop for Windows is not support ARM 64 Windows (Surface ProX), so only ARM 64 Windows useリンク（https://hub.docker.com/search?offering=community&operating_system=linux&q=&type=edition）with Windows Subsystem for Linux 2 (WSL2). Other user are able to use Docker Desktop, it operation is ommit in this article because it’s very easy.%改行%ARM 64 Windows user can use WSL2 after download and settingリンク（https://www.microsoft.com/en-id/p/ubuntu-20044-lts/9mttcl66cpxj?cid=msft_web_appsforwindows_chart#activetab=pivot:overviewtab）(choose any distribution, although this article describes procedure in case of Ubuntu) from Microsoft store.%改行%Launch WSL2 from Windows terminal after setting.%改行%図（https://blog.processtune.com/wp-content/uploads/2022/03/ubuntu.png）図（https://blog.processtune.com/wp-content/uploads/2022/03/WindowsTerminal.png）%改行%ARM 64 Windows user can use WSL2 after download and settingリンク（https://www.microsoft.com/en-id/p/ubuntu-20044-lts/9mttcl66cpxj?cid=msft_web_appsforwindows_chart#activetab=pivot:overviewtab）(choose any distribution, although this article describes procedure in case of Ubuntu) from Microsoft store.%改行%If use WSL2 first time, install tools to Ubuntu use Linux terminal.%改行%$ sudo apt install apt-transport-https ca-certificates curl gnupg lsb-release%改行%These tools is capables feature in table below.%改行%Tool%改行%Feature%改行%[https]%改行%Establish HTTPS connection%改行%[ca-certificates]%改行%Establish SSL connection%改行%[gnupgp]%改行%encrypting and decrypting key enable and signeture%改行%[lsb-release]%改行%Comform version information of Ubuntu%改行%[curl]%改行%Execute HTTP communication as client%改行%If Docker CE (community edition) does not installed, acquire GNU Privacy Guard (GnuPG, GPG) key of Docker engine and deploy it.%改行%$ curl -fsSL https://download.docker.com/linux/ubuntu/gpg | sudo gpg --dearmor -o /usr/share/keyrings/docker-archive-keyring.gpg echo "deb [arch=arm64 signed-by=/usr/share/keyrings/docker-archive-keyring.gpg] \ https://download.docker.com/linux/ubuntu $(lsb_release -cs) stable" | sudo tee /etc/apt/sources.list.d/docker.list > /dev/null%改行%Comform image enable install.%改行%$ sudo apt update $ sudo apt install docker-ce docker-ce-cli containerd.io $ apt-cache madison docker-ce%改行%Then install Docker engine.%改行%$ sudo apt install docker-ce=5:20.10.8~3-0~ubuntu-focal docker-ce-cli=5:20.10.8~3-0~ubuntu-focal containerd.io%改行%・Create reverse proxy.%改行%Create reverse proxy with Nginx that is provided as theリンク（https://hub.docker.com/_/nginx）from the Docker Hub. This image run on theリンク（https://www.docker.com/get-started）or theリンク（https://hub.docker.com/search?offering=community&operating_system=linux&q=&type=edition）, the Docker Desktop for Windows is not support ARM 64 Windows (Surface Pro X), so only ARM 64 Windows users have to use the Docker Engine for Linux distributions with WSL2. Other users are able to use the Docker Desktop, its operation is ommit in this article because it’s very easy.%改行%In the case of using WSL2 to mount a Docker image, start a Docker service use the [sudo] super user mode.%改行%$ sudo service docker start%改行%After enables a Docker service, pull a Nginx image from the Docker Hub, then launch the container of the image. There is needs that default port number [80] of Nginx is changed to the port number [8080] for listening http request for development environment. So install the vim editor using bash in the container, it’s important before it, update and upgrade the Advanced Package Tool (the [apt] command).%改行%$ docker pull nginx $ docker run -dit -p 8080:8080 --init --name rproxy nginx $ docker exec -it rproxy /bin/bash # apt update && upgrade -y # apt install vim # vim /etc/nginx/nginx.conf%改行%Modify to add the [server] settings to the [http] section of the [/etc/nginx/nginx.conf] file. the [server] settings defines the [listen] attribute, the [server_name] attribute and the [location] settings.%改行%After modify the [/etc/nginx/nginx.conf] file of the container, put the [exit] command to exit container. then put the [docker kill rproxy] command to terminate the container process of the reverse proxy on WSL2.%改行%ⓘ As additional information%改行%Kill process when WSL2 stackes. Right click on the [Windows Start] icon to open the [Task Manager], then select the [Task Manager] and right click on the [Virtual Machine Worker Process] of the [Task Manager]. Then select the [End task] menu of the context menu.%改行%図（https://blog.processtune.com/wp-content/uploads/2022/03/contextmenu.png）図（https://blog.processtune.com/wp-content/uploads/2022/03/EndWSL2Task.png）図（https://blog.processtune.com/wp-content/uploads/2022/03/EndWSL2TaskZoom.png）%改行%If you want to know details of process Information, you are able to get the [リンク（https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/sysinternals/downloads/sysinternals-suite?WT.mc_id=DT-MVP-4029060）] from theリンク（https://www.microsoft.com/store/apps/9p7knl5rwt25）.%改行%This reverse proxy is just only use on development environment, so it is able to ommit firewall settings. Several attributes such as the [ufw enable], [ufw allow] or [ufw default deny] can set if it needs although, for example providing this container to developers of development team, for portability, it is not good that set firewall features.%改行%At last, confirm request to the port number [8080] is forwarding to the port number [8081].%改行%Launch the container of the reverse proxy image with the command below this time. If added the [–rm] option when the [docker run] command used, the [docker container rm rproxy] command below is not needs. The [–net host] option is used to access network of container host from container inside.%改行%$ docker container rm rproxy $ docker run -dit --init --name rproxy --net host nginx%改行%・Test action of reverse proxy.%改行%The way of the article [リンク（https://docs.docker.com/samples/dotnetcore/）] to containerise uses the Docker Desktop, so ARM x64 Windows user is not able to use the procedure. Though Microsoft official document [リンク（https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/aspnet/core/host-and-deploy/docker/building-net-docker-images?view=aspnetcore-6.0&WT.mc_id=DT-MVP-4029060）] uses the Docker Desktop also, but the article describes how to [Run in a Linux container], so in this article, choose the way that deploy Windows project into a Linux container. Users excepting Windows x64 ARM users build the sample using Microsoft official document to containerise. The follow description is for Windows x64 ARM users.%改行%At first, prepare a sample web app to confirm that HTTP request is transferred to the port a proxy server throws.%改行%Open Ubuntu on the [Windows terminal] to pull Docker official image of the [リンク（https://hub.docker.com/_/microsoft-dotnet-sdk）].%改行%$ docker pull mcr.microsoft.com/dotnet/sdk:6.0%改行%Launch the container using image of the [リンク（https://hub.docker.com/_/microsoft-dotnet-sdk）], then pull the [リンク（https://github.com/cli/cli/blob/trunk/docs/install_linux.md）] into the container from github web site [リンク（https://github.com/cli/cli/blob/trunk/docs/install_linux.md）]. The container of Docker official image of the [リンク（https://hub.docker.com/_/microsoft-dotnet-sdk）] launches with the host networking same as the container of reverse proxy, The sample project is defined as the port number [5078] for now.%改行%$ docker run -dit --rm --net host --init --name svgiconcoloring mcr.microsoft.com/dotnet/sdk:latest $ docker exec -it svgiconcoloring /bin/bash # curl -fsSL https://cli.github.com/packages/githubcli-archive-keyring.gpg | dd of=/usr/share/keyrings/githubcli-archive-keyring.gpg # echo "deb [arch=$(dpkg --print-architecture) signed-by=/usr/share/keyrings/githubcli-archive-keyring.gpg] https://cli.github.com/packages stable main" | tee /etc/apt/sources.list.d/github-cli.list > /dev/null # sudo apt update && upgrade -y # sudo apt install gh # md /home/svgiconcoloring # cd /home/svgiconcoloring%改行%Authorise your git account using the [リンク（https://github.com/cli/cli/blob/trunk/docs/install_linux.md）], then clone the sample project at your work folder (uses the [/home/svgiconcoloring] folder, in the case of the command above). Authorise steps includes authentication flow in your browser. A browser authentication flow is called with the process below.%改行%Process%改行%Selection%改行%Distination of login%改行%GitHub.com%改行%Protocol%改行%HTTPS%改行%Get credentials%改行%Yes%改行%Authenticate%改行%Login with a web browser%改行%図（https://blog.processtune.com/wp-content/uploads/2022/03/authSteps.png）%改行%Move to the sample project folder(uses the [cd SVGIconColoring_en_2] command, in the case of fegure below), and put [dotnet certs https] command in the [Windows terminal] to install development certification. Then put [dotnet run] command in the [Windows terminal] to run sample application.%改行%図（https://blog.processtune.com/wp-content/uploads/2022/03/dotnetRun5078.png）%改行%After confirmation of running sample project, use vim as Nginx image above, modify the [Properties/launchSettings.json] file to change the port of sample application to [8081]. Yes, this sample web application is same as run on Windows above. This sample run on Alpine Linux now. The article [リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/?p=5925）] describes how to create an application for runnable on Azure Functions use this mechanism.%改行%図（https://blog.processtune.com/wp-content/uploads/2022/03/dotnetRun8081.png）%改行%Confirm the container of reverse proxy and svg icon coloring app are running, then put [localhost:8080] into the address box of a browser, and the sample app is able to execute.%改行%図（https://blog.processtune.com/wp-content/uploads/2022/03/dotnetRun8080.png）%改行%Categories:%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/asp-net-core/）リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/asp-net-core/blazor/）リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/docker/）リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/microservice/）リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/nginx/）リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/nginx/reverce-proxy/）リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/slide-show/）リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/wsl2/vim/）リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/visual-studio/visual-studio-code/）リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/category/wsl2/）%改行%Tags:%改行%No Tag%改行%Post navigation%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/2021/11/01/javascript-colors-svg-images-that-are-acquired-by-blazor-webassembly/）%改行%Post navigation%改行%リンク（https://blog.processtune.com/2022/01/15/create-reverse-proxy-using-dotnet-minimal/）%改行%No responses yet%改行%Leave a Replyリンク（https://blog.processtune.com/2021/12/01/nginx-run-on-windows-subsystem-for-linux-2/#respond）%改行%Your email address will not be published.Required fields are marked*%改行%Comment*%改行%Name*%改行%Email*%改行%Website%改行%Save my name, email, and website in this browser for the next time I comment.%改行%© 2025 Processtune Blog.
